```text
16	  ApiOperation,
17	  ApiResponse,
18	  ApiBearerAuth,
19	} from '@nestjs/swagger';
20	import { CalendarProvidersService } from './calendar-providers.service';
21	import { ConnectGoogleCalendarDto } from './dto/connect-google-calendar.dto';
22	import { ConnectMicrosoftCalendarDto } from './dto/connect-microsoft-calendar.dto';
23	import { ConnectSpecificCalendarDto } from './dto/connect-calendar.dto';
24	import { ConnectWithTokensDto } from './dto/connect-with-tokens.dto';
25	import { UpdateCalendarProviderDto } from './dto/update-calendar-provider.dto';
26	import { CalendarProviderResponseDto } from './dto/calendar-provider-response.dto';
27	import { OAuthUrlResponseDto } from './dto/oauth-url-response.dto';
28	import { AvailableCalendarsResponseDto } from './dto/list-calendars.dto';
29	import { ListCalendarsWithTokensResponseDto } from './dto/list-calendars-response.dto';
30	import { FirebaseAuthGuard } from '../../common/guards/firebase-auth.guard';
31	
32	@Controller('calendar-providers')
33	@ApiTags('calendar-providers')
34	@UseGuards(FirebaseAuthGuard)
35	@ApiBearerAuth()
36	export class CalendarProvidersController {
37	  constructor(
38	    private readonly calendarProvidersService: CalendarProvidersService,
39	  ) {}
40	
41	  @Get('google/auth-url')
42	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get Google OAuth authorization URL' })
43	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: OAuthUrlResponseDto })
44	  async getGoogleAuthUrl(): Promise<OAuthUrlResponseDto> {
45	    return this.calendarProvidersService.getGoogleAuthUrl();
46	  }
47	
48	  @Get('microsoft/auth-url')
49	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get Microsoft OAuth authorization URL' })
50	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: OAuthUrlResponseDto })
51	  async getMicrosoftAuthUrl(): Promise<OAuthUrlResponseDto> {
52	    return this.calendarProvidersService.getMicrosoftAuthUrl();
53	  }
54	
55	  @Post('google/connect')
56	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Connect Google Calendar with authorization code' })
57	  @ApiResponse({ status: 201, type: CalendarProviderResponseDto })
58	  async connectGoogleCalendar(
59	    @Body() dto: ConnectGoogleCalendarDto,
60	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto> {
61	    return this.calendarProvidersService.connectGoogleCalendar(dto.code);
62	  }
63	
64	  @Post('microsoft/connect')
65	  @ApiOperation({
66	    summary: 'Connect Microsoft Calendar with authorization code',
67	  })
68	  @ApiResponse({ status: 201, type: CalendarProviderResponseDto })
69	  async connectMicrosoftCalendar(
70	    @Body() dto: ConnectMicrosoftCalendarDto,
71	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto> {
72	    return this.calendarProvidersService.connectMicrosoftCalendar(dto.code);
73	  }
74	
75	  @Post('google/list-calendars')
76	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'List available Google calendars for selection (returns tokens for connect-multiple)' })
77	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: ListCalendarsWithTokensResponseDto })
78	  async listGoogleCalendars(
79	    @Body() dto: ConnectGoogleCalendarDto,
80	  ): Promise<ListCalendarsWithTokensResponseDto> {
81	    const result = await this.calendarProvidersService.listGoogleCalendars(dto.code);
82	    return { calendars: result.calendars, tokens: result.tokens };
83	  }
84	
85	  @Post('microsoft/list-calendars')
86	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'List available Microsoft calendars for selection (returns tokens for connect-multiple)' })
87	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: ListCalendarsWithTokensResponseDto })
88	  async listMicrosoftCalendars(
89	    @Body() dto: ConnectMicrosoftCalendarDto,
90	  ): Promise<ListCalendarsWithTokensResponseDto> {
91	    const result = await this.calendarProvidersService.listMicrosoftCalendars(dto.code);
92	    return { calendars: result.calendars, tokens: result.tokenData };
93	  }
94	
95	  @Post('google/connect-multiple')
96	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Connect specific Google calendars using tokens from list-calendars' })
97	  @ApiResponse({ status: 201, type: [CalendarProviderResponseDto] })
98	  async connectSpecificGoogleCalendars(
99	    @Body() dto: ConnectWithTokensDto,
100	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto[]> {
101	    return this.calendarProvidersService.connectSpecificGoogleCalendars(
102	      dto.tokens,
103	      dto.calendarIds || [],
104	    );
105	  }
106	
107	  @Post('microsoft/connect-multiple')
108	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Connect specific Microsoft calendars using tokens from list-calendars' })
109	  @ApiResponse({ status: 201, type: [CalendarProviderResponseDto] })
110	  async connectSpecificMicrosoftCalendars(
111	    @Body() dto: ConnectWithTokensDto,
112	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto[]> {
113	    return this.calendarProvidersService.connectSpecificMicrosoftCalendars(
114	      dto.tokens,

116	    );
117	  }
118	
119	  @Get()
120	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get all connected calendar providers' })
121	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: [CalendarProviderResponseDto] })
122	  async findAll(): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto[]> {
123	    return this.calendarProvidersService.findAll();
124	  }
125	
126	  @Get(':id')
127	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get calendar provider by ID' })
128	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: CalendarProviderResponseDto })
129	  @ApiResponse({ status: 404, description: 'Calendar provider not found' })
130	  async findOne(@Param('id') id: string): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto> {
131	    return this.calendarProvidersService.findOne(id);
132	  }
133	
134	  @Patch(':id')
135	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Update calendar provider settings (color, isPrimary, isActive)' })
136	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: CalendarProviderResponseDto })
137	  @ApiResponse({ status: 404, description: 'Calendar provider not found' })
138	  @ApiResponse({ status: 400, description: 'Invalid data provided' })
139	  async update(
140	    @Param('id') id: string,
141	    @Body() dto: UpdateCalendarProviderDto,
142	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto> {
143	    return this.calendarProvidersService.update(id, dto);
144	  }
145	
146	  @Delete(':id')
147	  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
148	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Disconnect calendar provider' })
149	  @ApiResponse({ status: 204, description: 'Calendar provider disconnected' })
150	  @ApiResponse({ status: 404, description: 'Calendar provider not found' })
151	  async disconnect(@Param('id') id: string): Promise<void> {
152	    return this.calendarProvidersService.disconnect(id);
153	  }
154	
155	  @Post(':id/sync')

157	  @ApiResponse({ status: 200, type: CalendarProviderResponseDto })
158	  @ApiResponse({ status: 404, description: 'Calendar provider not found' })
159	  async triggerSync(
160	    @Param('id') id: string,
161	  ): Promise<CalendarProviderResponseDto> {
162	    return this.calendarProvidersService.triggerSync(id);
163	  }
164	}
```

 `./api/src/modules/xero/xero-api.ts`

```text
1	// Typed Xero Accounting API resource helpers (ACCREC / receivables — Robyn
2	// invoices clients). Transport + auth live in xero-http.ts. Business decisions
3	// live in the engine and services; this file is I/O only.
4	//
5	// Idempotency discipline: every write does check-by-reference first. ACCREC
6	// invoices carry a unique-ish Reference we own, so we filter on it before create.
7	
8	import { xeroFetch, xeroRequest, assertElementOk, Pagination, resolveTenant } from './xero-http';
9	
10	// ---- Date helpers (YYYY-MM-DD in UTC) -------------------------------------
11	
12	function todayISO(): string {
13	  return new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
14	}
15	
16	function addDaysISO(dateISO: string, days: number): string {
17	  const d = new Date(`${dateISO}T00:00:00Z`);
18	  d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() + days);
19	  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
20	}
21	
22	// ---- Contacts -------------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	export interface XeroContact {
25	  ContactID: string;
26	  Name: string;
27	  EmailAddress?: string;
28	  ContactStatus?: string;
29	  IsCustomer?: boolean;
30	  IsSupplier?: boolean;
31	}
32	
33	export async function listContacts(page = 1, searchTerm?: string): Promise<XeroContact[]> {
34	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Contacts?: XeroContact[] }>('/Contacts', {
35	    query: { page, ...(searchTerm ? { SearchTerm: searchTerm } : {}) },
36	  });
37	  return res.Contacts ?? [];
38	}
39	
40	export async function findContactByEmail(email: string): Promise<XeroContact | null> {
41	  const where = `EmailAddress="${email.replace(/"/g, '')}"`;
42	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Contacts?: XeroContact[] }>('/Contacts', {
43	    query: { where, page: 1 },
44	  });
45	  return res.Contacts?.[0] ?? null;
46	}
47	
48	export async function findContactByName(name: string): Promise<XeroContact | null> {
49	  const where = `Name="${name.replace(/"/g, '')}"`;
50	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Contacts?: XeroContact[] }>('/Contacts', {
51	    query: { where, page: 1 },
52	  });
53	  return res.Contacts?.[0] ?? null;
54	}
55	
56	/** Idempotent: reuse an existing contact by email/name before creating. */
57	export async function ensureContact(
58	  name: string,
59	  email?: string,
60	): Promise<{ contact: XeroContact; created: boolean }> {
61	  if (email) {
62	    const byEmail = await findContactByEmail(email);
63	    if (byEmail) return { contact: byEmail, created: false };
64	  }
65	  const byName = await findContactByName(name);
66	  if (byName) return { contact: byName, created: false };
67	
68	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Contacts: XeroContact[] }>('/Contacts', {
69	    method: 'POST',
70	    json: { Contacts: [{ Name: name, ...(email ? { EmailAddress: email } : {}) }] },
71	    query: { summarizeErrors: 'false' },
72	    idempotencyKey: `contact:${name}:${email ?? ''}`.slice(0, 128),
73	  });
74	  return { contact: res.Contacts[0], created: true };
75	}
76	
77	// ---- Invoices (ACCREC) ----------------------------------------------------
78	
79	export interface XeroLineItem {
80	  Description: string;
81	  Quantity?: number;
82	  UnitAmount?: number;
83	  AccountCode?: string;
84	  TaxType?: string;
85	  LineAmount?: number;
86	  ItemCode?: string;
87	}
88	
89	export interface XeroInvoice {
90	  InvoiceID?: string;
91	  InvoiceNumber?: string;
92	  Type?: string;
93	  Contact?: { ContactID: string; Name?: string };
94	  Reference?: string;
95	  Status?: string;
96	  Date?: string;
97	  DueDate?: string;
98	  LineItems?: XeroLineItem[];
99	  LineAmountTypes?: string;
100	  SubTotal?: number;
101	  TotalTax?: number;
102	  Total?: number;
103	  AmountDue?: number;
104	  AmountPaid?: number;
105	  CurrencyCode?: string;
106	}
107	
108	export interface CreateInvoiceInput {
109	  contactId: string;
110	  reference: string; // our stable idempotency reference
111	  lineItems: XeroLineItem[];
112	  date?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
113	  dueDate?: string;
114	  status?: 'DRAFT' | 'SUBMITTED' | 'AUTHORISED';
115	  lineAmountTypes?: 'Exclusive' | 'Inclusive' | 'NoTax';
116	  currencyCode?: string;
117	}
118	
119	/** Find an ACCREC invoice we previously created, by our Reference. */
120	export async function findInvoiceByReference(reference: string): Promise<XeroInvoice | null> {
121	  const where = `Type=="ACCREC" AND Reference=="${reference.replace(/"/g, '')}"`;
122	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Invoices?: XeroInvoice[] }>('/Invoices', {
123	    query: { where, page: 1 },
124	  });
125	  return res.Invoices?.[0] ?? null;
126	}
127	
128	export async function getInvoice(invoiceId: string): Promise<XeroInvoice | null> {
129	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Invoices?: XeroInvoice[] }>(`/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {});
130	  return res.Invoices?.[0] ?? null;
131	}
132	
133	/** Idempotent create: returns the existing invoice if the Reference is taken. */
134	export async function createInvoice(
135	  input: CreateInvoiceInput,
136	): Promise<{ invoice: XeroInvoice; created: boolean }> {
137	  const existing = await findInvoiceByReference(input.reference);
138	  if (existing) return { invoice: existing, created: false };
139	
140	  // Xero rejects an AUTHORISED create with no DueDate; default to Net 14 from
141	  // the invoice date so both DRAFT and AUTHORISED writes always validate.
142	  const date = input.date ?? todayISO();
143	  const dueDate = input.dueDate ?? addDaysISO(date, 14);
144	
145	  const payload: XeroInvoice = {
146	    Type: 'ACCREC',
147	    Contact: { ContactID: input.contactId },
148	    Reference: input.reference,
149	    LineItems: input.lineItems,
150	    LineAmountTypes: input.lineAmountTypes ?? 'Exclusive',
151	    Status: input.status ?? 'DRAFT',
152	    Date: date,
153	    DueDate: dueDate,
154	    ...(input.currencyCode ? { CurrencyCode: input.currencyCode } : {}),
155	  };
156	
157	  const res = await xeroFetch<{
158	    Invoices: (XeroInvoice & {
159	      StatusAttributeString?: 'OK' | 'WARNING' | 'ERROR';
160	      ValidationErrors?: { Message?: string }[];
161	    })[];
162	  }>(
163	    '/Invoices',
164	    {
165	      method: 'POST',
166	      json: { Invoices: [payload] },
167	      query: { summarizeErrors: 'false' },
168	      idempotencyKey: `invoice:${input.reference}`.slice(0, 128),
169	    },
170	  );
171	  // summarizeErrors=false hides validation failures inside a 200 — assert the
172	  // element status so a bad write throws instead of returning a zero-GUID.
173	  const element = res.Invoices?.[0];
174	  if (!element) throw new Error('XeroInvoiceCreateFailed: no invoice element in response');
175	  assertElementOk(element, `create ACCREC invoice ${input.reference}`);
176	  return { invoice: element, created: true };
177	}
178	
179	/** Move a DRAFT/SUBMITTED invoice to AUTHORISED (raw API — MCP cannot). */
180	export async function authoriseInvoice(invoiceId: string): Promise<XeroInvoice> {
181	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Invoices: XeroInvoice[] }>(`/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
182	    method: 'POST',
183	    json: { Invoices: [{ InvoiceID: invoiceId, Status: 'AUTHORISED' }] },
184	  });
185	  return res.Invoices[0];
186	}
187	
188	export async function listInvoices(
189	  statuses?: string[],
190	  page = 1,
191	): Promise<{ invoices: XeroInvoice[]; pagination?: Pagination }> {
192	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Invoices?: XeroInvoice[]; pagination?: Pagination }>('/Invoices', {
193	    query: { page, ...(statuses ? { Statuses: statuses.join(',') } : {}), summaryOnly: 'false' },
194	  });
195	  return { invoices: res.Invoices ?? [], pagination: res.pagination };
196	}
197	
198	// ---- History & Notes (raw API only — no MCP tool) -------------------------
199	
200	export async function addHistoryNote(
201	  endpoint: 'Invoices' | 'Contacts' | 'Quotes',
202	  guid: string,
203	  details: string,
204	): Promise<void> {
205	  await xeroRequest(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`, {
206	    method: 'PUT',
207	    json: { HistoryRecords: [{ Details: details.slice(0, 2500) }] },
208	  });
209	}
210	
211	export async function getHistory(
212	  endpoint: 'Invoices' | 'Contacts' | 'Quotes',
213	  guid: string,
214	): Promise<Array<{ Changes?: string; DateUTC?: string; User?: string; Details?: string }>> {
215	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ HistoryRecords?: any[] }>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`, {});
216	  return res.HistoryRecords ?? [];
217	}
218	
219	// ---- Attachments (raw API only — no MCP tool) -----------------------------
220	
221	const FORBIDDEN_FILENAME = /[<>:"/\\|?*\x00+]/;
222	
223	export async function uploadAttachment(
224	  endpoint: 'Invoices' | 'Contacts' | 'Quotes',
225	  guid: string,
226	  filename: string,
227	  bytes: Uint8Array,
228	  mimeType: string,
229	): Promise<void> {
230	  if (FORBIDDEN_FILENAME.test(filename)) {
231	    throw new Error(`InvalidAttachmentFilename: "${filename}" contains a forbidden character`);
232	  }
233	  await xeroRequest(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/Attachments/${filename}`, {
234	    method: 'PUT',
235	    body: bytes,
236	    contentType: mimeType,
237	  });
238	}
239	
240	export async function listAttachments(
241	  endpoint: 'Invoices' | 'Contacts' | 'Quotes',
242	  guid: string,
243	): Promise<Array<{ AttachmentID: string; FileName: string; MimeType: string; ContentLength: number }>> {
244	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Attachments?: any[] }>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/Attachments/`, {});
245	  return res.Attachments ?? [];
246	}
247	
248	// ---- Payments (read for retainer cadence; write on demand) -----------------
249	
250	export interface XeroPayment {
251	  PaymentID?: string;
252	  Date?: string;
253	  Amount?: number;
254	  Reference?: string;
255	  Status?: string;
256	  Invoice?: { InvoiceID?: string; InvoiceNumber?: string };
257	}
258	
259	export async function listPayments(page = 1): Promise<XeroPayment[]> {
260	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Payments?: XeroPayment[] }>('/Payments', { query: { page } });
261	  return res.Payments ?? [];
262	}
263	
264	export async function createPayment(input: {
265	  invoiceId: string;
266	  accountCode: string;
267	  amount: number;
268	  date: string;
269	  reference: string;
270	}): Promise<XeroPayment> {
271	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Payments: XeroPayment[] }>('/Payments', {
272	    method: 'PUT',
273	    json: {
274	      Invoice: { InvoiceID: input.invoiceId },
275	      Account: { Code: input.accountCode },
276	      Date: input.date,
277	      Amount: input.amount,
278	      Reference: input.reference,
279	    },
280	    idempotencyKey: `payment:${input.reference}`.slice(0, 128),
281	  });
282	  return res.Payments[0];
283	}
284	
285	// ---- Quotes (loop-3 detector: accepted quote never invoiced) --------------
286	
287	export interface XeroQuote {
288	  QuoteID?: string;
289	  QuoteNumber?: string;
290	  Status?: string;
291	  Contact?: { ContactID: string; Name?: string };
292	  Total?: number;
293	  Date?: string;
294	  Reference?: string;
295	  LineItems?: XeroLineItem[];
296	}
297	
298	export async function listQuotes(status?: string, page = 1): Promise<XeroQuote[]> {
299	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Quotes?: XeroQuote[] }>('/Quotes', {
300	    query: { page, ...(status ? { Status: status } : {}) },
301	  });
302	  return res.Quotes ?? [];
303	}
304	
305	// ---- Accounts (revenue account codes for invoice lines) -------------------
306	
307	export interface XeroAccount {
308	  AccountID: string;
309	  Code: string;
310	  Name: string;
311	  Type: string;
312	  Class?: string;
313	  EnablePaymentsToAccount?: boolean;
314	}
315	
316	export async function listAccounts(): Promise<XeroAccount[]> {
317	  const res = await xeroFetch<{ Accounts?: XeroAccount[] }>('/Accounts', {});
318	  return res.Accounts ?? [];
319	}
320	
321	/** First sales/revenue account code, for ACCREC invoice lines. Falls back to 200. */
322	export async function defaultSalesAccountCode(): Promise<string> {
323	  try {
324	    const accounts = await listAccounts();
325	    const revenue = accounts.find(
326	      (a) => a.Type === 'REVENUE' || a.Class === 'REVENUE' || a.Code === '200',
327	    );
328	    return revenue?.Code ?? '200';
329	  } catch {
330	    return '200';
331	  }
332	}
333	
334	// ---- Reports: Aged Receivables by Contact (leak strip) --------------------
335	
336	export async function agedReceivablesByContact(contactId: string): Promise<any> {
337	  return xeroFetch<any>('/Reports/AgedReceivablesByContact', {
338	    query: { contactID: contactId },
339	  });
340	}
341	
342	// ---- Deep link ------------------------------------------------------------
343	
344	export async function invoiceDeepLink(invoiceId: string): Promise<string> {
345	  // The go.xero.com deep link for a receivable invoice (opens in the org UI).
346	  return `https://go.xero.com/app/invoicing/view/${invoiceId}`;
347	}
348	
349	// ---- Composed write: the money moment (G2) --------------------------------
350	// One idempotent path used by BOTH the auto-send loop and the manual approve
351	// flow. Ensures the contact, creates the ACCREC invoice (draft or authorised),
352	// writes Robyn's decision note to History, and attaches the evidence. Takes
353	// plain data and returns plain data — callers persist. Every step is safe to
354	// re-run (check-by-reference on the invoice, overwrite-by-filename on attachments).
355	
356	export interface WriteInvoiceEvidence {
357	  filename: string; // e.g. "robyn-evidence.txt" (no < > : " / \ | ? * +)
358	  text: string;
359	  mimeType?: string; // default text/plain
360	}
361	
362	export interface WriteInvoiceInput {
363	  clientName: string;
364	  clientEmail?: string;
365	  existingContactId?: string; // reuse if we already know it
366	  reference: string; // stable idempotency key -> Xero Reference
367	  lines: XeroLineItem[];
368	  currency?: string;
369	  date?: string;
370	  dueDate?: string;
371	  authorise: boolean; // false => leave DRAFT; true => AUTHORISED (sent)
372	  decisionNote: string; // Robyn's reasoning -> History & Notes
373	  evidence?: WriteInvoiceEvidence[]; // transcript excerpt + contract clause
374	}
375	
376	export interface WriteInvoiceResult {
377	  contactId: string;
378	  contactCreated: boolean;
379	  invoiceId: string;
380	  invoiceNumber: string | null;
381	  status: string | null;
382	  total: number | null;
383	  deepLink: string;
384	  alreadyExisted: boolean;
385	}
386	
387	export async function writeInvoice(input: WriteInvoiceInput): Promise<WriteInvoiceResult> {
388	  // 1. Contact (idempotent)
389	  let contactId = input.existingContactId ?? null;
390	  let contactCreated = false;
391	  if (!contactId) {
392	    const { contact, created } = await ensureContact(input.clientName, input.clientEmail);
393	    contactId = contact.ContactID;
394	    contactCreated = created;
395	  }
396	
397	  // 2. Invoice (idempotent by Reference)
398	  const { invoice, created } = await createInvoice({
399	    contactId,
400	    reference: input.reference,
401	    lineItems: input.lines,
402	    currencyCode: input.currency,
403	    date: input.date,
404	    dueDate: input.dueDate,
405	    status: input.authorise ? 'AUTHORISED' : 'DRAFT',
406	  });
407	  const invoiceId = invoice.InvoiceID as string;
408	
409	  // 3. If it existed as DRAFT but we now want it AUTHORISED, walk it up.
410	  let status = invoice.Status ?? null;
411	  if (input.authorise && status !== 'AUTHORISED' && status !== 'PAID') {
412	    const authed = await authoriseInvoice(invoiceId);
413	    status = authed.Status ?? status;
414	  }
415	
416	  // 4. Robyn's decision note -> History (raw API; no MCP tool).
417	  try {
418	    await addHistoryNote('Invoices', invoiceId, input.decisionNote);
419	  } catch {
420	    /* history is best-effort; never block the money moment on a note */
421	  }
422	
423	  // 5. Evidence attachments -> raw API (overwrite-by-filename is idempotent).
424	  for (const ev of input.evidence ?? []) {
425	    try {
426	      await uploadAttachment(
427	        'Invoices',
428	        invoiceId,
429	        ev.filename,
430	        new TextEncoder().encode(ev.text),
431	        ev.mimeType ?? 'text/plain',
432	      );
433	    } catch {
434	      /* attachment is best-effort evidence */
435	    }
436	  }
437	
438	  return {
439	    contactId,
440	    contactCreated,
441	    invoiceId,
442	    invoiceNumber: invoice.InvoiceNumber ?? null,
443	    status,
444	    total: invoice.Total ?? null,
445	    deepLink: await invoiceDeepLink(invoiceId),
446	    alreadyExisted: !created,
447	  };
448	}
449
```

```text
1	// Low-level Xero transport for a Custom Connection (client_credentials, one org).
2	// Framework-agnostic on purpose: the NestJS providers wrap it AND the seed
3	// scripts import it directly under tsx. There is exactly ONE token path in this
4	// repo and this is it (see .claude/skills/xero-auth).
5	//
6	// Adds over the skill's reference helper: broad->granular scope fallback,
7	// tenant-id resolution via GET /connections (cached), retry-on-401 (re-mint),
8	// and 429 Retry-After handling.
9	
10	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
11	import { dirname, join } from 'node:path';
12	
13	export const ACCOUNTING_BASE = 'https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0';
14	const TOKEN_URL = 'https://identity.xero.com/connect/token';
15	const CONNECTIONS_URL = 'https://api.xero.com/connections';
16	
17	// Project scopes (broad). A custom connection created after 29 Apr 2026 may be
18	// granular-only and reject these with 400 invalid_scope — we fall back.
19	const BROAD_SCOPES =
20	  'accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.settings accounting.reports.read';
21	const GRANULAR_SCOPES = [
22	  'accounting.invoices',
23	  'accounting.contacts',
24	  'accounting.attachments',
25	  'accounting.settings.read',
26	  'accounting.payments',
27	  // A granular-only Custom Connection has the specific report scopes, NOT the
28	  // broad accounting.reports.read. Request the ones this project reads.
29	  'accounting.reports.aged.read',
30	  'accounting.reports.profitandloss.read',
31	  'accounting.reports.balancesheet.read',
32	].join(' ');
33	
34	let dotEnvLoaded = false;
35	
36	/** Self-load api/.env once for standalone (tsx) runs. Never overrides set vars. */
37	export function loadDotEnv(): void {
38	  if (dotEnvLoaded) return;
39	  dotEnvLoaded = true;
40	  // Look for api/.env walking up from cwd; also try ./api/.env from repo root.
41	  const candidates: string[] = [];
42	  let dir = process.cwd();
43	  for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
44	    candidates.push(join(dir, '.env'));
45	    candidates.push(join(dir, 'api', '.env'));
46	    const parent = dirname(dir);
47	    if (parent === dir) break;
48	    dir = parent;
49	  }
50	  const found = candidates.find((p) => existsSync(p));
51	  if (!found) return;
52	  for (const line of readFileSync(found, 'utf8').split('\n')) {
53	    const m = /^(?:export\s+)?([A-Za-z_][\w.]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/.exec(line.trim());
54	    if (!m) continue;
55	    const key = m[1] as string;
56	    let value = (m[2] ?? '').trim();
57	    if (
58	      value.length >= 2 &&
59	      ((value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
60	        (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'")))
61	    ) {
62	      value = value.slice(1, -1);
63	    }
64	    if (process.env[key] === undefined) process.env[key] = value;
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	export function requireEnv(name: string): string {
69	  loadDotEnv();
70	  const v = process.env[name];
71	  if (!v || v.trim() === '') {
72	    throw new Error(
73	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — set it in api/.env. An empty Xero client ` +
74	        `id/secret surfaces from the token endpoint as 400 invalid_request.`,
75	    );
76	  }
77	  return v.trim();
78	}
79	
80	interface TokenResponse {
81	  access_token: string;
82	  expires_in: number;
83	  token_type: string;
84	  scope?: string;
85	}
86	
87	let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAtMs: number } | null = null;
88	let workingScopeString: string | null = null;
89	let tenant: { id: string; name: string } | null = null;
90	let tenantResolved = false;
91	
92	export class XeroAuthError extends Error {
93	  constructor(message: string) {
94	    super(message);
95	    this.name = 'XeroAuthError';
96	  }
97	}
98	
99	async function requestToken(scope: string): Promise<Response> {
100	  const clientId = requireEnv('XERO_CLIENT_ID');
101	  const clientSecret = requireEnv('XERO_CLIENT_SECRET');
102	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString('base64');
103	  return fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
104	    method: 'POST',
105	    headers: {
106	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
107	      'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
108	      Accept: 'application/json',
109	    },
110	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
111	  });
112	}
113	
114	/** Mint a token, discovering the scope string the app actually grants. */
115	async function mintToken(): Promise<string> {
116	  const override = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim();
117	  const chain =
118	    workingScopeString !== null
119	      ? [workingScopeString] // reuse the scope string we already know works ('' is valid)
120	      : override
121	        ? [override]
122	        : // Broad first; granular for post-Apr-2026 CCs; finally an empty scope,
123	          // which makes Xero grant exactly the scopes the app has (works for any
124	          // real Custom Connection).
125	          [BROAD_SCOPES, GRANULAR_SCOPES, ''];
126	
127	  let lastBody = '';
128	  for (const scope of chain) {
129	    const res = await requestToken(scope);
130	    if (res.ok) {
131	      const json = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
132	      workingScopeString = scope;
133	      cachedToken = {
134	        token: json.access_token,
135	        expiresAtMs: Date.now() + json.expires_in * 1000,
136	      };
137	      return json.access_token;
138	    }
139	    lastBody = await res.text();
140	    // Only fall through the chain on invalid_scope; other errors are terminal.
141	    if (!lastBody.includes('invalid_scope')) break;
142	  }
143	  throw new XeroAuthError(
144	    `XeroTokenError: could not obtain an accounting-scoped token. Last response: ` +
145	      `${lastBody.slice(0, 400)}. If this says invalid_scope, the api/.env creds are ` +
146	      `not a Custom Connection with accounting scopes — see HANDOFF.md.`,
147	  );
148	}
149	
150	export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
151	  if (cachedToken && cachedToken.expiresAtMs > Date.now() + 60_000) {
152	    return cachedToken.token;
153	  }
154	  return mintToken();
155	}
156	
157	export function invalidateToken(): void {
158	  cachedToken = null;
159	}
160	
161	/** Resolve the single-org tenant id (cached). Null for a clean CC that 400s. */
162	export async function resolveTenant(): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {
163	  if (tenantResolved) return tenant;
164	  tenantResolved = true;
165	  try {
166	    const token = await getAccessToken();
167	    const res = await fetch(CONNECTIONS_URL, {
168	      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: 'application/json' },
169	    });
170	    if (!res.ok) return null;
171	    const arr = (await res.json()) as Array<{ tenantId: string; tenantName: string }>;
172	    if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0) {
173	      tenant = { id: arr[0].tenantId, name: arr[0].tenantName };
174	    }
175	  } catch {
176	    tenant = null;
177	  }
178	  return tenant;
179	}
180	
181	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	// Error envelope parsing
183	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	
185	export interface XeroValidationError {
186	  Message?: string;
187	  Description?: string;
188	}
189	export interface XeroErrorElement {
190	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
191	  [k: string]: unknown;
192	}
193	export interface XeroApiException {
194	  ErrorNumber?: number;
195	  Type?: string;
196	  Message?: string;
197	  Elements?: XeroErrorElement[];
198	}
199	
200	export function flattenValidationErrors(env: XeroApiException): string[] {
201	  const out: string[] = [];
202	  for (const el of env.Elements ?? []) {
203	    for (const e of el.ValidationErrors ?? []) {
204	      const t = e.Message ?? e.Description;
205	      if (t) out.push(t);
206	    }
207	  }
208	  return out;
209	}
210	
211	export class XeroHttpError extends Error {
212	  constructor(
213	    readonly status: number,
214	    readonly envelope: XeroApiException | null,
215	    readonly rawBody: string,
216	  ) {
217	    const details = envelope ? flattenValidationErrors(envelope) : [];
218	    super(
219	      `XeroHttpError ${status}: ${envelope?.Message ?? rawBody.slice(0, 300)}` +
220	        (details.length ? ` | ${details.join('; ')}` : ''),
221	    );
222	    this.name = 'XeroHttpError';
223	  }
224	}
225	
226	export interface StatusAttributed {
227	  StatusAttributeString?: 'OK' | 'WARNING' | 'ERROR';
228	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
229	}
230	
231	export function assertElementOk<T extends StatusAttributed>(el: T, ctx: string): T {
232	  if (el.StatusAttributeString === 'ERROR') {
233	    const msg = (el.ValidationErrors ?? [])
234	      .map((e) => e.Message ?? e.Description ?? 'unknown')
235	      .join('; ');
236	    throw new Error(`XeroValidationError: ${ctx}: ${msg}`);
237	  }
238	  return el;
239	}
240	
241	export interface Pagination {
242	  page: number;
243	  pageSize: number;
244	  pageCount: number;
245	  itemCount: number;
246	}
247	
248	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
249	// Core request
250	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
251	
252	export interface XeroRequestInit {
253	  method?: 'GET' | 'PUT' | 'POST';
254	  json?: unknown;
255	  body?: Uint8Array;
256	  contentType?: string;
257	  query?: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined>;
258	  idempotencyKey?: string;
259	  ifModifiedSince?: Date;
260	  accept?: string;
261	  /** Absolute base override (e.g. reports live under the same base). */
262	  baseUrl?: string;
263	}
264	
265	const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
266	const sleep = (ms: number) => new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));
267	
268	export async function xeroRequest(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<Response> {
269	  const base = init.baseUrl ?? ACCOUNTING_BASE;
270	  const url = new URL(`${base}${path}`);
271	  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(init.query ?? {})) {
272	    if (v !== undefined) url.searchParams.set(k, String(v));
273	  }
274	
275	  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Accept: init.accept ?? 'application/json' };
276	  if (init.json !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json';
277	  if (init.body !== undefined) {
278	    if (!init.contentType) throw new Error('MissingContentType: raw uploads need a MIME type');
279	    headers['Content-Type'] = init.contentType;
280	  }
281	  if (init.idempotencyKey !== undefined) {
282	    if (init.idempotencyKey.length > 128) {
283	      throw new Error(`IdempotencyKeyTooLong: ${init.idempotencyKey.length} > 128`);
284	    }
285	    headers['Idempotency-Key'] = init.idempotencyKey;
286	  }
287	  if (init.ifModifiedSince !== undefined) {
288	    headers['If-Modified-Since'] = init.ifModifiedSince.toUTCString();
289	  }
290	
291	  // A resolved tenant id is safe to send on a CC and required if the token is
292	  // ever standard-app shaped. Omitted when unresolved (clean CC works without).
293	  const t = await resolveTenant();
294	  if (t) headers['Xero-Tenant-Id'] = t.id;
295	
296	  let body: string | ArrayBuffer | undefined;
297	  if (init.json !== undefined) body = JSON.stringify(init.json);
298	  else if (init.body !== undefined) {
299	    const buf = new ArrayBuffer(init.body.byteLength);
300	    new Uint8Array(buf).set(init.body);
301	    body = buf;
302	  }
303	
304	  for (let attempt = 0; ; attempt++) {
305	    headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${await getAccessToken()}`;
306	    const res = await fetch(url, { method: init.method ?? 'GET', headers, body });
307	
308	    if (res.status === 401 && attempt < MAX_RETRIES) {
309	      invalidateToken();
310	      continue;
311	    }
312	    if (res.status === 429 && attempt < MAX_RETRIES) {
313	      const retryAfter = Number(res.headers.get('retry-after') ?? '0');
314	      const waitMs = retryAfter > 0 ? retryAfter * 1000 : 1000;
315	      // eslint-disable-next-line no-console
316	      console.warn(`[xero] 429 (${res.headers.get('x-rate-limit-problem') ?? '?'}) retry ${waitMs}ms`);
317	      await sleep(waitMs);
318	      continue;
319	    }
320	    if (!res.ok) {
321	      const raw = await res.text();
322	      let env: XeroApiException | null = null;
323	      try {
324	        env = JSON.parse(raw) as XeroApiException;
325	      } catch {
326	        env = null;
327	      }
328	      throw new XeroHttpError(res.status, env, raw);
329	    }
330	    return res;
331	  }
332	}
333	
334	export async function xeroFetch<T>(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<T> {
335	  const res = await xeroRequest(path, init);
336	  return (await res.json()) as T;
337	}
338	
339	/** True if we can currently obtain an accounting-capable token + tenant. */
340	export async function xeroHealthCheck(): Promise<{
341	  ok: boolean;
342	  orgName: string | null;
343	  scope: string | null;
344	  reason: string | null;
345	}> {
346	  try {
347	    await getAccessToken();
348	    const t = await resolveTenant();
349	    // A working CC either resolves a tenant OR serves accounting without one.
350	    // Probe a cheap read to be sure.
351	    await xeroFetch('/Organisation', {});
352	    return { ok: true, orgName: t?.name ?? 'Xero organisation', scope: workingScopeString, reason: null };
353	  } catch (e) {
354	    const t = tenant;
355	    // If /Organisation 403s but we have a tenant + token, accounting reads still work.
356	    if (e instanceof XeroHttpError && e.status === 403 && t) {
357	      return { ok: true, orgName: t.name, scope: workingScopeString, reason: null };
358	    }
359	    return {
360	      ok: false,
361	      orgName: null,
362	      scope: workingScopeString,
363	      reason: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e),
364	    };
365	  }
366	}
367
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./api/src/modules/connections/connections.service.ts`

```text
1	import { Injectable, Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
2	import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
3	import { Repository } from 'typeorm';
4	import {
5	  ConnectionState,
6	  ConnectionKind,
7	  ConnectionStatus,
8	  AuditActor,
9	} from '../../entities';
10	import { XeroService } from '../xero/xero.service';
11	import { AuditService } from '../audit/audit.service';
12	import { ConnectionRowDto } from './dto/connection-row.dto';
13	
14	// The Connections panel. READ + refresh only. Owns the XERO row (computed live
15	// from Xero.health()) and surfaces the CALENDAR/EMAIL rows that their own
16	// modules upsert. It never triggers calendar/email jobs — those have their own
17	// "run now" endpoints (POST /meetings/sync, POST /email/poll).
18	@Injectable()
19	export class ConnectionsService {
20	  private readonly log = new Logger('ConnectionsService');
21	
22	  constructor(
23	    @InjectRepository(ConnectionState)
24	    private readonly repo: Repository<ConnectionState>,
25	    private readonly xero: XeroService,
26	    private readonly audit: AuditService,
27	  ) {}
28	
29	  // GET /connections — three rows in a stable order: XERO, CALENDAR, EMAIL.
30	  // XERO is probed live (no persistence side effect on a read); CALENDAR/EMAIL
31	  // are read from their ConnectionState rows, defaulting to DOWN if never run.
32	  async list(): Promise<ConnectionRowDto[]> {
33	    const xeroRow = await this.probeXero();
34	    const calendarRow = await this.readRow(
35	      ConnectionKind.CALENDAR,
36	      'Calendar not yet connected. Run a sync to ingest meetings.',
37	    );
38	    const emailRow = await this.readRow(
39	      ConnectionKind.EMAIL,
40	      'Mailbox not yet polled. Run a poll to check for client agreements.',
41	    );
42	    return [xeroRow, calendarRow, emailRow];
43	  }
44	
45	  // POST /connections/xero/recheck — force a fresh Xero health probe, persist the
46	  // XERO ConnectionState row, audit the probe, and return the refreshed row.
47	  async recheckXero(): Promise<ConnectionRowDto> {
48	    const row = await this.probeXero();
49	
50	    // Upsert the XERO ConnectionState row (idempotent by unique `kind`).
51	    let entity = await this.repo.findOne({ where: { kind: ConnectionKind.XERO } });
52	    if (!entity) {
53	      entity = this.repo.create({ kind: ConnectionKind.XERO });
54	    }
55	    entity.status = row.status;
56	    entity.label = row.label;
57	    entity.detail = row.detail;
58	    entity.lastSyncAt = row.lastSyncAt ? new Date(row.lastSyncAt) : null;
59	    entity.nextPollAt = null;
60	    await this.repo.save(entity);
61	
62	    await this.audit.record({
63	      actor: AuditActor.SYSTEM,
64	      action: 'connection.xero.recheck',
65	      summary:
66	        row.status === ConnectionStatus.LIVE
67	          ? `Xero health check: LIVE (${row.label ?? 'org'})`
68	          : `Xero health check: DOWN — ${row.detail ?? 'unknown reason'}`,
69	      subjectType: 'ConnectionState',
70	      subjectId: entity.id,
71	      inputs: { status: row.status, label: row.label, detail: row.detail },
72	    });
73	
74	    return row;
75	  }
76	
77	  // Live Xero probe → a XERO row. Xero has no FALLBACK: it is LIVE (creds working)
78	  // or DOWN (creds pending / probe failed). Never crashes on a Xero outage.
79	  private async probeXero(): Promise<ConnectionRowDto> {
80	    const now = new Date();
81	    try {
82	      const h = await this.xero.health();
83	      if (h.ok) {
84	        return {
85	          kind: ConnectionKind.XERO,
86	          status: ConnectionStatus.LIVE,
87	          label: h.orgName ?? 'Xero organisation',
88	          detail: h.scope
89	            ? `Connected via Custom Connection. Scopes: ${h.scope}`
90	            : 'Connected via Custom Connection.',
91	          lastSyncAt: now.toISOString(),
92	          nextPollAt: null,
93	        };
94	      }
95	      return {
96	        kind: ConnectionKind.XERO,
97	        status: ConnectionStatus.DOWN,
98	        label: h.orgName,
99	        detail: h.reason ?? 'Xero not connected — credentials pending.',
100	        lastSyncAt: null,
101	        nextPollAt: null,
102	      };
103	    } catch (err) {
104	      const reason = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
105	      this.log.warn(`Xero health probe threw: ${reason}`);
106	      return {
107	        kind: ConnectionKind.XERO,
108	        status: ConnectionStatus.DOWN,
109	        label: null,
110	        detail: `Xero health probe failed: ${reason}`,
111	        lastSyncAt: null,
112	        nextPollAt: null,
113	      };
114	    }
115	  }
116	
117	  // Read a persisted ConnectionState row (CALENDAR/EMAIL). Defaults to a DOWN
118	  // row with a helpful detail when the owning module has never run its job.
119	  private async readRow(
120	    kind: ConnectionKind,
121	    neverRunDetail: string,
122	  ): Promise<ConnectionRowDto> {
123	    const row = await this.repo.findOne({ where: { kind } });
124	    if (!row) {
125	      return {
126	        kind,
127	        status: ConnectionStatus.DOWN,
128	        label: null,
129	        detail: neverRunDetail,
130	        lastSyncAt: null,
131	        nextPollAt: null,
132	      };
133	    }
134	    return {
135	      kind: row.kind,
136	      status: row.status,
137	      label: row.label,
138	      detail: row.detail,
139	      lastSyncAt: row.lastSyncAt ? row.lastSyncAt.toISOString() : null,
140	      nextPollAt: row.nextPollAt ? row.nextPollAt.toISOString() : null,
141	    };
142	  }
143	}
144
```

```text
1	import { Controller, Get, Post, HttpCode } from '@nestjs/common';
2	import { ApiTags, ApiOperation, ApiOkResponse } from '@nestjs/swagger';
3	import { ConnectionsService } from './connections.service';
4	import { ConnectionRowDto } from './dto/connection-row.dto';
5	
6	// Served at /api/connections (global 'api' prefix applied centrally).
7	@ApiTags('connections')
8	@Controller('connections')
9	export class ConnectionsController {
10	  constructor(private readonly connections: ConnectionsService) {}
11	
12	  @Get()
13	  @ApiOperation({
14	    summary: 'List integration health',
15	    description:
16	      'Three rows — XERO (probed live from Xero health), CALENDAR and EMAIL (read from their ConnectionState rows, DOWN if never run). Read-only: does not trigger any sync/poll job.',
17	  })
18	  @ApiOkResponse({ type: ConnectionRowDto, isArray: true })
19	  async list(): Promise<ConnectionRowDto[]> {
20	    return this.connections.list();
21	  }
22	
23	  @Post('xero/recheck')
24	  @HttpCode(200)
25	  @ApiOperation({
26	    summary: 'Re-probe Xero health',
27	    description:
28	      'Forces a fresh Xero health check, upserts the XERO ConnectionState row, records an audit event, and returns the refreshed row. The "check now" for Xero. Never throws when Xero is down — returns a DOWN row with the reason.',
29	  })
30	  @ApiOkResponse({ type: ConnectionRowDto })
31	  async recheckXero(): Promise<ConnectionRowDto> {
32	    return this.connections.recheckXero();
33	  }
34	}
35
```

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useEffect, useMemo, useRef } from "react";
4	import { MapPin } from "lucide-react";
5	import { CalendarEvent, Calendar } from "@/interfaces/calendarInterface";
6	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
7	import {
8	  HOUR_HEIGHT,
9	  HOURS_IN_DAY,
10	  formatEventTimeRange,
11	  formatHourLabel,
12	  getEventColor,
13	  getWeekDays,
14	  hexToRgba,
15	  isToday,
16	  toDateKey,
17	  eventOverlapsDate,
18	} from "@/lib/calendar-utils";
19	
20	interface PositionedEvent {
21	  event: CalendarEvent;
22	  top: number;
23	  height: number;
24	  left: number;
25	  width: number;
26	}
27	
28	interface AllDaySpan {
29	  event: CalendarEvent;
30	  startCol: number;
31	  span: number;
32	}
33	
34	interface WeekViewProps {
35	  currentDate: Date;
36	  events: CalendarEvent[];
37	  calendars: Calendar[];
38	  onEventClick: (event: CalendarEvent) => void;
39	  onSlotClick?: (date: Date, hour: number) => void;
40	}
41	
42	function calculateTimedEventPosition(
43	  event: CalendarEvent,
44	  date: Date
45	): { top: number; height: number; startTime: number; endTime: number } | null {
46	  const dateKey = toDateKey(date);
47	  const eventStart = new Date(event.startTime);
48	  const eventEnd = new Date(event.endTime);
49	  const eventStartKey = toDateKey(eventStart);
50	  const eventEndKey = toDateKey(eventEnd);
51	
52	  if (dateKey < eventStartKey || dateKey > eventEndKey) {
53	    return null;
54	  }
55	
56	  let startMinutes = 0;
57	  let endMinutes = HOURS_IN_DAY * 60;
58	
59	  if (dateKey === eventStartKey) {
60	    startMinutes = eventStart.getHours() * 60 + eventStart.getMinutes();
61	  }
62	
63	  if (dateKey === eventEndKey) {
64	    endMinutes = eventEnd.getHours() * 60 + eventEnd.getMinutes();
65	  }
66	
67	  const top = (startMinutes / 60) * HOUR_HEIGHT;
68	  const height = Math.max(
69	    ((endMinutes - startMinutes) / 60) * HOUR_HEIGHT,
70	    22
71	  );
72	
73	  return { top, height, startTime: startMinutes, endTime: endMinutes };
74	}
75	
76	function layoutTimedEvents(
77	  dayEvents: CalendarEvent[],
78	  date: Date
79	): PositionedEvent[] {
80	  type TimedEvent = PositionedEvent & { startTime: number; endTime: number };
81	
82	  const positioned: TimedEvent[] = dayEvents
83	    .map((event) => {
84	      const pos = calculateTimedEventPosition(event, date);
85	      if (!pos) return null;
86	      return {
87	        event,
88	        top: pos.top,
89	        height: pos.height,
90	        left: 0,
91	        width: 100,
92	        startTime: pos.startTime,
93	        endTime: pos.endTime,
94	      };
95	    })
96	    .filter((entry): entry is TimedEvent => entry !== null)
97	    .sort((a, b) => a.startTime - b.startTime);
98	
99	  const groups: TimedEvent[][] = [];
100	
101	  positioned.forEach((event) => {
102	    const overlappingIndices: number[] = [];
103	
104	    groups.forEach((group, index) => {
105	      const overlaps = group.some(
106	        (item) =>
107	          event.startTime < item.endTime && event.endTime > item.startTime
108	      );
109	      if (overlaps) overlappingIndices.push(index);
110	    });
111	
112	    if (overlappingIndices.length === 0) {
113	      groups.push([event]);
114	      return;
115	    }
116	
117	    if (overlappingIndices.length === 1) {
118	      groups[overlappingIndices[0]].push(event);
119	      return;
120	    }
121	
122	    const mergedGroup = [event];
123	    overlappingIndices.forEach((index) => {
124	      mergedGroup.push(...groups[index]);
125	    });
126	    overlappingIndices
127	      .sort((a, b) => b - a)
128	      .forEach((index) => groups.splice(index, 1));
129	    groups.push(mergedGroup);
130	  });
131	
132	  const result: PositionedEvent[] = [];
133	
134	  groups.forEach((group) => {
135	    if (group.length === 1) {
136	      result.push({
137	        event: group[0].event,
138	        top: group[0].top,
139	        height: group[0].height,
140	        left: 0,
141	        width: 100,
142	      });
143	      return;
144	    }
145	
146	    const columns: TimedEvent[][] = [];
147	
148	    group.forEach((event) => {
149	      let placed = false;
150	      for (const column of columns) {
151	        const hasOverlap = column.some(
152	          (item) =>
153	            event.startTime < item.endTime && event.endTime > item.startTime
154	        );
155	        if (!hasOverlap) {
156	          column.push(event);
157	          placed = true;
158	          break;
159	        }
160	      }
161	      if (!placed) columns.push([event]);
162	    });
163	
164	    const totalColumns = columns.length;
165	    columns.forEach((column, columnIndex) => {
166	      column.forEach((event) => {
167	        result.push({
168	          event: event.event,
169	          top: event.top,
170	          height: event.height,
171	          left: (columnIndex * 100) / totalColumns,
172	          width: 100 / totalColumns,
173	        });
174	      });
175	    });
176	  });
177	
178	  return result;
179	}
180	
181	function buildAllDaySpans(
182	  events: CalendarEvent[],
183	  weekDays: Date[]
184	): AllDaySpan[] {
185	  const weekStartKey = toDateKey(weekDays[0]);
186	  const weekEndKey = toDateKey(weekDays[6]);
187	
188	  return events
189	    .filter((event) => event.isAllDay)
190	    .map((event) => {
191	      const eventStartKey = toDateKey(new Date(event.startTime));
192	      const eventEndKey = toDateKey(new Date(event.endTime));
193	
194	      if (eventEndKey < weekStartKey || eventStartKey > weekEndKey) {
195	        return null;
196	      }
197	
198	      const visibleStartKey =
199	        eventStartKey < weekStartKey ? weekStartKey : eventStartKey;
200	      const visibleEndKey =
201	        eventEndKey > weekEndKey ? weekEndKey : eventEndKey;
202	
203	      const startCol = weekDays.findIndex(
204	        (day) => toDateKey(day) === visibleStartKey
205	      );
206	      const endCol = weekDays.findIndex(
207	        (day) => toDateKey(day) === visibleEndKey
208	      );
209	
210	      if (startCol === -1 || endCol === -1) return null;
211	
212	      return {
213	        event,
214	        startCol,
215	        span: endCol - startCol + 1,
216	      };
217	    })
218	    .filter((entry): entry is AllDaySpan => entry !== null);
219	}
220	
221	export function WeekView({
222	  currentDate,
223	  events,
224	  calendars,
225	  onEventClick,
226	  onSlotClick,
227	}: WeekViewProps) {
228	  const scrollRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
229	  const weekDays = useMemo(() => getWeekDays(currentDate), [currentDate]);
230	  const dayNames = ["Sun", "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat"];
231	
232	  const allDaySpans = useMemo(
233	    () => buildAllDaySpans(events, weekDays),
234	    [events, weekDays]
235	  );
236	
237	  const now = new Date();
238	  const showNowIndicator = weekDays.some((day) => isToday(day));
239	  const nowTop =
240	    (now.getHours() + now.getMinutes() / 60) * HOUR_HEIGHT;
241	
242	  useEffect(() => {
243	    const container = scrollRef.current;
244	    if (!container) return;
245	
246	    const currentHour = new Date().getHours();
247	    const targetHour = showNowIndicator
248	      ? Math.max(currentHour - 2, 6)
249	      : 8;
250	    container.scrollTop = targetHour * HOUR_HEIGHT;
251	  }, [currentDate, showNowIndicator]);
252	
253	  const renderEventBlock = (
254	    event: CalendarEvent,
255	    day: Date,
256	    positioned: PositionedEvent
257	  ) => {
258	    const color = getEventColor(event, calendars);
259	
260	    return (
261	      <button
262	        key={`${event.id}-${toDateKey(day)}`}
263	        type="button"
264	        className={cn(
265	          "absolute overflow-hidden rounded-md px-1.5 py-1 text-left cursor-pointer pointer-events-auto",
266	          "border-l-[3px] transition-opacity hover:opacity-90 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring"
267	        )}
268	        style={{
269	          top: `${positioned.top}px`,
270	          height: `${positioned.height}px`,
271	          left: `calc(${positioned.left}% + 2px)`,
272	          width: `calc(${positioned.width}% - 4px)`,
273	          backgroundColor: hexToRgba(color, 0.22),
274	          borderLeftColor: color,
275	        }}
276	        onClick={() => onEventClick(event)}
277	        title={`${event.title}\n${formatEventTimeRange(event, day)}`}
278	      >
279	        <div className="text-xs font-semibold leading-tight truncate text-foreground">
280	          {event.title}
281	        </div>
282	        {positioned.height > 28 && (
283	          <div className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground truncate">
284	            {formatEventTimeRange(event, day)}
285	          </div>
286	        )}
287	        {event.location && positioned.height > 44 && (
288	          <div className="mt-0.5 flex items-center gap-1 text-[10px] text-muted-foreground truncate">
289	            <MapPin className="h-3 w-3 shrink-0" />
290	            <span className="truncate">{event.location}</span>
291	          </div>
292	        )}
293	      </button>
294	    );
295	  };
296	
297	  return (
298	    <div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden rounded-lg border bg-background">
299	      <div className="flex shrink-0 border-b bg-muted/30">
300	        <div className="w-10 shrink-0 border-r md:w-14" />
301	        <div className="grid flex-1 grid-cols-7">
302	          {weekDays.map((day) => (
303	            <div
304	              key={toDateKey(day)}
305	              className={cn(
306	                "border-r px-2 py-2 text-center last:border-r-0",
307	                isToday(day) && "bg-primary/5"
308	              )}
309	            >
310	              <div className="text-[11px] font-medium uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground">
311	                {dayNames[day.getDay()]}
312	              </div>
313	              <div
314	                className={cn(
315	                  "mx-auto mt-1 flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-lg font-semibold",
316	                  isToday(day) && "bg-primary text-primary-foreground"
317	                )}
318	              >
319	                {day.getDate()}
320	              </div>
321	            </div>
322	          ))}
323	        </div>
324	      </div>
325	
326	      {allDaySpans.length > 0 && (
327	        <div className="flex shrink-0 border-b bg-muted/10">
328	          <div className="flex w-10 shrink-0 items-center justify-end border-r pr-1 text-[10px] text-muted-foreground md:w-14 md:pr-2">
329	            All day
330	          </div>
331	          <div className="relative grid flex-1 grid-cols-7 min-h-[36px] py-1">
332	            {weekDays.map((day) => (
333	              <div
334	                key={`allday-${toDateKey(day)}`}
335	                className="border-r last:border-r-0"
336	              />
337	            ))}
338	            {allDaySpans.map(({ event, startCol, span }) => {
339	              const color = getEventColor(event, calendars);
340	              return (
341	                <button
342	                  key={`allday-${event.id}`}
343	                  type="button"
344	                  className="absolute mx-0.5 rounded px-2 py-1 text-left text-xs font-medium truncate cursor-pointer hover:opacity-90 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring"
345	                  style={{
346	                    left: `calc(${(startCol / 7) * 100}% + 2px)`,
347	                    width: `calc(${(span / 7) * 100}% - 4px)`,
348	                    backgroundColor: hexToRgba(color, 0.25),
349	                    borderLeft: `3px solid ${color}`,
350	                  }}
351	                  onClick={() => onEventClick(event)}
352	                >
353	                  {event.title}
354	                </button>
355	              );
356	            })}
357	          </div>
358	        </div>
359	      )}
360	
361	      <div ref={scrollRef} className="relative min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto">
362	        <div className="flex">
363	          <div className="sticky left-0 z-20 w-10 shrink-0 border-r bg-background md:w-14">
364	            {Array.from({ length: HOURS_IN_DAY }, (_, hour) => (
365	              <div
366	                key={hour}
367	                className="relative border-b text-right text-[10px] text-muted-foreground md:text-[11px]"
368	                style={{ height: `${HOUR_HEIGHT}px` }}
369	              >
370	                {hour > 0 && (
371	                  <span className="absolute -top-2 right-1 md:right-2">
372	                    {formatHourLabel(hour)}
373	                  </span>
374	                )}
375	              </div>
376	            ))}
377	          </div>
378	
379	          <div className="relative grid flex-1 grid-cols-7">
380	            {weekDays.map((day) => {
381	              const timedEvents = events.filter(
382	                (event) => !event.isAllDay && eventOverlapsDate(event, day)
383	              );
384	              const positionedEvents = layoutTimedEvents(timedEvents, day);
385	
386	              return (
387	                <div
388	                  key={toDateKey(day)}
389	                  className={cn(
390	                    "relative border-r last:border-r-0",
391	                    isToday(day) && "bg-primary/[0.03]"
392	                  )}
393	                  style={{ minHeight: `${HOUR_HEIGHT * HOURS_IN_DAY}px` }}
394	                >
395	                  {Array.from({ length: HOURS_IN_DAY }, (_, hour) => (
396	                    <button
397	                      key={hour}
398	                      type="button"
399	                      className="relative z-0 block w-full border-b cursor-pointer hover:bg-accent/30 transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring"
400	                      style={{ height: `${HOUR_HEIGHT}px` }}
401	                      onClick={() => onSlotClick?.(day, hour)}
402	                      aria-label={`Create event on ${day.toDateString()} at ${formatHourLabel(hour)}`}
403	                    />
404	                  ))}
405	
406	                  <div className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-10">
407	                    {positionedEvents.map((positioned) =>
408	                      renderEventBlock(positioned.event, day, positioned)
409	                    )}
410	                  </div>
411	                </div>
412	              );
413	            })}
414	
415	          </div>
416	        </div>
417	
418	        {showNowIndicator && (
419	          <div
420	            className="pointer-events-none absolute left-0 right-0 z-10 flex items-center"
421	            style={{ top: `${nowTop}px` }}
422	          >
423	            <div className="flex w-10 shrink-0 justify-end pr-1 md:w-14">
424	              <span className="h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-red-500" />
425	            </div>
426	            <div className="h-px flex-1 bg-red-500" />
427	          </div>
428	        )}
429	      </div>
430	    </div>
431	  );
432	}
433
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useMemo } from "react";
4	import { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from "lucide-react";
5	import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
6	import { Calendar } from "@/interfaces/calendarInterface";
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
8	import { isSameDay, isToday, toDateKey } from "@/lib/calendar-utils";
9	
10	interface CalendarSidebarProps {
11	  currentDate: Date;
12	  calendars: Calendar[];
13	  onDateChange: (date: Date) => void;
14	}
15	
16	const DAY_LABELS = ["S", "M", "T", "W", "T", "F", "S"];
17	const MONTH_NAMES = [
18	  "January",
19	  "February",
20	  "March",
21	  "April",
22	  "May",
23	  "June",
24	  "July",
25	  "August",
26	  "September",
27	  "October",
28	  "November",
29	  "December",
30	];
31	
32	export function CalendarSidebarContent({
33	  currentDate,
34	  calendars,
35	  onDateChange,
36	}: CalendarSidebarProps) {
37	  const miniMonth = currentDate.getMonth();
38	  const miniYear = currentDate.getFullYear();
39	
40	  const miniDays = useMemo(() => {
41	    const firstDay = new Date(miniYear, miniMonth, 1);
42	    const lastDay = new Date(miniYear, miniMonth + 1, 0);
43	    const days: (Date | null)[] = [];
44	
45	    for (let i = 0; i < firstDay.getDay(); i++) {
46	      days.push(null);
47	    }
48	
49	    for (let day = 1; day <= lastDay.getDate(); day++) {
50	      days.push(new Date(miniYear, miniMonth, day));
51	    }
52	
53	    return days;
54	  }, [miniMonth, miniYear]);
55	
56	  const weekDayKeys = useMemo(() => {
57	    const start = new Date(currentDate);
58	    start.setDate(currentDate.getDate() - currentDate.getDay());
59	    return Array.from({ length: 7 }, (_, index) => {
60	      const day = new Date(start);
61	      day.setDate(start.getDate() + index);
62	      return toDateKey(day);
63	    });
64	  }, [currentDate]);
65	
66	  const shiftMiniMonth = (delta: number) => {
67	    onDateChange(new Date(miniYear, miniMonth + delta, 1));
68	  };
69	
70	  return (
71	    <>
72	      <div className="p-4 border-b">
73	        <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-3">
74	          <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold">
75	            {MONTH_NAMES[miniMonth]} {miniYear}
76	          </h3>
77	          <div className="flex gap-0.5">
78	            <Button
79	              variant="ghost"
80	              size="icon"
81	              className="h-7 w-7 cursor-pointer"
82	              onClick={() => shiftMiniMonth(-1)}
83	            >
84	              <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
85	            </Button>
86	            <Button
87	              variant="ghost"
88	              size="icon"
89	              className="h-7 w-7 cursor-pointer"
90	              onClick={() => shiftMiniMonth(1)}
91	            >
92	              <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />
93	            </Button>
94	          </div>
95	        </div>
96	
97	        <div className="grid grid-cols-7 gap-0.5 mb-1">
98	          {DAY_LABELS.map((label, index) => (
99	            <div
100	              key={`${label}-${index}`}
101	              className="text-center text-[10px] font-medium text-muted-foreground py-1"
102	            >
103	              {label}
104	            </div>
105	          ))}
106	        </div>
107	
108	        <div className="grid grid-cols-7 gap-0.5">
109	          {miniDays.map((date, index) => {
110	            if (!date) {
111	              return <div key={`empty-${index}`} className="h-8" />;
112	            }
113	
114	            const inCurrentWeek = weekDayKeys.includes(toDateKey(date));
115	
116	            return (
117	              <button
118	                key={toDateKey(date)}
119	                type="button"
120	                onClick={() => onDateChange(date)}
121	                className={cn(
122	                  "h-8 w-full rounded-full text-xs font-medium cursor-pointer transition-colors",
123	                  "hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring",
124	                  inCurrentWeek && !isToday(date) && "bg-accent/60",
125	                  isToday(date) &&
126	                    "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90"
127	                )}
128	              >
129	                {date.getDate()}
130	              </button>
131	            );
132	          })}
133	        </div>
134	      </div>
135	
136	      <div className="p-4">
137	        <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground mb-3">
138	          My Calendars
139	        </h3>
140	        <ul className="space-y-2">
141	          {calendars.map((calendar) => (
142	            <li key={calendar.id} className="flex items-center gap-2.5 text-sm">
143	              <span
144	                className="h-3 w-3 shrink-0 rounded-sm"
145	                style={{
146	                  backgroundColor: calendar.calendarColor || "#3b82f6",
147	                }}
148	              />
149	              <span className="truncate">{calendar.calendarName}</span>
150	            </li>
151	          ))}
152	        </ul>
153	      </div>
154	    </>
155	  );
156	}
157	
158	export function CalendarSidebar(props: CalendarSidebarProps) {
159	  return (
160	    <aside className="hidden lg:flex w-64 shrink-0 flex-col border-r bg-muted/20 overflow-y-auto">
161	      <CalendarSidebarContent {...props} />
162	    </aside>
163	  );
164	}
165
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
