<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard/components/calendar/DayView.tsx`

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useMemo } from "react";
4	import { CalendarEvent, Calendar } from "@/interfaces/calendarInterface";
5	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
6	
7	interface PositionedEvent {
8	  event: CalendarEvent;
9	  top: number;
10	  height: number;
11	  left: number;
12	  width: number;
13	}
14	
15	interface DayViewProps {
16	  currentDate: Date;
17	  events: CalendarEvent[];
18	  calendars: Calendar[];
19	  onEventClick: (event: CalendarEvent) => void;
20	}
21	
22	export function DayView({ currentDate, events, calendars, onEventClick }: DayViewProps) {
23	  // Get calendar color by provider ID
24	  const getCalendarColor = (providerId: string) => {
25	    const calendar = calendars.find(c => c.id === providerId);
26	    return calendar?.calendarColor || '#3b82f6';
27	  };
28	
29	  // Generate time slots (24 hours)
30	  const timeSlots = useMemo(() => {
31	    const slots: string[] = [];
32	    for (let hour = 0; hour < 24; hour++) {
33	      const period = hour >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM';
34	      const displayHour = hour === 0 ? 12 : hour > 12 ? hour - 12 : hour;
35	      slots.push(`${displayHour}:00 ${period}`);
36	    }
37	    return slots;
38	  }, []);
39	
40	  // Height of one hour in pixels
41	  const HOUR_HEIGHT = 80;
42	
43	  // Calculate event position and dimensions
44	  const calculateEventPosition = (event: CalendarEvent): { top: number; height: number } | null => {
45	    const dateStr = currentDate.toISOString().split('T')[0];
46	    const eventStart = new Date(event.startTime);
47	    const eventEnd = new Date(event.endTime);
48	    const eventStartStr = eventStart.toISOString().split('T')[0];
49	    const eventEndStr = eventEnd.toISOString().split('T')[0];
50	
51	    // Check if event is on this date
52	    if (dateStr < eventStartStr || dateStr > eventEndStr) {
53	      return null;
54	    }
55	
56	    // For all-day events
57	    if (event.isAllDay) {
58	      return { top: 0, height: HOUR_HEIGHT };
59	    }
60	
61	    let startHour = 0;
62	    let startMinute = 0;
63	    let endHour = 24;
64	    let endMinute = 0;
65	
66	    if (dateStr === eventStartStr) {
67	      startHour = eventStart.getHours();
68	      startMinute = eventStart.getMinutes();
69	    }
70	
71	    if (dateStr === eventEndStr) {
72	      endHour = eventEnd.getHours();
73	      endMinute = eventEnd.getMinutes();
74	    }
75	
76	    const top = (startHour + startMinute / 60) * HOUR_HEIGHT;
77	    const bottom = (endHour + endMinute / 60) * HOUR_HEIGHT;
78	    const height = bottom - top;
79	
80	    return { top, height: Math.max(height, 30) }; // Minimum height of 30px
81	  };
82	
83	  // Get positioned events for the day
84	  const getPositionedEvents = (): PositionedEvent[] => {
85	    const dateStr = currentDate.toISOString().split('T')[0];
86	    
87	    // Filter events for this day
88	    const dayEvents = events.filter(event => {
89	      const eventStart = new Date(event.startTime);
90	      const eventEnd = new Date(event.endTime);
91	      const eventStartStr = eventStart.toISOString().split('T')[0];
92	      const eventEndStr = eventEnd.toISOString().split('T')[0];
93	      
94	      return dateStr >= eventStartStr && dateStr <= eventEndStr;
95	    });
96	
97	    // Separate all-day events from timed events
98	    const allDayEvents = dayEvents.filter(e => e.isAllDay);
99	    const timedEvents = dayEvents.filter(e => !e.isAllDay);
100	
101	    // Calculate positions for timed events only
102	    const positioned: Array<PositionedEvent & { startTime: number; endTime: number }> = timedEvents
103	      .map(event => {
104	        const pos = calculateEventPosition(event);
105	        if (!pos) return null;
106	
107	        const eventStart = new Date(event.startTime);
108	        const eventEnd = new Date(event.endTime);
109	        const eventStartStr = eventStart.toISOString().split('T')[0];
110	        const eventEndStr = eventEnd.toISOString().split('T')[0];
111	        
112	        // Calculate start/end times in minutes for this specific day
113	        let startTime = 0;
114	        let endTime = 24 * 60; // Default to full day
115	        
116	        // If event starts on this day, use actual start time
117	        if (dateStr === eventStartStr) {
118	          startTime = eventStart.getHours() * 60 + eventStart.getMinutes();
119	        }
120	        
121	        // If event ends on this day, use actual end time
122	        if (dateStr === eventEndStr) {
123	          endTime = eventEnd.getHours() * 60 + eventEnd.getMinutes();
124	        }
125	
126	        return {
127	          event,
128	          top: pos.top,
129	          height: pos.height,
130	          left: 0,
131	          width: 100,
132	          startTime,
133	          endTime
134	        };
135	      })
136	      .filter((e): e is PositionedEvent & { startTime: number; endTime: number } => e !== null)
137	      .sort((a, b) => a.startTime - b.startTime);
138	
139	    // Group overlapping events with proper merging
140	    const groups: Array<Array<PositionedEvent & { startTime: number; endTime: number }>> = [];
141	    
142	    positioned.forEach(event => {
143	      // Find ALL groups this event overlaps with
144	      const overlappingGroupIndices: number[] = [];
145	      
146	      groups.forEach((group, index) => {
147	        const overlaps = group.some(e => {
148	          return event.startTime < e.endTime && event.endTime > e.startTime;
149	        });
150	        if (overlaps) {
151	          overlappingGroupIndices.push(index);
152	        }
153	      });
154	      
155	      if (overlappingGroupIndices.length === 0) {
156	        // No overlap - create new group
157	        groups.push([event]);
158	      } else if (overlappingGroupIndices.length === 1) {
159	        // Overlaps with one group - add to it
160	        groups[overlappingGroupIndices[0]].push(event);
161	      } else {
162	        // Overlaps with multiple groups - merge them all
163	        const mergedGroup = [event];
164	        overlappingGroupIndices.forEach(index => {
165	          mergedGroup.push(...groups[index]);
166	        });
167	        
168	        // Remove old groups (in reverse order to maintain indices)
169	        overlappingGroupIndices.reverse().forEach(index => {
170	          groups.splice(index, 1);
171	        });
172	        
173	        // Add merged group
174	        groups.push(mergedGroup);
175	      }
176	    });
177	
178	    // Process each group independently
179	    const result: PositionedEvent[] = [];
180	    
181	    groups.forEach(group => {
182	      if (group.length === 1) {
183	        // Single event, no overlap - full width
184	        result.push({
185	          event: group[0].event,
186	          top: group[0].top,
187	          height: group[0].height,
188	          left: 0,
189	          width: 100
190	        });
191	      } else {
192	        // Multiple overlapping events - need to arrange in columns
193	        const columns: Array<Array<PositionedEvent & { startTime: number; endTime: number }>> = [];
194	        
195	        group.forEach(event => {
196	          // Find a column where this event doesn't overlap
197	          let placed = false;
198	          for (const column of columns) {
199	            const hasOverlap = column.some(e => {
200	              return event.startTime < e.endTime && event.endTime > e.startTime;
201	            });
202	            
203	            if (!hasOverlap) {
204	              column.push(event);
205	              placed = true;
206	              break;
207	            }
208	          }
209	          
210	          if (!placed) {
211	            columns.push([event]);
212	          }
213	        });
214	        
215	        const totalColumns = columns.length;
216	        
217	        columns.forEach((column, columnIndex) => {
218	          column.forEach(event => {
219	            const width = 100 / totalColumns;
220	            const left = (columnIndex * 100) / totalColumns;
221	            
222	            result.push({
223	              event: event.event,
224	              top: event.top,
225	              height: event.height,
226	              left,
227	              width
228	            });
229	          });
230	        });
231	      }
232	    });
233	
234	    // Add all-day events with full width at the top
235	    allDayEvents.forEach(event => {
236	      const pos = calculateEventPosition(event);
237	      if (pos) {
238	        result.push({
239	          event,
240	          top: pos.top,
241	          height: pos.height,
242	          left: 0,
243	          width: 100
244	        });
245	      }
246	    });
247	
248	    return result;
249	  };
250	
251	  // Format time for display
252	  const formatEventTime = (event: CalendarEvent) => {
253	    if (event.isAllDay) return 'All Day';
254	    
255	    const eventStart = new Date(event.startTime);
256	    const eventEnd = new Date(event.endTime);
257	
258	    const formatTime = (d: Date) => {
259	      const hours = d.getHours();
260	      const minutes = d.getMinutes();
261	      const period = hours >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM';
262	      const displayHour = hours === 0 ? 12 : hours > 12 ? hours - 12 : hours;
263	      return `${displayHour}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')} ${period}`;
264	    };
265	
266	    return `${formatTime(eventStart)} - ${formatTime(eventEnd)}`;
267	  };
268	
269	  const isToday = () => {
270	    const today = new Date();
271	    return currentDate.toDateString() === today.toDateString();
272	  };
273	
274	  const dayNames = ["Sunday", "Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday"];
275	  const monthNames = [
276	    "January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
277	    "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"
278	  ];
279	
280	  return (
281	    <div className="flex flex-col h-full border rounded-lg overflow-hidden">
282	      {/* Day Header */}
283	      <div className={cn(
284	        "p-4 border-b bg-muted",
285	        isToday() && "bg-primary/10"
286	      )}>
287	        <div className="text-sm font-medium text-muted-foreground">
288	          {dayNames[currentDate.getDay()]}
289	        </div>
290	        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1">
291	          <div
292	            className={cn(
293	              "text-2xl font-bold",
294	              isToday() && "inline-flex items-center justify-center w-10 h-10 rounded-full bg-primary text-primary-foreground"
295	            )}
296	          >
297	            {currentDate.getDate()}
298	          </div>
299	          <div className="text-xl font-semibold">
300	            {monthNames[currentDate.getMonth()]} {currentDate.getFullYear()}
301	          </div>
302	        </div>
303	      </div>
304	
305	      {/* Time Grid */}
306	      <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto relative">
307	        <div className="flex">
308	          {/* Time Labels Column */}
309	          <div className="w-16 md:w-24 flex-shrink-0 border-r bg-background sticky left-0 z-10">
310	            {timeSlots.map((timeSlot, hourIndex) => (
311	              <div
312	                key={hourIndex}
313	                className="text-xs md:text-sm text-muted-foreground text-right pr-2 md:pr-3 border-b"
314	                style={{ height: `${HOUR_HEIGHT}px` }}
315	              >
316	                {timeSlot}
317	              </div>
318	            ))}
319	          </div>
320	
321	          {/* Events Column */}
322	          <div
323	            className={cn(
324	              "flex-1 relative border-r",
325	              isToday() && "bg-primary/5"
326	            )}
327	            style={{ minHeight: `${HOUR_HEIGHT * 24}px` }}
328	          >
329	            {/* Hour Grid Lines */}
330	            {timeSlots.map((_, hourIndex) => (
331	              <div
332	                key={hourIndex}
333	                className="border-b"
334	                style={{ height: `${HOUR_HEIGHT}px` }}
335	              />
336	            ))}
337	
338	            {/* Events */}
339	            {getPositionedEvents().map((positioned) => (
340	              <div
341	                key={positioned.event.id}
342	                className="absolute px-2 py-1 rounded text-sm cursor-pointer hover:opacity-90 overflow-hidden transition-opacity shadow-sm"
343	                style={{
344	                  top: `${positioned.top}px`,
345	                  height: `${positioned.height}px`,
346	                  left: `${positioned.left}%`,
347	                  width: `${positioned.width}%`,
348	                  backgroundColor: positioned.event.eventColor || getCalendarColor(positioned.event.calendarProviderId),
349	                  color: '#ffffff',
350	                  border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)'
351	                }}
352	                onClick={() => onEventClick(positioned.event)}
353	                title={`${positioned.event.title}\n${formatEventTime(positioned.event)}`}
354	              >
355	                <div className="font-semibold">{positioned.event.title}</div>
356	                {positioned.height > 40 && (
357	                  <div className="text-xs opacity-90 mt-1">
358	                    {formatEventTime(positioned.event)}
359	                  </div>
360	                )}
361	                {positioned.event.location && positioned.height > 70 && (
362	                  <div className="text-xs opacity-80 mt-1">
363	                    📍 {positioned.event.location}
364	                  </div>
365	                )}
366	                {positioned.event.description && positioned.height > 100 && (
367	                  <div className="text-xs opacity-75 mt-2 line-clamp-2">
368	                    {positioned.event.description}
369	                  </div>
370	                )}
371	              </div>
372	            ))}
373	          </div>
374	        </div>
375	      </div>
376	    </div>
377	  );
378	}
379
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard/components/content-studio/McpServersTab.tsx`

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { Plus, Pencil, Trash2, ServerCog } from "lucide-react";
5	import { toast } from "sonner";
6	import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge";
7	import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
8	import { Card, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card";
9	import { Switch } from "@/components/ui/switch";
10	import {
11	  useGetMcpServersQuery,
12	  useUpdateMcpServerMutation,
13	  useDeleteMcpServerMutation,
14	} from "@/managers/apiManager";
15	import { McpServer } from "@/interfaces/contentStudioInterface";
16	import { McpServerDialog } from "@/components/content-studio/McpServerDialog";
17	
18	export function McpServersTab() {
19	  const { data: servers, isLoading } = useGetMcpServersQuery();
20	  const [update] = useUpdateMcpServerMutation();
21	  const [del] = useDeleteMcpServerMutation();
22	  const [dialogOpen, setDialogOpen] = useState(false);
23	  const [editing, setEditing] = useState<McpServer | undefined>();
24	
25	  const openNew = () => {
26	    setEditing(undefined);
27	    setDialogOpen(true);
28	  };
29	  const openEdit = (s: McpServer) => {
30	    setEditing(s);
31	    setDialogOpen(true);
32	  };
33	  const toggleEnabled = async (s: McpServer) => {
34	    try {
35	      await update({ id: s.id, data: { enabled: !s.enabled } }).unwrap();
36	    } catch (e: any) {
37	      toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? "Failed to update");
38	    }
39	  };
40	  const remove = async (s: McpServer) => {
41	    try {
42	      await del(s.id).unwrap();
43	      toast.success("Server removed");
44	    } catch (e: any) {
45	      toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? "Failed to delete");
46	    }
47	  };
48	
49	  return (
50	    <div>
51	      <div className="mb-4 flex items-start justify-between gap-3">
52	        <p className="max-w-md text-sm text-muted-foreground">
53	          External MCP servers the agent connects to at generation time. It discovers each server's
54	          tools and can call them while drafting.
55	        </p>
56	        <Button size="sm" className="shrink-0" onClick={openNew}>
57	          <Plus className="mr-1 h-4 w-4" /> Add server
58	        </Button>
59	      </div>
60	
61	      {isLoading ? (
62	        <div className="flex h-40 items-center justify-center">
63	          <div className="h-6 w-6 animate-spin rounded-full border-4 border-primary border-t-transparent" />
64	        </div>
65	      ) : !servers?.length ? (
66	        <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center rounded-lg border-2 border-dashed py-16 text-center">
67	          <ServerCog className="mb-2 h-8 w-8 text-muted-foreground" />
68	          <p className="font-medium">No MCP servers</p>
69	          <p className="mt-1 max-w-sm text-sm text-muted-foreground">
70	            Connect a remote MCP server (Streamable HTTP or SSE) to give the agent extra tools.
71	          </p>
72	          <Button className="mt-4" onClick={openNew}>
73	            <Plus className="mr-1 h-4 w-4" /> Add server
74	          </Button>
75	        </div>
76	      ) : (
77	        <div className="space-y-3">
78	          {servers.map((s) => (
79	            <Card key={s.id}>
80	              <CardContent className="flex items-start justify-between gap-4 p-4">
81	                <div className="min-w-0 flex-1">
82	                  <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
83	                    <h3 className="font-semibold">{s.name}</h3>
84	                    <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-[10px] uppercase">
85	                      {s.transport}
86	                    </Badge>
87	                    {s.authType !== "none" && (
88	                      <Badge variant="outline" className="text-[10px]">
89	                        {s.authType}
90	                      </Badge>
91	                    )}
92	                    {!s.enabled && (
93	                      <Badge variant="outline" className="text-[10px]">
94	                        disabled
95	                      </Badge>
96	                    )}
97	                  </div>
98	                  {s.description && (
99	                    <p className="mt-1 text-sm text-muted-foreground">{s.description}</p>
100	                  )}
101	                  <p className="mt-1 truncate font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground">{s.url}</p>
102	                  {s.toolAllowlist?.length > 0 && (
103	                    <p className="mt-1 text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
104	                      tools: {s.toolAllowlist.join(", ")}
105	                    </p>
106	                  )}
107	                  {s.lastError && (
108	                    <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-destructive">⚠ {s.lastError}</p>
109	                  )}
110	                </div>
111	                <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
112	                  <Switch
113	                    checked={s.enabled}
114	                    onCheckedChange={() => toggleEnabled(s)}
115	                    aria-label="Toggle enabled"
116	                  />
117	                  <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={() => openEdit(s)}>
118	                    <Pencil className="h-4 w-4" />
119	                  </Button>
120	                  <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={() => remove(s)}>
121	                    <Trash2 className="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" />
122	                  </Button>
123	                </div>
124	              </CardContent>
125	            </Card>
126	          ))}
127	        </div>
128	      )}
129	
130	      {dialogOpen && (
131	        <McpServerDialog
132	          key={editing?.id ?? "new"}
133	          server={editing}
134	          open={dialogOpen}
135	          onOpenChange={setDialogOpen}
136	        />
137	      )}
138	    </div>
139	  );
140	}
141
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard/components/content-studio/McpServerDialog.tsx`

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { CheckCircle2, XCircle } from "lucide-react";
5	import { toast } from "sonner";
6	import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
7	import {
8	  Dialog,
9	  DialogContent,
10	  DialogFooter,
11	  DialogHeader,
12	  DialogTitle,
13	} from "@/components/ui/dialog";
14	import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input";
15	import { Label } from "@/components/ui/label";
16	import {
17	  Select,
18	  SelectContent,
19	  SelectItem,
20	  SelectTrigger,
21	  SelectValue,
22	} from "@/components/ui/select";
23	import { Switch } from "@/components/ui/switch";
24	import { Textarea } from "@/components/ui/textarea";
25	import {
26	  useCreateMcpServerMutation,
27	  useUpdateMcpServerMutation,
28	  useTestMcpServerMutation,
29	} from "@/managers/apiManager";
30	import {
31	  McpAuthType,
32	  McpServer,
33	  McpServerTool,
34	  McpTransport,
35	} from "@/interfaces/contentStudioInterface";
36	
37	interface McpServerDialogProps {
38	  server?: McpServer;
39	  open: boolean;
40	  onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void;
41	}
42	
43	function parseHeaders(text: string): Record<string, string> {
44	  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
45	  for (const line of text.split("\n")) {
46	    const idx = line.indexOf(":");
47	    if (idx === -1) continue;
48	    const k = line.slice(0, idx).trim();
49	    const v = line.slice(idx + 1).trim();
50	    if (k) out[k] = v;
51	  }
52	  return out;
53	}
54	
55	export function McpServerDialog({ server, open, onOpenChange }: McpServerDialogProps) {
56	  const editing = !!server;
57	  const [name, setName] = useState(server?.name ?? "");
58	  const [description, setDescription] = useState(server?.description ?? "");
59	  const [url, setUrl] = useState(server?.url ?? "");
60	  const [transport, setTransport] = useState<McpTransport>(
61	    (server?.transport as McpTransport) ?? "http",
62	  );
63	  const [authType, setAuthType] = useState<McpAuthType>(
64	    (server?.authType as McpAuthType) ?? "none",
65	  );
66	  const [authToken, setAuthToken] = useState("");
67	  const [headersText, setHeadersText] = useState("");
68	  const [enabled, setEnabled] = useState(server?.enabled ?? true);
69	  const [allowlist, setAllowlist] = useState<string[]>(server?.toolAllowlist ?? []);
70	  const [tools, setTools] = useState<McpServerTool[]>([]);
71	
72	  const [create, { isLoading: creating }] = useCreateMcpServerMutation();
73	  const [update, { isLoading: updating }] = useUpdateMcpServerMutation();
74	  const [test, { isLoading: testing }] = useTestMcpServerMutation();
75	  const saving = creating || updating;
76	
77	  // When editing a server that already has a stored secret and the user hasn't
78	  // re-entered one, fall back to the stored secret (by id) for tests/saves.
79	  const noNewSecret = authToken.trim() === "" && headersText.trim() === "";
80	  const useStoredSecret = editing && authType !== "none" && noNewSecret && !!server?.authConfigured;
81	
82	  const buildAuthFields = () => {
83	    if (authType === "bearer" && authToken.trim()) return { authToken: authToken.trim() };
84	    if (authType === "headers" && headersText.trim()) {
85	      return { authHeaders: parseHeaders(headersText) };
86	    }
87	    return {};
88	  };
89	
90	  const runTest = async () => {
91	    if (!url.trim()) {
92	      toast.error("Enter a URL first");
93	      return;
94	    }
95	    try {
96	      const res = await test({
97	        name: name.trim() || "test",
98	        url: url.trim(),
99	        transport,
100	        authType,
101	        ...buildAuthFields(),
102	        ...(useStoredSecret ? { id: server!.id } : {}),
103	      }).unwrap();
104	      if (res.ok) {
105	        setTools(res.tools);
106	        toast.success(`Connected — ${res.tools.length} tool(s) found`);
107	      } else {
108	        setTools([]);
109	        toast.error(res.error ?? "Connection failed");
110	      }
111	    } catch (e: any) {
112	      toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? "Connection failed");
113	    }
114	  };
115	
116	  const toggleTool = (toolName: string) =>
117	    setAllowlist((cur) =>
118	      cur.includes(toolName) ? cur.filter((t) => t !== toolName) : [...cur, toolName],
119	    );
120	
121	  const save = async () => {
122	    if (!name.trim() || !url.trim()) {
123	      toast.error("Name and URL are required");
124	      return;
125	    }
126	    if (!editing && authType === "bearer" && !authToken.trim()) {
127	      toast.error("A bearer token is required");
128	      return;
129	    }
130	    if (!editing && authType === "headers" && !headersText.trim()) {
131	      toast.error("At least one header is required");
132	      return;
133	    }
134	    const base = {
135	      name: name.trim(),
136	      description: description.trim() || undefined,
137	      url: url.trim(),
138	      transport,
139	      authType,
140	      toolAllowlist: allowlist,
141	      enabled,
142	      ...buildAuthFields(),
143	    };
144	    try {
145	      if (editing) await update({ id: server!.id, data: base }).unwrap();
146	      else await create(base).unwrap();
147	      toast.success(editing ? "Server updated" : "Server added");
148	      onOpenChange(false);
149	    } catch (e: any) {
150	      toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? "Failed to save server");
151	    }
152	  };
153	
154	  return (
155	    <Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={onOpenChange}>
156	      <DialogContent className="max-h-[88vh] overflow-y-auto sm:max-w-lg">
157	        <DialogHeader>
158	          <DialogTitle>{editing ? "Edit MCP server" : "Add MCP server"}</DialogTitle>
159	        </DialogHeader>
160	        <div className="space-y-4">
161	          <div>
162	            <Label htmlFor="mcp-name">Name</Label>
163	            <Input
164	              id="mcp-name"
165	              value={name}
166	              onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
167	              placeholder="e.g. MyMan MCP"
168	              className="mt-1"
169	            />
170	          </div>
171	          <div>
172	            <Label htmlFor="mcp-desc">Description (optional)</Label>
173	            <Input
174	              id="mcp-desc"
175	              value={description}
176	              onChange={(e) => setDescription(e.target.value)}
177	              className="mt-1"
178	            />
179	          </div>
180	          <div>
181	            <Label htmlFor="mcp-url">URL</Label>
182	            <Input
183	              id="mcp-url"
184	              value={url}
185	              onChange={(e) => setUrl(e.target.value)}
186	              placeholder="https://example.com/mcp"
187	              className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs"
188	            />
189	          </div>
190	          <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-3">
191	            <div>
192	              <Label>Transport</Label>
193	              <Select value={transport} onValueChange={(v) => setTransport(v as McpTransport)}>
194	                <SelectTrigger className="mt-1">
195	                  <SelectValue />
196	                </SelectTrigger>
197	                <SelectContent>
198	                  <SelectItem value="http">Streamable HTTP</SelectItem>
199	                  <SelectItem value="sse">SSE</SelectItem>
200	                </SelectContent>
201	              </Select>
202	            </div>
203	            <div>
204	              <Label>Auth</Label>
205	              <Select value={authType} onValueChange={(v) => setAuthType(v as McpAuthType)}>
206	                <SelectTrigger className="mt-1">
207	                  <SelectValue />
208	                </SelectTrigger>
209	                <SelectContent>
210	                  <SelectItem value="none">None</SelectItem>
211	                  <SelectItem value="bearer">Bearer token</SelectItem>
212	                  <SelectItem value="headers">Custom headers</SelectItem>
213	                </SelectContent>
214	              </Select>
215	            </div>
216	          </div>
217	
218	          {authType === "bearer" && (
219	            <div>
220	              <Label htmlFor="mcp-token">Bearer token</Label>
221	              <Input
222	                id="mcp-token"
223	                type="password"
224	                value={authToken}
225	                onChange={(e) => setAuthToken(e.target.value)}
226	                placeholder={
227	                  useStoredSecret ? "•••••• (stored — leave blank to keep)" : "token"
228	                }
229	                className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs"
230	              />
231	            </div>
232	          )}
233	          {authType === "headers" && (
234	            <div>
235	              <Label htmlFor="mcp-headers">Headers (one per line: Name: value)</Label>
236	              <Textarea
237	                id="mcp-headers"
238	                value={headersText}
239	                onChange={(e) => setHeadersText(e.target.value)}
240	                rows={3}
241	                placeholder={
242	                  useStoredSecret
243	                    ? "stored — leave blank to keep existing headers"
244	                    : "X-Api-Key: abc123"
245	                }
246	                className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs"
247	              />
248	            </div>
249	          )}
250	
251	          <div className="rounded-lg border p-3">
252	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
253	              <span className="text-sm font-medium">Tools</span>
254	              <Button variant="outline" size="sm" disabled={testing} onClick={runTest}>
255	                {testing ? "Testing…" : "Test connection"}
256	              </Button>
257	            </div>
258	            {tools.length > 0 ? (
259	              <div className="mt-3 space-y-2">
260	                <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
261	                  Leave all unchecked to allow every tool, or pick which tools the agent may call.
262	                </p>
263	                <div className="max-h-48 space-y-1 overflow-y-auto">
264	                  {tools.map((t) => (
265	                    <label
266	                      key={t.name}
267	                      className="flex cursor-pointer items-start gap-2 rounded p-1 hover:bg-muted"
268	                    >
269	                      <input
270	                        type="checkbox"
271	                        className="mt-1"
272	                        checked={allowlist.includes(t.name)}
273	                        onChange={() => toggleTool(t.name)}
274	                      />
275	                      <span className="min-w-0">
276	                        <span className="font-mono text-xs">{t.name}</span>
277	                        {t.description && (
278	                          <span className="block truncate text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
279	                            {t.description}
280	                          </span>
281	                        )}
282	                      </span>
283	                    </label>
284	                  ))}
285	                </div>
286	              </div>
287	            ) : (
288	              <p className="mt-2 text-xs text-muted-foreground">
289	                {allowlist.length
290	                  ? `Allowlist: ${allowlist.join(", ")} — test the connection to edit.`
291	                  : "Test the connection to list and (optionally) restrict tools."}
292	              </p>
293	            )}
294	          </div>
295	
296	          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
297	            <Label htmlFor="mcp-enabled">Enabled</Label>
298	            <Switch id="mcp-enabled" checked={enabled} onCheckedChange={setEnabled} />
299	          </div>
300	        </div>
301	        <DialogFooter>
302	          <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => onOpenChange(false)}>
303	            Cancel
304	          </Button>
305	          <Button onClick={save} disabled={saving}>
306	            {saving ? "Saving…" : "Save"}
307	          </Button>
308	        </DialogFooter>
309	      </DialogContent>
310	    </Dialog>
311	  );
312	}
313	
314	/** Small status pill reused by the list. */
315	export function McpStatusIcon({ error }: { error?: string | null }) {
316	  return error ? (
317	    <XCircle className="h-4 w-4 text-destructive" />
318	  ) : (
319	    <CheckCircle2 className="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" />
320	  );
321	}
322

import {
  PrimaryGeneratedColumn,
  CreateDateColumn,
  UpdateDateColumn,
} from 'typeorm';

export abstract class BaseEntity {
  @PrimaryGeneratedColumn('uuid')
  id!: string;

  @CreateDateColumn({ type: 'timestamptz' })
  createdAt!: Date;

  @UpdateDateColumn({ type: 'timestamptz' })
  updatedAt!: Date;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { BillingProfile } from './shapes';

@Entity('clients')
export class Client extends BaseEntity {
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  xeroContactId!: string | null; // null until created in Xero

  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  name!: string;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', default: () => "'[]'" })
  emails!: string[];

  // Autonomy policy: default OFF. ON => Robyn may auto-send within terms.
  @Column({ type: 'boolean', default: false })
  autonomyEnabled!: boolean;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', nullable: true })
  billingProfile!: BillingProfile | null;

  // Convenience denormalised total of unbilled exposure (recomputed by engine).
  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 12, scale: 2, default: 0 })
  unbilledExposureGbp!: number;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { InvoiceProposalState } from './enums';
import { ProposalLine, PolicyResult } from './shapes';

@Entity('invoice_proposals')
export class InvoiceProposal extends BaseEntity {
  // Source is exactly one of meetingId or detectionId.
  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  meetingId!: string | null;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  detectionId!: string | null;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid' })
  clientId!: string;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', default: () => "'[]'" })
  lines!: ProposalLine[];

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', default: 'GBP' })
  currency!: string;

  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 12, scale: 2, default: 0 })
  subtotal!: number;

  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 12, scale: 2, default: 0 })
  taxTotal!: number;

  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 12, scale: 2, default: 0 })
  total!: number;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb' })
  policyResult!: PolicyResult;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: InvoiceProposalState, default: InvoiceProposalState.DRAFT })
  state!: InvoiceProposalState;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  xeroInvoiceId!: string | null;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  xeroInvoiceNumber!: string | null;

  // Deep-link into the Xero UI once written.
  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  xeroDeepLink!: string | null;

  // The stable Reference used for idempotent check-by-reference in Xero.
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  reference!: string | null;

  // Whether this proposal was auto-sent (autonomy ON) vs human-approved.
  @Column({ type: 'boolean', default: false })
  autoSent!: boolean;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { DetectionType, DetectionState } from './enums';
import { DetectionEvidence } from './shapes';

@Entity('detections')
export class Detection extends BaseEntity {
  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: DetectionType })
  type!: DetectionType;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  clientId!: string | null;

  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 12, scale: 2, default: 0 })
  valueGbp!: number;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb' })
  evidence!: DetectionEvidence;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: DetectionState, default: DetectionState.OPEN })
  state!: DetectionState;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  xeroInvoiceId!: string | null;

  // Stable dedupe key so re-running detectors is idempotent.
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  dedupeKey!: string;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { MeetingState } from './enums';
import { MatchProposal } from './shapes';

export interface MeetingAttendee {
  email: string;
  name?: string;
  organizer?: boolean;
}

@Entity('meetings')
export class Meeting extends BaseEntity {
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  gcalEventId!: string; // stable id from calendar (or synthesised for .ics)

  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  title!: string;

  @Column({ type: 'timestamptz' })
  start!: Date;

  @Column({ type: 'timestamptz' })
  end!: Date;

  // Duration in hours (denormalised for the engine's maths).
  @Column({ type: 'numeric', precision: 6, scale: 2 })
  durationHours!: number;

  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', default: () => "'[]'" })
  attendees!: MeetingAttendee[];

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  clientId!: string | null;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: MeetingState, default: MeetingState.DETECTED })
  state!: MeetingState;

  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  transcriptId!: string | null;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  skipReason!: string | null;

  // Fuzzy match proposals from the LLM (surfaced in CONFIRM_CLIENT_MATCH).
  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', nullable: true })
  matchProposals!: MatchProposal[] | null;

  // 'personal' meetings (no external attendees) are auto-skipped.
  @Column({ type: 'boolean', default: false })
  isPersonal!: boolean;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', default: 'ics' })
  source!: string; // 'google' | 'ics'
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { TaskType, TaskState, TaskRefType } from './enums';

@Entity('tasks')
export class Task extends BaseEntity {
  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: TaskType })
  type!: TaskType;

  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: TaskRefType })
  refType!: TaskRefType;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid' })
  refId!: string;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: TaskState, default: TaskState.OPEN })
  state!: TaskState;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  title!: string;

  @Column({ type: 'text', nullable: true })
  summary!: string | null;

  // Free-form context the card needs to render (client name, quote, amount...).
  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', nullable: true })
  context!: Record<string, unknown> | null;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  resolution!: string | null;

  @Column({ type: 'timestamptz', nullable: true })
  resolvedAt!: Date | null;

  // Idempotency: at most one OPEN task per (type, refId).
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  dedupeKey!: string;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { ConnectionKind, ConnectionStatus } from './enums';

// Integration health, surfaced truthfully on the Connections panel. One row
// per integration kind. Never faked — LIVE vs FALLBACK is the honest state.
@Entity('connection_states')
export class ConnectionState extends BaseEntity {
  @Index({ unique: true })
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: ConnectionKind })
  kind!: ConnectionKind;

  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: ConnectionStatus, default: ConnectionStatus.DOWN })
  status!: ConnectionStatus;

  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  label!: string | null; // org name / mailbox / calendar id

  @Column({ type: 'text', nullable: true })
  detail!: string | null; // human explanation of the current status

  @Column({ type: 'timestamptz', nullable: true })
  lastSyncAt!: Date | null;

  @Column({ type: 'timestamptz', nullable: true })
  nextPollAt!: Date | null;
}
import { Entity, Column, Index } from 'typeorm';
import { BaseEntity } from './base.entity';
import { AuditActor } from './enums';

// Every state change, Xero write, policy decision and poll result creates one
// of these. No silent mutations — this table is the audit trail screen.
@Entity('audit_events')
export class AuditEvent extends BaseEntity {
  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'enum', enum: AuditActor })
  actor!: AuditActor;

  // Short machine verb, e.g. "meeting.transcript_attached", "xero.invoice.created".
  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'varchar' })
  action!: string;

  // Human sentence for the audit screen.
  @Column({ type: 'text' })
  summary!: string;

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'varchar', nullable: true })
  subjectType!: string | null; // 'meeting' | 'proposal' | 'client' | ...

  @Index()
  @Column({ type: 'uuid', nullable: true })
  subjectId!: string | null;

  // The inputs to the decision — kept so an auditor can reconstruct it.
  @Column({ type: 'jsonb', nullable: true })
  inputs!: Record<string, unknown> | null;
}

import { Contract } from './contract.entity';
import { PotentialClient } from './potential-client.entity';

import { Injectable, Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
import * as api from './xero-api';
import { xeroHealthCheck, resolveTenant } from './xero-http';

// Thin NestJS surface over the raw Accounting API helpers. Business decisions
// live in the engine and services — this is I/O + a health probe only.
@Injectable()
export class XeroService {
  private readonly log = new Logger('XeroService');

  // Contacts
  ensureContact = api.ensureContact;
  findContactByEmail = api.findContactByEmail;
  findContactByName = api.findContactByName;
  listContacts = api.listContacts;

  // Invoices (ACCREC)
  createInvoice = api.createInvoice;
  authoriseInvoice = api.authoriseInvoice;
  getInvoice = api.getInvoice;
  findInvoiceByReference = api.findInvoiceByReference;
  listInvoices = api.listInvoices;

  // Evidence (raw API only)
  addHistoryNote = api.addHistoryNote;
  getHistory = api.getHistory;
  uploadAttachment = api.uploadAttachment;
  listAttachments = api.listAttachments;

  // The composed money-moment write (G2): contact + invoice + history + attachment.
  writeInvoice = api.writeInvoice;

  // Payments / quotes / accounts / reports
  listPayments = api.listPayments;
  createPayment = api.createPayment;
  listQuotes = api.listQuotes;
  listAccounts = api.listAccounts;
  defaultSalesAccountCode = api.defaultSalesAccountCode;
  agedReceivablesByContact = api.agedReceivablesByContact;
  invoiceDeepLink = api.invoiceDeepLink;

  async health(): Promise<{ ok: boolean; orgName: string | null; scope: string | null; reason: string | null }> {
    const h = await xeroHealthCheck();
    if (!h.ok) this.log.warn(`Xero not live: ${h.reason}`);
    return h;
  }

  async orgName(): Promise<string | null> {
    const t = await resolveTenant();
    return t?.name ?? null;
  }
}
import { z } from 'zod';

// Zod on every LLM output. Nothing unvalidated crosses this boundary. The LLM
// PARSES and PROPOSES only — the engine decides billed/unbilled downstream.

export const TranscriptScopeItemSchema = z.object({
  description: z.string().min(1),
  est_hours: z.number().min(0).max(1000),
  evidence_quote: z.string().min(1),
  billable: z.boolean(),
});

export const TranscriptParsedSchema = z.object({
  summary: z.string().nullable(),
  action_points: z.array(z.string()),
  scope_items: z.array(TranscriptScopeItemSchema),
});
export type TranscriptParsedLLM = z.infer<typeof TranscriptParsedSchema>;

export const ClauseSchema = z.object({ ref: z.string(), text: z.string() });

export const ContractParsedSchema = z.object({
  rate: z.number().nullable(),
  rate_unit: z.enum(['HOUR', 'DAY', 'FIXED']).nullable(),
  currency: z.string().default('GBP'),
  payment_terms: z.string().nullable(),
  scope_summary: z.string().nullable(),
  clauses: z.array(ClauseSchema),
});
export type ContractParsedLLM = z.infer<typeof ContractParsedSchema>;

export const AgreementClassificationSchema = z.object({
  agreement: z.boolean(),
  evidence_quote: z.string(),
  reasoning: z.string(),
});
export type AgreementClassificationLLM = z.infer<typeof AgreementClassificationSchema>;

export const MatchProposalSchema = z.object({
  client_id: z.string(),
  client_name: z.string(),
  confidence: z.number().min(0).max(1),
  reason: z.string(),
});
export const MatchProposalsSchema = z.object({ proposals: z.array(MatchProposalSchema) });
export type MatchProposalsLLM = z.infer<typeof MatchProposalsSchema>;
import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ConfigModule } from '@nestjs/config';
import { TypeOrmModule } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
import { ScheduleModule } from '@nestjs/schedule';
import { join } from 'node:path';

import { ALL_ENTITIES } from './entities';
import { AuditModule } from './modules/audit/audit.module';
import { XeroModule } from './modules/xero/xero.module';
import { LlmModule } from './modules/llm/llm.module';
import { EngineModule } from './modules/engine/engine.module';
import { ClientsModule } from './modules/clients/clients.module';
import { MeetingsModule } from './modules/meetings/meetings.module';
import { EmailModule } from './modules/email/email.module';
import { ProposalsModule } from './modules/proposals/proposals.module';
import { DetectionsModule } from './modules/detections/detections.module';
import { TasksModule } from './modules/tasks/tasks.module';
import { ConnectionsModule } from './modules/connections/connections.module';
import { DashboardModule } from './modules/dashboard/dashboard.module';
import { HealthModule } from './modules/health/health.module';

@Module({
  imports: [
    ConfigModule.forRoot({
      isGlobal: true,
      envFilePath: join(__dirname, '..', '.env'),
    }),
    TypeOrmModule.forRoot({
      type: 'postgres',
      url: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
      entities: ALL_ENTITIES,
      synchronize: true, // hackathon: schema auto-managed. No migrations.
      retryAttempts: 3,
      retryDelay: 1500,
    }),
    ScheduleModule.forRoot(),
    // Shared kernel
    AuditModule,
    XeroModule,
    LlmModule,
    EngineModule,
    // Feature surfaces
    ClientsModule,
    MeetingsModule,
    EmailModule,
    ProposalsModule,
    DetectionsModule,
    TasksModule,
    ConnectionsModule,
    DashboardModule,
    HealthModule,
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
// Domain enums. These are the *only* legal states — every transition either
// writes to Xero or raises a Task. No silent third path.

export enum MeetingState {
  DETECTED = 'DETECTED',
  AWAITING_TRANSCRIPT = 'AWAITING_TRANSCRIPT',
  TRANSCRIPT_ATTACHED = 'TRANSCRIPT_ATTACHED',
  CLIENT_MATCHED = 'CLIENT_MATCHED',
  UNKNOWN_ATTENDEE = 'UNKNOWN_ATTENDEE',
  INVOICE_PROPOSED = 'INVOICE_PROPOSED',
  SENT = 'SENT',
  SKIPPED = 'SKIPPED',
}

export enum PotentialClientState {
  QUEUED = 'QUEUED',
  WATCHING = 'WATCHING',
  AGREEMENT_DETECTED = 'AGREEMENT_DETECTED',
  PROMOTED = 'PROMOTED',
  DISMISSED = 'DISMISSED',
}

export enum InvoiceProposalState {
  DRAFT = 'DRAFT', // built by the engine, not yet in Xero
  IN_REVIEW = 'IN_REVIEW', // waiting on a human (Task REVIEW_INVOICE)
  APPROVED = 'APPROVED', // human approved, about to write
  SENT = 'SENT', // written to Xero (draft or authorised)
  REJECTED = 'REJECTED',
}

export enum DetectionType {
  QUOTE_NOT_INVOICED = 'QUOTE_NOT_INVOICED',
  RETAINER_STOPPED = 'RETAINER_STOPPED',
  EXPENSE_NOT_RECHARGED = 'EXPENSE_NOT_RECHARGED',
}

export enum DetectionState {
  OPEN = 'OPEN',
  PROPOSED = 'PROPOSED',
  RESOLVED = 'RESOLVED',
  DISMISSED = 'DISMISSED',
}

export enum TaskType {
  PROVIDE_TRANSCRIPT = 'PROVIDE_TRANSCRIPT',
  CONFIRM_CLIENT_MATCH = 'CONFIRM_CLIENT_MATCH',
  REVIEW_INVOICE = 'REVIEW_INVOICE',
  CONFIRM_AGREEMENT = 'CONFIRM_AGREEMENT',
  ATTACH_CONTRACT = 'ATTACH_CONTRACT',
}

export enum TaskState {
  OPEN = 'OPEN',
  RESOLVED = 'RESOLVED',
}

export enum TaskRefType {
  MEETING = 'MEETING',
  PROPOSAL = 'PROPOSAL',
  POTENTIAL_CLIENT = 'POTENTIAL_CLIENT',
  CLIENT = 'CLIENT',
  DETECTION = 'DETECTION',
}

// Where a value on an invoice line came from — this is the provenance chip.
export enum ProvenanceKind {
  CONTRACT_CLAUSE = 'CONTRACT_CLAUSE',
  TRANSCRIPT_QUOTE = 'TRANSCRIPT_QUOTE',
  CALENDAR_BLOCK = 'CALENDAR_BLOCK',
  LEDGER = 'LEDGER', // detector-sourced (quote / payment / expense)
}

export enum BillingSource {
  CONTRACT = 'CONTRACT',
  INFERRED = 'INFERRED',
}

export enum TranscriptSource {
  PASTED = 'PASTED',
  GRANOLA = 'GRANOLA',
  UPLOAD = 'UPLOAD',
}

export enum ConnectionKind {
  XERO = 'XERO',
  CALENDAR = 'CALENDAR',
  EMAIL = 'EMAIL',
}

export enum ConnectionStatus {
  LIVE = 'LIVE',
  FALLBACK = 'FALLBACK',
  DOWN = 'DOWN',
}

// The actor on an AuditEvent — Robyn (the deterministic engine / cron),
// the human operator, or the LLM edge (parse/propose only, never decides).
export enum AuditActor {
  ROBYN = 'ROBYN',
  HUMAN = 'HUMAN',
  LLM = 'LLM',
  XERO = 'XERO',
  SYSTEM = 'SYSTEM',
}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core/CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md`

```text
1	# Calendar Sync Debugging Guide
2	
3	## Issue: Events only showing from one calendar
4	
5	The sync system is designed correctly - each calendar provider has its own `providerAccountId` (calendar ID) and syncs independently. If you're only seeing events from one calendar, here's how to debug:
6	
7	## Step 1: Check All Connected Calendars
8	
9	```bash
10	GET /api/v1/calendar-providers
11	```
12	
13	**What to look for:**
14	- Do you see multiple providers listed?
15	- Check the `providerAccountId` - each should be different (e.g., "primary", "work-calendar-id")
16	- Check `lastSyncedAt` - has each calendar been synced?
17	- Check `syncStatus` - are any in "error" state?
18	- Check `syncError` - any error messages?
19	
20	## Step 2: Check Events Per Calendar
21	
22	```bash
23	GET /api/v1/calendar-events?calendarProviderId={provider-id}
24	```
25	
26	Run this for EACH provider ID to see which calendars have events.
27	
28	## Step 3: Trigger Manual Sync
29	
30	```bash
31	POST /api/v1/calendar-providers/{provider-id}/sync
32	```
33	
34	Try syncing each calendar manually and check the response.
35	
36	## Common Issues
37	
38	### Issue 1: Only Primary Calendar Connected
39	**Symptom:** Only one provider in the list  
40	**Cause:** `connect-multiple` wasn't called with all calendar IDs  
41	**Fix:** Reconnect and select all calendars
42	
43	### Issue 2: Sync Failed for Some Calendars
44	**Symptom:** Multiple providers but some have `syncStatus: "error"`  
45	**Cause:** Token issues, permission issues, or invalid calendar IDs  
46	**Fix:** Check `syncError` field, may need to reconnect
47	
48	### Issue 3: Events Not Linked to Correct Provider
49	**Symptom:** All events have the same `calendarProviderId`  
50	**Cause:** Bug in sync logic (shouldn't happen with current code)  
51	**Fix:** Check database directly
52	
53	## Database Queries (if needed)
54	
55	```sql
56	-- Check all providers
57	SELECT id, provider_type, provider_account_id, calendar_name, is_active, sync_status, last_synced_at
58	FROM calendar_providers
59	WHERE is_deleted = false;
60	
61	-- Check events per provider
62	SELECT cp.calendar_name, COUNT(ce.id) as event_count
63	FROM calendar_providers cp
64	LEFT JOIN calendar_events ce ON ce.calendar_provider_id = cp.id AND ce.is_deleted = false
65	WHERE cp.is_deleted = false
66	GROUP BY cp.id, cp.calendar_name;
67	
68	-- Check specific provider's events
69	SELECT id, title, start_time, calendar_provider_id
70	FROM calendar_events
71	WHERE calendar_provider_id = 'your-provider-id' AND is_deleted = false
72	ORDER BY start_time;
73	```
74	
75	## Expected Behavior
76	
77	With 3 connected calendars (e.g., Personal, Work, Family):
78	
79	1. **3 provider entries** in `calendar_providers` table
80	2. **Each with unique `provider_account_id`** (e.g., "primary", "work-id", "family-id")
81	3. **Events distributed** across providers based on which calendar they came from
82	4. **GET /calendar-events** returns ALL events from ALL calendars
83	5. **GET /calendar-events?calendarProviderId=X** returns events from specific calendar
84	
85	## Verification Steps
86	
87	### 1. List all providers
88	```typescript
89	const providers = await fetch('/api/v1/calendar-providers').then(r => r.json());
90	console.log('Connected calendars:', providers.length);
91	providers.forEach(p => {
92	  console.log(`- ${p.calendarName} (${p.providerAccountId}): ${p.syncStatus}`);
93	});
94	```
95	
96	### 2. Check events per calendar
97	```typescript
98	for (const provider of providers) {
99	  const events = await fetch(
100	    `/api/v1/calendar-events?calendarProviderId=${provider.id}`
101	  ).then(r => r.json());
102	  console.log(`${provider.calendarName}: ${events.length} events`);
103	}
104	```
105	
106	### 3. Trigger sync for all
107	```typescript
108	for (const provider of providers) {
109	  await fetch(`/api/v1/calendar-providers/${provider.id}/sync`, {
110	    method: 'POST'
111	  });
112	  console.log(`Synced ${provider.calendarName}`);
113	}
114	```
115	
116	## How Sync Works (Technical)
117	
118	### Google Calendar
119	```typescript
120	// Line 125 in calendar-sync.service.ts
121	const syncParams = {
122	  calendarId: provider.providerAccountId,  // ← Uses specific calendar ID
123	  maxResults: 250,
124	  singleEvents: true,
125	  orderBy: 'startTime',
126	  timeMin: new Date().toISOString(),
127	};
128	
129	const response = await calendar.events.list(syncParams);
130	```
131	
132	### Microsoft Calendar
133	```typescript
134	// Line 198 in calendar-sync.service.ts
135	const response = await client
136	  .api(`/me/calendars/${provider.providerAccountId}/events`)  // ← Uses specific calendar ID
137	  .select('id,subject,bodyPreview,start,end,location,attendees,isAllDay,isCancelled,recurrence,lastModifiedDateTime')
138	  .filter(`start/dateTime ge '${new Date().toISOString()}'`)
139	  .top(250)
140	  .orderby('start/dateTime')
141	  .get();
142	```
143	
144	Each provider syncs its own calendar independently using `provider.providerAccountId`.
145	
146	## Solution
147	
148	If events are only showing from one calendar, the most likely causes are:
149	
150	1. **Only one calendar was connected**
151	   - Solution: Use `connect-multiple` with all calendar IDs
152	
153	2. **Sync failed for other calendars**
154	   - Solution: Check `syncError` and reconnect if needed
155	
156	3. **Frontend is filtering by one provider**
157	   - Solution: Remove `calendarProviderId` filter or loop through all providers
158	
159	The backend is working correctly - each calendar syncs independently! 🎉
160
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./api/src/modules/dashboard/dashboard.service.ts`

```text
1	import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
2	import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
3	import {
4	  Between,
5	  FindOptionsWhere,
6	  In,
7	  LessThanOrEqual,
8	  MoreThanOrEqual,
9	  Repository,
10	} from 'typeorm';
11	import {
12	  Client,
13	  Detection,
14	  DetectionState,
15	  InvoiceProposal,
16	  InvoiceProposalState,
17	  Meeting,
18	  MeetingState,
19	  Task,
20	  TaskState,
21	} from '../../entities';
22	import { AuditService } from '../audit/audit.service';
23	import {
24	  AuditEventDto,
25	  CalendarColorKey,
26	  CalendarEventDto,
27	  DashboardSummaryDto,
28	  LeakStripDto,
29	} from './dto/dashboard.dto';
30	
31	const MONTH_NAMES = [
32	  'January',
33	  'February',
34	  'March',
35	  'April',
36	  'May',
37	  'June',
38	  'July',
39	  'August',
40	  'September',
41	  'October',
42	  'November',
43	  'December',
44	];
45	
46	// Meeting states whose work is still recoverable (not yet SENT / SKIPPED) and
47	// that do NOT already carry a proposal (INVOICE_PROPOSED is counted in the
48	// in-review bucket instead, so it is excluded here to avoid double counting).
49	const UNBILLED_MEETING_STATES: MeetingState[] = [
50	  MeetingState.DETECTED,
51	  MeetingState.AWAITING_TRANSCRIPT,
52	  MeetingState.TRANSCRIPT_ATTACHED,
53	  MeetingState.CLIENT_MATCHED,
54	];
55	
56	@Injectable()
57	export class DashboardService {
58	  constructor(
59	    @InjectRepository(Meeting)
60	    private readonly meetings: Repository<Meeting>,
61	    @InjectRepository(Client)
62	    private readonly clients: Repository<Client>,
63	    @InjectRepository(InvoiceProposal)
64	    private readonly proposals: Repository<InvoiceProposal>,
65	    @InjectRepository(Detection)
66	    private readonly detections: Repository<Detection>,
67	    @InjectRepository(Task)
68	    private readonly tasks: Repository<Task>,
69	    private readonly audit: AuditService,
70	  ) {}
71	
72	  private static round2(n: number): number {
73	    return Math.round((n + Number.EPSILON) * 100) / 100;
74	  }
75	
76	  // Colour token for the calendar view, derived purely from meeting state.
77	  private colorKeyFor(m: Meeting): CalendarColorKey {
78	    if (m.isPersonal) return 'gray';
79	    switch (m.state) {
80	      case MeetingState.SENT:
81	        return 'teal'; // billed
82	      case MeetingState.INVOICE_PROPOSED:
83	        return 'amber'; // proposal in review
84	      case MeetingState.UNKNOWN_ATTENDEE:
85	        return 'purple'; // unknown attendee
86	      case MeetingState.SKIPPED:
87	        return 'gray'; // skipped
88	      default:
89	        return 'amber-outline'; // awaiting (detected / awaiting transcript / attached / matched)
90	    }
91	  }
92	
93	  // Best-effort £ estimate of a single unbilled meeting from its client's rate.
94	  private estimateMeetingValue(m: Meeting, client?: Client): number {
95	    const bp = client?.billingProfile;
96	    if (!bp || bp.rate == null) return 0;
97	    const rate = Number(bp.rate);
98	    const hours = Number(m.durationHours);
99	    if (!Number.isFinite(rate) || !Number.isFinite(hours)) return 0;
100	    if (bp.rate_unit === 'HOUR') return rate * hours;
101	    if (bp.rate_unit === 'DAY') return rate * (hours / 8); // ~8h working day
102	    return 0; // FIXED / unknown — not estimable per meeting
103	  }
104	
105	  private async clientsById(ids: string[]): Promise<Map<string, Client>> {
106	    const unique = [...new Set(ids.filter(Boolean))];
107	    if (unique.length === 0) return new Map();
108	    const rows = await this.clients.find({ where: { id: In(unique) } });
109	    return new Map(rows.map((c) => [c.id, c]));
110	  }
111	
112	  // GET /dashboard/calendar — colour-coded events with client + proposal links.
113	  async calendar(from?: string, to?: string): Promise<CalendarEventDto[]> {
114	    const where: FindOptionsWhere<Meeting> = {};
115	    if (from && to) {
116	      where.start = Between(new Date(from), new Date(to));
117	    } else if (from) {
118	      where.start = MoreThanOrEqual(new Date(from));
119	    } else if (to) {
120	      where.start = LessThanOrEqual(new Date(to));
121	    }
122	
123	    const meetings = await this.meetings.find({
124	      where,
125	      order: { start: 'ASC' },
126	    });
127	    if (meetings.length === 0) return [];
128	
129	    const clientMap = await this.clientsById(
130	      meetings.map((m) => m.clientId ?? '').filter(Boolean),
131	    );
132	
133	    // Map each meeting to its most recent proposal (prefer non-rejected).
134	    const meetingIds = meetings.map((m) => m.id);
135	    const proposalRows = await this.proposals.find({
136	      where: { meetingId: In(meetingIds) },
137	      order: { createdAt: 'DESC' },
138	    });
139	    const proposalByMeeting = new Map<string, string>();
140	    for (const p of proposalRows) {
141	      if (!p.meetingId) continue;
142	      const existing = proposalByMeeting.get(p.meetingId);
143	      if (!existing) {
144	        proposalByMeeting.set(p.meetingId, p.id);
145	      } else if (p.state !== InvoiceProposalState.REJECTED) {
146	        // A newer non-rejected proposal wins over an older rejected one.
147	        proposalByMeeting.set(p.meetingId, p.id);
148	      }
149	    }
150	
151	    return meetings.map((m) => ({
152	      id: m.id,
153	      title: m.title,
154	      start: m.start.toISOString(),
155	      end: m.end.toISOString(),
156	      state: m.state,
157	      colorKey: this.colorKeyFor(m),
158	      clientName: m.clientId ? (clientMap.get(m.clientId)?.name ?? null) : null,
159	      proposalId: proposalByMeeting.get(m.id) ?? null,
160	    }));
161	  }
162	
163	  // GET /dashboard/leak-strip — "£2,880 was walking away" this month.
164	  async leakStrip(): Promise<LeakStripDto> {
165	    const [openDetections, inReviewProposals, unbilledMeetings] =
166	      await Promise.all([
167	        this.detections.find({ where: { state: DetectionState.OPEN } }),
168	        this.proposals.find({
169	          where: { state: InvoiceProposalState.IN_REVIEW },
170	        }),
171	        this.meetings.find({
172	          where: {
173	            state: In(UNBILLED_MEETING_STATES),
174	            isPersonal: false,
175	          },
176	        }),
177	      ]);
178	
179	    const detectionGbp = openDetections.reduce(
180	      (sum, d) => sum + Number(d.valueGbp),
181	      0,
182	    );
183	    const reviewGbp = inReviewProposals.reduce(
184	      (sum, p) => sum + Number(p.total),
185	      0,
186	    );
187	
188	    const meetingClientMap = await this.clientsById(
189	      unbilledMeetings.map((m) => m.clientId ?? '').filter(Boolean),
190	    );
191	    const meetingGbp = unbilledMeetings.reduce(
192	      (sum, m) =>
193	        sum +
194	        this.estimateMeetingValue(
195	          m,
196	          m.clientId ? meetingClientMap.get(m.clientId) : undefined,
197	        ),
198	      0,
199	    );
200	
201	    const breakdown = [
202	      {
203	        source: 'Ledger detections',
204	        gbp: DashboardService.round2(detectionGbp),
205	        count: openDetections.length,
206	      },
207	      {
208	        source: 'Invoices in review',
209	        gbp: DashboardService.round2(reviewGbp),
210	        count: inReviewProposals.length,
211	      },
212	      {
213	        source: 'Unbilled meetings',
214	        gbp: DashboardService.round2(meetingGbp),
215	        count: unbilledMeetings.length,
216	      },
217	    ];
218	
219	    // Label the strip with the month of the most recent unbilled meeting so
220	    // the demo reads "June 2026"; fall back to the current month.
221	    let labelDate = new Date();
222	    if (unbilledMeetings.length > 0) {
223	      labelDate = unbilledMeetings.reduce(
224	        (latest, m) => (m.start > latest ? m.start : latest),
225	        unbilledMeetings[0].start,
226	      );
227	    }
228	    const monthLabel = `${MONTH_NAMES[labelDate.getMonth()]} ${labelDate.getFullYear()}`;
229	
230	    return {
231	      monthLabel,
232	      recoverableGbp: DashboardService.round2(
233	        detectionGbp + reviewGbp + meetingGbp,
234	      ),
235	      breakdown,
236	    };
237	  }
238	
239	  // GET /dashboard/audit — the audit trail feed (newest first).
240	  async auditTrail(limit = 100): Promise<AuditEventDto[]> {
241	    const events = await this.audit.list(limit);
242	    return events.map((e) => ({
243	      id: e.id,
244	      actor: e.actor,
245	      action: e.action,
246	      summary: e.summary,
247	      subjectType: e.subjectType,
248	      subjectId: e.subjectId,
249	      inputs: e.inputs,
250	      createdAt: e.createdAt.toISOString(),
251	    }));
252	  }
253	
254	  // GET /dashboard/summary — the headline stats row.
255	  async summary(): Promise<DashboardSummaryDto> {
256	    const now = new Date();
257	    const monthStart = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth(), 1);
258	    const monthEnd = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth() + 1, 1);
259	
260	    const [openTasks, clientRows, invoicesSentThisMonth, clientsCount] =
261	      await Promise.all([
262	        this.tasks.count({ where: { state: TaskState.OPEN } }),
263	        this.clients.find(),
264	        this.proposals.count({
265	          where: {
266	            state: InvoiceProposalState.SENT,
267	            updatedAt: Between(monthStart, monthEnd),
268	          },
269	        }),
270	        this.clients.count(),
271	      ]);
272	
273	    const unbilledGbp = clientRows.reduce(
274	      (sum, c) => sum + Number(c.unbilledExposureGbp),
275	      0,
276	    );
277	
278	    return {
279	      openTasks,
280	      unbilledGbp: DashboardService.round2(unbilledGbp),
281	      invoicesSentThisMonth,
282	      clientsCount,
283	    };
284	  }
285	}
286
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core/CALENDAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md`

```text
1	# Calendar Management System - Implementation Summary
2	
3	## Overview
4	This document outlines the implementation of the Calendar Management System MVP, which enables syncing Google and Microsoft calendars into a unified backend system.
5	
6	## Completed Components
7	
8	### 1. Database Schema
9	**Migration**: `1760921663654-CreateCalendarTables.ts`
10	
11	#### Tables Created:
12	- **calendar_providers**: Stores OAuth credentials and provider metadata
13	  - Supports Google and Microsoft calendar providers
14	  - Stores encrypted access/refresh tokens
15	  - Tracks sync status and last sync time
16	  - Includes sync token for incremental syncing
17	
18	- **calendar_events**: Unified storage for all calendar events
19	  - Links to calendar_providers via foreign key
20	  - Stores event details (title, description, time, location, attendees)
21	  - Supports recurring events and all-day events
22	  - Maintains external event ID for sync deduplication
23	
24	### 2. API Endpoints
25	
26	#### Calendar Providers (`/api/v1/calendar-providers`)
27	- `GET /google/auth-url` - Get Google OAuth URL
28	- `GET /microsoft/auth-url` - Get Microsoft OAuth URL
29	- `POST /google/connect` - Connect Google Calendar with auth code
30	- `POST /microsoft/connect` - Connect Microsoft Calendar with auth code
31	- `GET /` - List all connected calendars
32	- `GET /:id` - Get specific calendar provider
33	- `DELETE /:id` - Disconnect calendar
34	- `POST /:id/sync` - Trigger manual sync
35	
36	#### Calendar Events (`/api/v1/calendar-events`)
37	- `GET /` - Get all events with filters (date range, provider, status)
38	- `GET /:id` - Get specific event
39	
40	### 3. Modules Structure
41	
42	```
43	src/modules/
44	├── calendar-providers/
45	│   ├── entities/
46	│   │   └── calendar-provider.entity.ts
47	│   ├── dto/
48	│   │   ├── connect-google-calendar.dto.ts
49	│   │   ├── connect-microsoft-calendar.dto.ts
50	│   │   ├── calendar-provider-response.dto.ts
51	│   │   └── oauth-url-response.dto.ts
52	│   ├── calendar-providers.controller.ts
53	│   ├── calendar-providers.service.ts
54	│   └── calendar-providers.module.ts
55	│
56	└── calendar-events/
57	    ├── entities/
58	    │   └── calendar-event.entity.ts
59	    ├── dto/
60	    │   ├── calendar-event-response.dto.ts
61	    │   └── query-calendar-events.dto.ts
62	    ├── calendar-events.controller.ts
63	    ├── calendar-events.service.ts
64	    └── calendar-events.module.ts
65	```
66	
67	### 4. Dependencies Installed
68	- `googleapis` - Google Calendar API client
69	- `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` - Microsoft Graph API client
70	- `@azure/msal-node` - Microsoft Authentication Library
71	- `@nestjs/schedule` - Cron job scheduling (for future sync scheduler)
72	
73	## Environment Variables Required
74	
75	Add these to your `.env` file:
76	
77	```env
78	# Google Calendar OAuth
79	GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-google-client-id.apps.googleusercontent.com
80	GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-google-client-secret
81	GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/api/v1/calendar-providers/google/callback
82	
83	# Microsoft Calendar OAuth
84	MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID=your-microsoft-client-id
85	MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET=your-microsoft-client-secret
86	MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID=common
87	MICROSOFT_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/api/v1/calendar-providers/microsoft/callback
88	```
89	
90	## Setup Instructions
91	
92	### 1. Google Calendar OAuth Setup
93	1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
94	2. Create a new project or select existing
95	3. Enable Google Calendar API
96	4. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Web application)
97	5. Add authorized redirect URI: `http://localhost:3000/api/v1/calendar-providers/google/callback`
98	6. Copy Client ID and Client Secret to `.env`
99	
100	### 2. Microsoft Calendar OAuth Setup
101	1. Go to [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
102	2. Navigate to Azure Active Directory > App registrations
103	3. Create new registration
104	4. Add redirect URI: `http://localhost:3000/api/v1/calendar-providers/microsoft/callback`
105	5. Create client secret under Certificates & secrets
106	6. Add API permissions: `Calendars.Read`
107	7. Copy Application (client) ID, Directory (tenant) ID, and client secret to `.env`
108	
109	### 3. Run Migration
110	```bash
111	npm run migration:run
112	```
113	
114	### 4. Start Application
115	```bash
116	npm run start:dev
117	```
118	
119	## Frontend Integration Flow
120	
121	### Connecting Google Calendar:
122	1. Frontend calls `GET /api/v1/calendar-providers/google/auth-url`
123	2. Backend returns OAuth URL
124	3. Frontend redirects user to OAuth URL
125	4. User approves permissions
126	5. Google redirects to callback with authorization code
127	6. Frontend extracts code from URL and calls `POST /api/v1/calendar-providers/google/connect` with code
128	7. Backend exchanges code for tokens and saves provider
129	8. Frontend shows success message
130	
131	### Connecting Microsoft Calendar:
132	Same flow as Google, but using Microsoft endpoints.
133	
134	### Viewing Events:
135	```javascript
136	// Get all events
137	GET /api/v1/calendar-events
138	
139	// Filter by date range
140	GET /api/v1/calendar-events?startDate=2024-01-01T00:00:00Z&endDate=2024-01-31T23:59:59Z
141	
142	// Filter by provider
143	GET /api/v1/calendar-events?calendarProviderId=123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
144	
145	// Filter by status
146	GET /api/v1/calendar-events?status=confirmed
147	```
148	
149	## What's NOT Implemented Yet (Future Phases)
150	
151	### Phase 2: Calendar Sync Service
152	- [ ] Sync scheduler service with cron jobs (every 15 minutes)
153	- [ ] Initial full sync implementation (fetch last 30 days + next 90 days)
154	- [ ] Incremental sync using sync tokens/delta queries
155	- [ ] Token refresh logic for expired tokens
156	- [ ] Error handling and retry mechanism
157	- [ ] Webhook support for real-time updates
158	
159	### Phase 3: Slot Booking System
160	- [ ] Available slot calculation based on existing events
161	- [ ] Booking link generation
162	- [ ] Custom booking links for special occasions
163	- [ ] Booking confirmation and calendar event creation
164	- [ ] Email notifications
165	
166	## Testing Status
167	
168	⚠️ **Tests are pending** - Following your implementation flow, tests need to be written and must pass before considering this feature complete.
169	
170	### Test Cases to Implement:
171	
172	#### Calendar Providers (Positive):
173	1. Successfully get Google OAuth URL
174	2. Successfully get Microsoft OAuth URL
175	3. Successfully connect Google calendar with valid code
176	4. Successfully connect Microsoft calendar with valid code
177	5. List all connected calendars
178	6. Get specific calendar provider by ID
179	7. Disconnect calendar provider
180	8. Trigger manual sync
181	
182	#### Calendar Providers (Negative):
183	1. Connect with invalid OAuth code
184	2. Get non-existent calendar provider
185	3. Disconnect non-existent calendar provider
186	4. Trigger sync on inactive calendar
187	
188	#### Calendar Events (Positive):
189	1. Get all events
190	2. Filter events by date range
191	3. Filter events by provider ID
192	4. Filter events by status
193	5. Get specific event by ID
194	
195	#### Calendar Events (Negative):
196	1. Get non-existent event
197	2. Invalid date range format
198	3. Invalid UUID format for provider ID
199	
200	## Security Considerations
201	
202	1. **Token Storage**: Access tokens are stored in database - consider encryption at rest
203	2. **Token Refresh**: Implement automatic token refresh before expiry
204	3. **Rate Limiting**: OAuth endpoints should have rate limiting
205	4. **Scope Minimization**: Currently using read-only scopes (good for MVP)
206	5. **HTTPS Required**: OAuth callbacks must use HTTPS in production
207	
208	## Known Limitations
209	
210	1. **Single User System**: No multi-user support (per project requirements)
211	2. **No Event Creation**: Read-only access for MVP
212	3. **No Webhook Support**: Polling-based sync only
213	4. **No Conflict Resolution**: Events are synced as-is from providers
214	5. **No Calendar Selection**: Syncs primary calendar only
215	
216	## Next Steps
217	
218	1. ✅ Complete test file creation
219	2. ✅ Implement sync scheduler service
220	3. ✅ Implement actual sync logic (fetch events from Google/Microsoft)
221	4. ✅ Add token refresh mechanism
222	5. ✅ Run all tests and verify
223	6. ✅ Test OAuth flow end-to-end
224	7. ✅ Document API in Swagger/OpenAPI
225	
226	## API Documentation
227	
228	Once the server is running, visit:
229	- Swagger UI: `http://localhost:3000/api/docs`
230	- OpenAPI JSON: `http://localhost:3000/api/docs-json`
231	
232	## Database Indexes
233	
234	The migration includes optimized indexes for:
235	- Provider type lookups
236	- Active provider filtering
237	- Event time range queries
238	- External event ID lookups
239	- Unique constraint on (provider_id, external_event_id) to prevent duplicates
240	
241	## Notes
242	
243	- Migration was generated properly using TypeORM after ensuring Docker containers were running
244	- Build successful with no TypeScript errors
245	- All modules registered in AppModule
246	- Environment variables documented in `.env.example`
247
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard/components/content-studio/WebSearchTab.tsx`

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { toast } from "sonner";
5	import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
6	import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input";
7	import { Label } from "@/components/ui/label";
8	import { Switch } from "@/components/ui/switch";
9	import { Textarea } from "@/components/ui/textarea";
10	import {
11	  useGetAgentConfigQuery,
12	  useUpdateAgentConfigMutation,
13	} from "@/managers/apiManager";
14	import { AgentConfig } from "@/interfaces/contentStudioInterface";
15	
16	const toLines = (domains?: string[]) => (domains ?? []).join("\n");
17	const parseDomains = (text: string) =>
18	  text
19	    .split(/[\n,]/)
20	    .map((d) => d.trim().replace(/^https?:\/\//, ""))
21	    .filter(Boolean);
22	
23	function Editor({ config }: { config: AgentConfig }) {
24	  const [enabled, setEnabled] = useState(config.webSearchEnabled);
25	  const [maxUses, setMaxUses] = useState(String(config.webSearchMaxUses ?? 5));
26	  const [allowed, setAllowed] = useState(toLines(config.webSearchAllowedDomains));
27	  const [blocked, setBlocked] = useState(toLines(config.webSearchBlockedDomains));
28	
29	  const [update, { isLoading: saving }] = useUpdateAgentConfigMutation();
30	
31	  const save = async () => {
32	    const n = parseInt(maxUses, 10);
33	    try {
34	      await update({
35	        webSearchEnabled: enabled,
36	        webSearchMaxUses: Number.isFinite(n) ? Math.min(20, Math.max(1, n)) : 5,
37	        webSearchAllowedDomains: parseDomains(allowed),
38	        webSearchBlockedDomains: parseDomains(blocked),
39	      }).unwrap();
40	      toast.success("Web search settings saved");
41	    } catch (e: any) {
42	      toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? "Failed to save");
43	    }
44	  };
45	
46	  return (
47	    <div className="space-y-6">
48	      <div className="flex items-start justify-between rounded-lg border p-4">
49	        <div>
50	          <Label htmlFor="ws-enabled" className="font-medium">
51	            Enable web search
52	          </Label>
53	          <p className="mt-1 max-w-md text-xs text-muted-foreground">
54	            Lets the agent search the web while drafting (Anthropic web search / Gemini Google
55	            Search grounding). No API key needed — it uses the model provider.
56	          </p>
57	        </div>
58	        <Switch id="ws-enabled" checked={enabled} onCheckedChange={setEnabled} />
59	      </div>
60	
61	      <div className={enabled ? "space-y-6" : "space-y-6 opacity-50 pointer-events-none"}>
62	        <div className="max-w-[200px]">
63	          <Label htmlFor="ws-max">Max searches per run</Label>
64	          <Input
65	            id="ws-max"
66	            type="number"
67	            min={1}
68	            max={20}
69	            value={maxUses}
70	            onChange={(e) => setMaxUses(e.target.value)}
71	            className="mt-1"
72	          />
73	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground">Anthropic only (1–20).</p>
74	        </div>
75	
76	        <div>
77	          <Label htmlFor="ws-allowed">Allowed domains</Label>
78	          <Textarea
79	            id="ws-allowed"
80	            value={allowed}
81	            onChange={(e) => setAllowed(e.target.value)}
82	            rows={3}
83	            className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs"
84	            placeholder="one domain per line, e.g. github.com"
85	          />
86	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground">
87	            Restrict search to these domains. <strong>Anthropic only</strong>; leave empty for no
88	            restriction.
89	          </p>
90	        </div>
91	
92	        <div>
93	          <Label htmlFor="ws-blocked">Blocked domains</Label>
94	          <Textarea
95	            id="ws-blocked"
96	            value={blocked}
97	            onChange={(e) => setBlocked(e.target.value)}
98	            rows={3}
99	            className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs"
100	            placeholder="one domain per line"
101	          />
102	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground">
103	            Never search these (Anthropic blocked domains / Gemini excluded domains).
104	          </p>
105	        </div>
106	      </div>
107	
108	      <div className="flex justify-end">
109	        <Button onClick={save} disabled={saving}>
110	          {saving ? "Saving…" : "Save web search"}
111	        </Button>
112	      </div>
113	    </div>
114	  );
115	}
116	
117	export function WebSearchTab() {
118	  const { data: config, isLoading } = useGetAgentConfigQuery();
119	  if (isLoading || !config) {
120	    return (
121	      <div className="flex h-40 items-center justify-center">
122	        <div className="h-6 w-6 animate-spin rounded-full border-4 border-primary border-t-transparent" />
123	      </div>
124	    );
125	  }
126	  return <Editor key={config.id} config={config} />;
127	}
128
```
