```text
"use client";

import * as React from "react";
import { CheckCircle2, RotateCw } from "lucide-react";

import { useApi, type Schemas } from "@/lib/api";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { PageHeader } from "@/components/page-header";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { LoadingState } from "@/components/loading-state";
import { ErrorState } from "@/components/error-state";
import { EmptyState } from "@/components/empty-state";
import { SummaryRow } from "./summary-row";
import { TaskList } from "./task-list";
import type { TaskDto } from "./context";

/**
 * The Tasks inbox — Robyn's home screen and primary resolution surface. Reads
 * GET /tasks and GET /dashboard/summary, and refetches both after every
 * resolution so a resolved card leaves and the headline numbers update at once.
 */
export function TasksSurface() {
  const tasksQ = useApi<TaskDto[]>("/tasks", { refreshMs: 20_000 });
  const summaryQ = useApi<Schemas["DashboardSummaryDto"]>(
    "/dashboard/summary",
    { refreshMs: 60_000 },
  );

  const refetchAll = React.useCallback(() => {
    tasksQ.refetch();
    summaryQ.refetch();
  }, [tasksQ.refetch, summaryQ.refetch]);

  const tasks = tasksQ.data ?? [];
  const refreshing = tasksQ.isValidating || summaryQ.isValidating;

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <PageHeader
        title="Tasks inbox"
        description="Everything Robyn couldn't decide on its own. Resolve each one right here."
        actions={
          <Button
            type="button"
            variant="outline"
            size="sm"
            onClick={refetchAll}
            disabled={refreshing}
            aria-label="Refresh tasks"
          >
            <RotateCw className={cn("h-3.5 w-3.5", refreshing && "animate-spin")} />
            {refreshing ? "Refreshing" : "Refresh"}
          </Button>
        }
      />

      <SummaryRow
        data={summaryQ.data}
        isLoading={summaryQ.isLoading}
        error={summaryQ.error}
---
"use client";

import * as React from "react";
import Link from "next/link";
import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { NAV_ITEMS, isActive } from "@/lib/nav";
import { useApi, type Schemas } from "@/lib/api";
import { Brand } from "@/components/brand";

/** Left navigation for the five surfaces. Home is the Tasks inbox. */
export function AppSidebar({ onNavigate }: { onNavigate?: () => void }) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  // Open-task count powers the inbox badge. Fails quietly — nav still works.
  const { data: counts } = useApi<Schemas["TaskCountsDto"]>("/tasks/counts", {
    refreshMs: 30_000,
  });
  const openTasks = counts?.total ?? 0;

  return (
    <div className="flex h-full flex-col">
      <div className="flex h-16 items-center px-5">
        <Link
          href="/"
          onClick={onNavigate}
          className="rounded-md focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring"
          aria-label="Robyn home"
        >
          <Brand />
        </Link>
      </div>

      <nav className="flex-1 space-y-1 px-3 py-2" aria-label="Primary">
        {NAV_ITEMS.map((item) => {
          const active = isActive(pathname, item);
          const Icon = item.icon;
          const showCount = item.href === "/" && openTasks > 0;
          return (
            <Link
              key={item.href}
              href={item.href}
              onClick={onNavigate}
              aria-current={active ? "page" : undefined}
              className={cn(
                "group flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-3 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors duration-fast focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring",
                active
                  ? "bg-secondary text-foreground"
                  : "text-muted-foreground hover:bg-secondary/60 hover:text-foreground",
              )}
```

function round2(n: number): number {
  return Math.round((n + Number.EPSILON) * 100) / 100;

function errMsg(err: unknown): string {
  return err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);

/*  Data hooks — native fetch with loading / error / refetch, no extra deps.    */
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

export interface UseApiState<T> {
  data: T | undefined;
  error: ApiError | undefined;
  isLoading: boolean;
  /** True on a background refetch while stale data is still shown. */
  isValidating: boolean;
  refetch: () => void;
}

/**
 * Fetch `path` on mount (and when it changes). Returns loading/error/data plus
 * a `refetch`. Pass `path = null` to skip (e.g. waiting on a param).
 */
export function useApi<T>(
  path: string | null,
  options?: { refreshMs?: number },
): UseApiState<T> {
  const [data, setData] = useState<T | undefined>(undefined);
  const [error, setError] = useState<ApiError | undefined>(undefined);
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState<boolean>(path != null);
  const [isValidating, setIsValidating] = useState<boolean>(false);
  const activeRef = useRef(0);

  const load = useCallback(
    async (isRefetch: boolean) => {
      if (path == null) return;
      const ticket = ++activeRef.current;
      if (isRefetch) setIsValidating(true);
      else setIsLoading(true);
      try {
        const result = await get<T>(path);
        if (ticket !== activeRef.current) return;
        setData(result);
        setError(undefined);
      } catch (err) {
        if (ticket !== activeRef.current) return;
        setError(
          err instanceof ApiError
            ? err
            : new ApiError(0, "Something went wrong.", err),
        );
      } finally {
        if (ticket === activeRef.current) {

}

```text
// Side-effectful lifecycle hooks (cron registration, eager Xero syncs, IMAP
// connect) must only run when the server actually listens — never during the
// OpenAPI export (which instantiates the module graph but never serves).
// main.ts sets ROBYN_SERVE=1; openapi.ts leaves it unset.

export function isServing(): boolean {
  return process.env.ROBYN_SERVE === '1';
}

```md
# Porting blueprint: Google OAuth + Calendar sync (from myMan_backend_core)

Reference root: `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core` (abbrev `REF`). All citations `file:line` relative to `REF`.
Stack of reference: NestJS 11, TypeORM 0.3.27, `googleapis@^164.0.0`, `@nestjs/schedule@^6.0.1` (`package.json:49,54,64,80`).

---

## 1. The exact Google OAuth dance

### Client construction (shared pattern)
- `new google.auth.OAuth2(clientId, clientSecret, redirectUri)` built once in the service constructor from `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` / `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` config — `src/modules/calendar-providers/calendar-providers.service.ts:55-67`.
- Redirect URI is NOT a backend route. It is `${frontendUrl}/calendar/callback` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:40-44`, with frontend URL chosen by `NODE_ENV` from `FRONTEND_URL_PROD` / `FRONTEND_URL_DEV` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:46-53`). See gotcha #1.

### Auth-URL generation
`calendar-providers.service.ts:93-101`:
\```ts
this.googleOAuth2Client.generateAuthUrl({
  access_type: 'offline',                       // required to get refresh_token
  scope: ['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly',
          'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events'],
  prompt: 'consent',                            // forces refresh_token on re-consent
  state: JSON.stringify({ provider: 'google', timestamp: Date.now() }),
});
\```
Returned as `{ authUrl }` (`dto/oauth-url-response.dto.ts:3-10`).

### Callback / code exchange
There is no backend GET callback (deliberately removed, see gotcha #1). Frontend lands on `/calendar/callback?code=...&state=...`, then POSTs the code back to an authenticated endpoint. Exchange:
- `const { tokens } = await this.googleOAuth2Client.getToken(code); this.googleOAuth2Client.setCredentials(tokens);` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:133-134`.
- Then `calendar.calendarList.list()` to find the `primary` calendar (`calendar-providers.service.ts:137-139`), and a `CalendarProvider` row is created with `accessToken: tokens.access_token`, `refreshToken: tokens.refresh_token`, `tokenExpiresAt: new Date(tokens.expiry_date)` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:145-158`.
- On save, an immediate background sync is fired: `this.calendarSyncService.syncProvider(savedProvider.id).catch(...)` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:161-165`.
- Two-step variant: `listGoogleCalendars(code)` exchanges the code ONCE and returns `{ calendars, tokens }` to the frontend (`calendar-providers.service.ts:224-253`); `connectSpecificGoogleCalendars(tokens, calendarIds)` then reuses those tokens without a second exchange (`calendar-providers.service.ts:296-358`). Important: an auth code is single-use; that's why tokens are round-tripped.

### Token storage (entity)
`src/modules/calendar-providers/entities/calendar-provider.entity.ts`:
- `accessToken: text` (line 37-38), `refreshToken: text nullable` (40-41), `tokenExpiresAt: timestamp nullable` (43-44)
- `providerType` enum google|microsoft (28-32), `providerAccountId` = Google calendarId (34-35)
- `syncStatus` enum active|syncing|error|inactive (61-66), `syncError: text` (68-69), `lastSyncedAt` (58-59)
- `syncToken: varchar(500) nullable` for incremental sync (71-72), `isActive` (55-56), `isDeleted` soft delete (83-84)

### Refresh logic
`src/modules/calendar-sync/calendar-sync.service.ts:376-401`:
- Trigger: before each sync, `isTokenExpired(provider.tokenExpiresAt)` with a 5-minute early buffer (`calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`); if expired → `refreshGoogleToken(provider)` then reload the row (`calendar-sync.service.ts:101-109`).
- Mechanics: fresh `new google.auth.OAuth2(clientId, clientSecret)` (no redirect URI needed), `setCredentials({ refresh_token })`, `await oauth2Client.refreshAccessToken()`, then persist `accessToken` + `tokenExpiresAt = new Date(credentials.expiry_date)` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:379-394`). Refresh token itself is not rotated/rewritten.
- Failure handling: log + rethrow (`calendar-sync.service.ts:397-400`); the caller marks the provider `syncStatus: ERROR, syncError: error.message` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:65-71`). No automatic re-consent; user must reconnect.

### Disconnect
Soft delete only — `isDeleted = true, isActive = false`, tokens left in row; no Google token revocation call (`calendar-providers.service.ts:494-506`). Controller: `DELETE /calendar-providers/:id`, 204 (`calendar-providers.controller.ts:146-153`).

---

## 2. googleapis usage details

- Client per sync run: `google.calendar({ version: 'v3', auth: oauth2Client })` with `setCredentials({ access_token, refresh_token })` — `calendar-sync.service.ts:111-121`.
- `calendar.events.list` params — `calendar-sync.service.ts:124-130`:
  \```ts
  { calendarId: provider.providerAccountId, maxResults: 250,
    singleEvents: true, orderBy: 'startTime', timeMin: new Date().toISOString() }
  \```
  (future events only; `singleEvents: true` expands recurrences; no `timeMax` in reference).
- Incremental sync: if `provider.syncToken` exists, set `syncParams.syncToken` and DELETE `timeMin` — they are mutually exclusive (`calendar-sync.service.ts:132-136`). After sync, persist `response.data.nextSyncToken` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:158-163`). On HTTP 410 / "Sync token" error: null out syncToken and recurse into a full sync (`calendar-sync.service.ts:168-179`).
- Pagination: NOT implemented in the reference — single page, `maxResults: 250`, `nextPageToken` ignored (`calendar-sync.service.ts:141-142`). Fine for a demo; loop on `pageToken = response.data.nextPageToken` if you need >250.

---

## 3. Controller surface

`src/modules/calendar-providers/calendar-providers.controller.ts`:
- Class-level `@UseGuards(FirebaseAuthGuard)` + `@ApiBearerAuth()` on `@Controller('calendar-providers')` (lines 32-36). Global prefix makes it `/api/v1/calendar-providers`.
- Routes: `GET google/auth-url` (41-46), `POST google/connect` body `{code}` (55-62), `POST google/list-calendars` (75-83), `POST google/connect-multiple` body `{tokens, calendarIds}` (95-105), `GET /` (119-124), `GET :id` (126-132), `PATCH :id` (134-144), `DELETE :id` 204 (146-153), `POST :id/sync` (155-163). Microsoft mirrors omitted here.
- DTOs: `ConnectGoogleCalendarDto { code: string }` with `@IsString @IsNotEmpty` (`dto/connect-google-calendar.dto.ts`); `ConnectWithTokensDto { tokens: object, calendarIds?: string[] }` (`dto/connect-with-tokens.dto.ts`).
- State param does NOT carry user identity — it is only `{ provider, timestamp }` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:100`). Identity comes from the Firebase bearer token on the POST that carries the code; the reference is effectively single-user (`CALENDAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md:210` "Single User System"). For our single-user demo, same shape works with no guard.

---

## 4. Sync strategy

- Triggers: (a) immediately after connect, fire-and-forget (`calendar-providers.service.ts:161-165`); (b) manual `POST :id/sync` → `triggerSync` sets `syncStatus=SYNCING` then awaits `syncProvider` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:508-533`); (c) cron every 15 min via `@Cron('*/15 * * * *')` → `syncAllProviders()` — `src/modules/calendar-sync/calendar-sync.scheduler.ts:14-26`, registered with `ScheduleModule.forRoot()` in `calendar-sync.module.ts:12`.
- Circular dep note: providers module and sync module reference each other; broken with `forwardRef` + a string-token provider `'CalendarSyncService'` (`calendar-sync.module.ts:17-20`, `calendar-providers.service.ts:33-34`, `calendar-providers.module.ts:13`). For the port: avoid entirely by putting sync + oauth in one module.
- Upsert key: `(calendarProviderId, externalEventId)` — lookup at `calendar-sync.service.ts:251-256`; migration adds a unique constraint on that pair (`CALENDAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md:239`).
- Update detection: skip write when `existingEvent.externalUpdatedAt === event.updated` (Google's `updated` timestamp stored as string) — `calendar-sync.service.ts:297-302`; entity field `externalUpdatedAt: varchar(100)` (`calendar-event.entity.ts:69-70`).
- Cancellations: `event.status === 'cancelled'` → soft delete (`isDeleted: true, status: CANCELLED`) — `calendar-sync.service.ts:258-270`. All-day detection: `!!event.start.date` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:289`); start/end use `dateTime || date` (285-286).
- Full-sync reconciliation: after a non-syncToken sync, local future events whose externalEventId wasn't returned get soft-deleted (`removeStaleLocalEvents`, `calendar-sync.service.ts:466-489`, called at 154-156).
- Event entity fields worth copying: `externalEventId varchar(500)`, `title`, `description`, `startTime/endTime timestamp`, `location`, `attendees jsonb`, `isAllDay`, `status enum`, `rawData jsonb`, `externalUpdatedAt`, `isDeleted` — `calendar-event.entity.ts:32-98`.
- Read API: `GET /calendar-events` filters by providerId/status/date-range with TypeORM `Between/MoreThanOrEqual/LessThanOrEqual` on `startTime` — `src/modules/calendar-events/calendar-events.service.ts:25-51`.

---

## 5. Gotchas (from OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md / CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md)

1. **Backend GET callback + auth guard = 401.** Google redirects the browser with no bearer token; a guarded backend callback endpoint 401s. Fix used: redirect to a FRONTEND page, which extracts `code` and POSTs it to an authenticated endpoint (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:3-8,13-22`). For our demo (no auth), a plain unguarded backend `GET /callback` is fine — simpler than the reference.
2. **Redirect URI must match Google Cloud Console EXACTLY** — every URI used must be registered (dev + prod separately) (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:100-118`). `redirect_uri_mismatch` is the classic failure.
3. **Auth codes are single-use.** The list-then-connect flow exchanges once and hands `tokens` back to the client for the second call (`calendar-providers.service.ts:246-249`) — never call `getToken(code)` twice.
4. **`prompt: 'consent'` + `access_type: 'offline'` or no refresh_token.** Google only returns `refresh_token` on first consent unless you force the consent screen (`calendar-providers.service.ts:94,99`). `tokens.refresh_token` can still be null — entity column is nullable (`calendar-provider.entity.ts:40-41`).
5. **`syncToken` and `timeMin` are mutually exclusive**; and a 410 means the token expired → clear and full-resync (`calendar-sync.service.ts:132-136,168-179`).
6. **Refresh 5 min early** to avoid mid-request expiry races (`calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`); after refreshing, RELOAD the entity so the sync uses the new access token (`calendar-sync.service.ts:104-108`).
7. **Sync failures must land in `syncStatus`/`syncError`** or debugging is blind — the whole CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md workflow is "check syncStatus, check syncError, resync per provider" (`CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md:13-18,43-46`).
8. **Scopes changed mid-project** (readonly → events) requiring re-consent (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:33-38`) — pick final scopes before first connect or you'll reconnect.

---

## 6. Port plan for the target app (NestJS 10, TypeORM, single-user demo)

One module, e.g. `api/src/modules/google/`. No guards, no multi-user, backend-callback flow (valid here because nothing is guarded — gotcha #1 does not apply). `npm i googleapis`.

### Entity (single row, `google_connection`)
\```ts
id uuid PK; accessToken text; refreshToken text nullable;
tokenExpiresAt timestamptz nullable; grantedScopes text;      // space-separated, from token response
calendarSyncToken varchar(500) nullable; lastSyncedAt timestamptz nullable;
syncStatus varchar; syncError text nullable;
\```
(Trimmed from `calendar-provider.entity.ts`; drop providerType/isPrimary/color. `grantedScopes` is new — see degradation below.) Plus a `google_event` table if events must persist: copy `externalEventId` + unique `(connectionId, externalEventId)` upsert key per section 4.

### Endpoints (mirror reference shapes, citations = pattern source)
- `GET /google/auth-url` → `generateAuthUrl({ access_type:'offline', prompt:'consent', state: <random nonce>, scope: [calendar.readonly, gmail.readonly] })` — pattern `calendar-providers.service.ts:93-101`. Persist the nonce; single user means state is CSRF-only.
- `GET /google/callback?code&state` (registered as the redirect URI): verify state, `oauth2Client.getToken(code)` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:133`), upsert the single row storing `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `new Date(tokens.expiry_date)`, **and `tokens.scope`** into `grantedScopes`; fire background sync (`calendar-providers.service.ts:145-165`); redirect (302) to the web app's settings page.
- `POST /google/sync` → refresh-if-needed (5-min buffer, `calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`) then `events.list` with `timeMin`/`singleEvents:true`/`orderBy:'startTime'` or `syncToken` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:124-141`), upsert per section 4, save `nextSyncToken`, handle 410 (`calendar-sync.service.ts:168-179`).
- `DELETE /google/disconnect` → soft-clear the row (pattern `calendar-providers.service.ts:494-506`); optionally also `oauth2Client.revokeToken(accessToken)` (improvement over reference).
- Skip the cron initially; the reference's 15-min `@Cron` (`calendar-sync.scheduler.ts:14`) is a 10-line add later.

### Gmail readonly in the same consent + per-scope degradation
- Add `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly` to the same `scope` array in `generateAuthUrl` — one consent screen, one token pair.
- Google users can UNCHECK individual scopes; the token response's `scope` field (space-separated) lists what was actually granted. Store it (`grantedScopes`) at callback time; gate each feature: `grantedScopes.includes('gmail.readonly')` → enable mail ingest, else show "Gmail not granted" without breaking calendar. Reference has no equivalent — this is the one net-new piece.
- Gmail read basics (same authed client):
  \```ts
  const gmail = google.gmail({ version: 'v1', auth: oauth2Client });
  const list = await gmail.users.messages.list({ userId: 'me', q: 'has:attachment newer_than:30d', maxResults: 50 });
  // list.data.messages: [{id, threadId}]; page with list.data.nextPageToken
  const msg = await gmail.users.messages.get({ userId: 'me', id, format: 'full' }); // payload.parts for attachments
  \```
  A Gmail call with an ungranted scope returns 403 `insufficientPermissions` — catch it and flip the feature flag rather than erroring the whole sync.
- Reuse the exact refresh routine from `calendar-sync.service.ts:376-401` for both APIs (one token pair covers all granted scopes).

### Env
`GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI` (the backend callback URL, registered verbatim in Google Cloud Console — gotcha #2), asserted non-empty at boot per this repo's rules.

```

WROTE undefined/oauth-backend.md
- Reference located at /Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core; blueprint written to /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/undefined/oauth-backend.md with file:line citations throughout.
- OAuth: generateAuthUrl with access_type=offline + prompt=consent (service.ts:93-101); code exchange via getToken(code) (service.ts:133); tokens in CalendarProvider entity (accessToken/refreshToken/tokenExpiresAt, entity.ts:37-44).
- Refresh: 5-min-early expiry check (sync.service.ts:443-448), refreshAccessToken + persist (376-401), failures land in syncStatus/syncError.
- googleapis: events.list with calendarId/maxResults 250/singleEvents/orderBy startTime/timeMin (sync.service.ts:124-130); syncToken incremental sync with 410 fallback (132-179); NO pagination in reference.
- Sync: upsert on (calendarProviderId, externalEventId), skip-if externalUpdatedAt unchanged, cancelled=soft delete, 15-min cron scheduler.
- Key gotcha: guarded backend GET callback caused 401 (OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:3-8) — they moved redirect to frontend; our unauthed demo can use a plain backend GET /callback instead.
- State param carries only {provider,timestamp}, not identity — identity came from the bearer token on the code-POST; single-user port needs neither.
- Port plan: one google/ module, single-row entity with grantedScopes column, 4 endpoints, gmail.readonly in same consent, degrade per token response 'scope' field + 403 insufficientPermissions catch.

```md
# Data-feasibility map: agentic finance chat + Xero-style dashboard charts

All paths relative to `api/src/`. Line numbers from current `main` (commit 915752e).

---

## 1. Chat tool inventory (read-only, over existing services)

All candidate tools call existing injectable services. XeroModule is `@Global()` (`modules/xero/xero.module.ts:6`), so a new ChatModule gets `XeroService` for free; DB-backed tools need the relevant `TypeOrmModule.forFeature` repos or the owning service exported.

| # | Tool | Backing call | Input params | Output shape | New code needed? |
|---|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|------------------|
| 1 | `dashboard_summary` | `DashboardService.summary()` — `modules/dashboard/dashboard.service.ts:255-284` | none | `{openTasks, unbilledGbp, invoicesSentThisMonth, clientsCount}` | No |
| 2 | `leak_breakdown` | `DashboardService.leakStrip()` — `dashboard.service.ts:164-237` | none | `{monthLabel, recoverableGbp, breakdown:[{source,gbp,count}x3]}` (detections + in-review proposals + unbilled meetings) | No |
| 3 | `list_proposals` | `ProposalsService.list(state?)` — `modules/proposals/proposals.service.ts:66-78` | `state?: DRAFT\|IN_REVIEW\|APPROVED\|SENT\|REJECTED` (`entities/enums.ts:23-29`) | `ProposalSummaryDto[]` (clientName, total, state, reference, xeroDeepLink — mapped at `proposals.service.ts:416-441`) | No |
| 4 | `proposal_detail` | `ProposalsService.detail(id)` — `proposals.service.ts:80-167` | `id: uuid` | summary + `lines[]` + evidence chain (meeting/transcript/detection/contract) + live `xeroStatus` | No |
| 5 | `list_detections` | `DetectionsService.list(state?, type?)` — `modules/detections/detections.service.ts:433-459` | `state?: OPEN\|PROPOSED\|RESOLVED\|DISMISSED`, `type?:` 3 detector types (`enums.ts:32-43`) | `DetectionDto[]` (valueGbp, summary, source, proposalId link) | No |
| 6 | `open_tasks` | `TasksService.listOpen()` — `modules/tasks/tasks.service.ts:44-50`; badge counts via `counts()` — `tasks.service.ts:61-70` | none | `TaskDto[]` enriched with client/meeting/proposal context + resolution endpoint hint (`tasks.service.ts:81-96`) | No |
| 7 | `list_meetings` | `MeetingsService.list(state?)` — `modules/meetings/meetings.service.ts:379-396`; ranged calendar via `DashboardService.calendar(from,to)` — `dashboard.service.ts:113-161` | `state?: MeetingState` or `from/to` ISO dates | `MeetingListItemDto[]` / `CalendarEventDto[]` (colorKey, clientName, proposalId) | No |
| 8 | `meeting_utilisation` | aggregate over `Meeting` repo: group by `state` (`enums.ts:4-13`), sum `durationHours` (`entities/meeting.entity.ts` numeric col), billed = SENT, unbilled = `UNBILLED_MEETING_STATES` (`dashboard.service.ts:49-54`) | `month?: YYYY-MM` | `{month, billedHours, unbilledHours, personalHours, byState[]}` | **Yes — small new query method** (one `find` + reduce, pattern identical to `leakStrip`) |
| 9 | `client_revenue` | aggregate: `InvoiceProposal` where `state=SENT` grouped by `clientId`, sum `total` (`entities/invoice-proposal.entity.ts` cols `clientId`, `total`, `state`); names via `ClientsService.listClients()` — `modules/clients/clients.service.ts:54` | `topN?: number` | `[{clientId, clientName, sentGbp, proposalCount}]` | **Yes — small new query method** |
| 10 | `list_unpaid_invoices` (live Xero) | `XeroService.listInvoices(['AUTHORISED','SUBMITTED'])` — `modules/xero/xero-api.ts:188-196`; fields `AmountDue`, `DueDate`, `Status`, `Total` on `XeroInvoice` (`xero-api.ts:89-106`) | `statuses?: string[]`, `page?: number` | `{invoices: XeroInvoice[], pagination}` — compute `overdue = DueDate < today` in the tool wrapper | No (overdue flag computed in wrapper) |
| 11 | `payments_received` (live Xero) | `XeroService.listPayments()` — `xero-api.ts:259-262` (`XeroPayment {Date, Amount, Reference, Invoice}` `xero-api.ts:250-257`) | `page?: number` | payment list; group by month in wrapper | No (but see the `/Date(ms)/` parsing caveat in §3 chart 2) |
| 12 | `aged_receivables_live` | `XeroService.agedReceivablesByContact(contactId)` — `xero-api.ts:336-340` (raw report envelope, typed `any`) | `clientId: uuid` → resolve `Client.xeroContactId` (`entities/client.entity.ts` unique col) | Xero report Rows envelope | No, but wrapper must map clientId→contactId and handle `xeroContactId=null` |
| 13 | `audit_trail` | `DashboardService.auditTrail(limit)` — `dashboard.service.ts:240-252` / `AuditService.list(limit=200)` — `modules/audit/audit.service.ts:36` | `limit?: number` | `AuditEventDto[]` newest-first | No |

Guard-rails: tools 10-12 hit Xero live — respect the 60/min budget (retry/429 handling already inside `xeroRequest`, `modules/xero/xero-http.ts:304-331`); probe `XeroService.health()` (`modules/xero/xero.service.ts:43-47`) once per chat turn and degrade to DB-backed tools when not live (same pattern as `DetectionsService.runDetectors`, `detections.service.ts:82-87`). Every tool call should write an `AuditEvent` via `AuditService.record` (`audit.service.ts`) per the no-silent-reads-into-actions rule.

---

## 2. Existing Anthropic integration (`modules/llm/`)

- **SDK, not raw fetch:** `import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'` — `llm.service.ts:2`; version `^0.68.0` (`api/package.json:18`). Client built once in the constructor with `loadConfig().anthropicApiKey` (`llm.service.ts:27-29`; env assert at `config/env.ts:65`).
- **Model:** `const MODEL = 'claude-sonnet-5'` — `llm.service.ts:20`. Single constant; chat service should import/share it.
- **Call shape:** non-streaming `this.client.messages.create({model, max_tokens, system, messages:[single user turn]})` — `llm.service.ts:49-62`. Per-call `max_tokens`: 800-2500 (default 2000, `llm.service.ts:45`).
- **Validation pattern:** `complete<S extends z.ZodTypeAny>()` (`llm.service.ts:41-73`) — extract JSON from the text block (`extractJson`, `llm.service.ts:31-39`, tolerates fences/prose), `schema.parse(...)`, retry ONCE with a corrective suffix, then throw. All schemas in `modules/llm/schemas.ts` (zod, "nothing unvalidated crosses this boundary", `schemas.ts:3-4`).
- **Health probe:** 8-token ping (`llm.service.ts:144-156`).

**Missing for multi-turn tool-use chat (what a ChatService adds on top of this plumbing):**
1. **No `tools` parameter anywhere** — no tool definitions, no `tool_use`/`tool_result` loop. SDK 0.68.0 supports the manual loop (`stop_reason === 'tool_use'` → execute → push `tool_result` blocks in ONE user message) and the beta tool runner (`client.beta.messages.toolRunner` + `betaZodTool` from `@anthropic-ai/sdk/helpers/beta/zod`) — the zod-per-tool-input style matches the project's existing zod discipline.
2. **No streaming** — everything is `messages.create`. For chat use `client.messages.stream(...)` (iterate `content_block_delta` / `text` events, `finalMessage()` for the complete turn) or `toolRunner({..., stream: true})`; surface to the frontend as SSE from a Nest controller (`@Sse()` or raw `res.write`), which also satisfies the "optimistic/streaming where the agent is thinking" frontend rule.
3. **No conversation state** — `complete()` sends one user turn. Chat needs message-history assembly (client-supplied history is fine for demo; a `ChatMessage` entity is optional) and the tool_use/tool_result blocks appended verbatim between turns.
4. **No system prompt for chat persona/tool policy** — the four existing prompts are extraction-specific (`llm.service.ts:76-142`).

Reuse: the `Anthropic` client instance (export it or add a `chat()` method on `LlmService`), the `MODEL` constant, the zod-validate-everything pattern, and the audit-on-LLM-failure pattern (`AuditActor.LLM`, e.g. `meetings.service.ts:140-148`).

---

## 3. Charts feasibility (Xero-dashboard-style board)

XeroModule is global, so `DashboardService` can inject `XeroService` without module changes (`xero.module.ts:6-10`); all four charts fit as new methods on `dashboard.service.ts` + new GET endpoints on `dashboard.controller.ts` (+ regenerated `openapi.json` / `web/lib/api-types.ts` per the contract rule).

### Chart 1 — Invoices owed (draft / awaiting payment / overdue, with £)
- **Live source:** `XeroService.listInvoices(['DRAFT','SUBMITTED','AUTHORISED'])` (`xero-api.ts:188-196`). Buckets: `Status==='DRAFT'||'SUBMITTED'` → draft (sum `Total`); `AUTHORISED && DueDate >= today` → awaiting payment (sum `AmountDue`); `AUTHORISED && DueDate < today` → overdue (sum `AmountDue`). Fields on `XeroInvoice` at `xero-api.ts:96-104`.
- **Local fallback (Xero down):** `InvoiceProposal` by `state` — DRAFT/IN_REVIEW → draft, SENT → awaiting (sum `total`); no overdue signal locally.
- **Wiring:** new `GET /dashboard/invoices-owed` extending `dashboard.service.ts`. One Xero page read per load — cache-friendly, fits the 60/min rule.

### Chart 2 — Cash in per month (payments received)
- **Live source:** `XeroService.listPayments()` (`xero-api.ts:259-262`), group `Date` by `YYYY-MM`, sum `Amount`.
- **Caveat:** the Accounting API returns JSON dates as `/Date(1718150400000+0000)/` — `XeroPayment.Date` is typed `string` (`xero-api.ts:252`); the aggregation needs a tiny `parseXeroDate()` helper (regex the epoch ms). Nothing in the codebase parses this yet — flagged as new code.
- **Local fallback:** SENT `InvoiceProposal.createdAt` by month (the retainer detector already does exactly this month-keying — `detections.service.ts:273-282`, `monthKey` helper `detections.service.ts:735-737`).
- **Wiring:** new `GET /dashboard/cash-in` extending `dashboard.service`.

### Chart 3 — Money Robyn found (recovered vs pending)
- **Source (all local, already injected in DashboardService — `dashboard.service.ts:65-66`):** `Detection` rows: pending = `state IN (OPEN, PROPOSED)` sum `valueGbp`; recovered = `state=RESOLVED` sum `valueGbp` **plus** detection-sourced proposals that reached SENT (join `InvoiceProposal.detectionId` — the map pattern already exists at `detections.service.ts:446-450`). Dismissed excluded.
- **Wiring:** extend `dashboard.service` (no new repos needed); new `GET /dashboard/robyn-found`. Optionally split by `DetectionType` for a stacked bar.

### Chart 4 — Unbilled pipeline
- **Source:** exactly the `leakStrip()` inputs (`dashboard.service.ts:165-199`): open detections £ + IN_REVIEW proposals £ + unbilled-meeting estimates (`estimateMeetingValue`, `dashboard.service.ts:94-103`). Per-client variant: group the same rows by `clientId`, or use the denormalised `Client.unbilledExposureGbp` (`entities/client.entity.ts`, already summed in `summary()` at `dashboard.service.ts:273-276`).
- **Wiring:** the totals endpoint already exists (`GET /dashboard/leak-strip` returns the 3-part breakdown — chartable as-is with **zero backend work**); a per-client stacked version is a small extension of `leakStrip()`.

Summary: charts 3 and 4 are pure-DB and near-free; charts 1 and 2 are live-Xero reads (one API call each, cache/If-Modified-Since friendly) plus small aggregation methods. All four belong in `dashboard.service.ts` per the existing "aggregate read surface" comment (`dashboard.module.ts:14-16`).

---

## 4. Agent-settings persistence

- **Closest existing pattern:** `ConnectionState` (`entities/connection-state.entity.ts`) — a one-row-per-kind config table with a unique-indexed key column, extending `BaseEntity` (uuid PK + `createdAt`/`updatedAt`, `entities/base.entity.ts`). Upsert pattern in `MeetingsService.upsertCalendarConnection` (`meetings.service.ts:924-932`): find-by-kind, create-if-missing, save.
- **Recommended shape:** `AgentSetting { @Index({unique:true}) key: varchar; value: jsonb }` — use a plain `varchar` key, **not** a Postgres enum column: with `synchronize: true` (`app.module.ts:35`, "hackathon: schema auto-managed. No migrations") a **new table/column auto-creates on boot**, but altering an existing enum's members is where TypeORM synchronize gets flaky — varchar keys avoid that entirely. Register the entity in `ALL_ENTITIES` (`entities/index.ts:27-37`) or it won't be created.
- **Per-client autonomy already exists** and is the model precedent: `Client.autonomyEnabled` (`entities/client.entity.ts`) toggled via `ClientsService.setAutonomy` (`clients.service.ts:93`) — global chat-agent settings (e.g. `chat.model`, `chat.max_tool_calls`, `chat.xero_live_tools_enabled`) should mirror that but in the key-value table.
- Settings mutations must write an `AuditEvent` (`entities/audit-event.entity.ts:5-6`: "no silent mutations").

```

```md
# UI-Pattern Blueprint: content-studio → Settings section

Source repo: `DASH = /Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard`
All citations `file:line` relative to DASH. Stack: Next.js app router, shadcn/ui, RTK Query, sonner toasts, lucide icons.

---

## 1. Overall layout

- Settings is a **separate route** (`/content-studio/settings`), not a sidebar: `app/(authenticated)/content-studio/settings/page.tsx`.
- Page shell: `container mx-auto max-w-3xl px-4 py-6` (settings/page.tsx:14). Narrow single column, no sidebar.
- Header block: ghost back-link button with `ArrowLeft` icon (`settings/page.tsx:16-20`), then `h1 text-2xl font-bold` + one-sentence `text-sm text-muted-foreground` description (settings/page.tsx:21-24).
- Subsection nav = **shadcn Tabs**, `TabsList mb-4 h-auto flex-wrap`, one `TabsTrigger` per subsection, each `TabsContent` renders a fully self-contained tab component (settings/page.tsx:27-47). Four tabs: Skills / System prompt / Tools & MCP / Web search.
- Entry point from the feature page: outline button `asChild` wrapping a `Link` in the page header actions (`app/(authenticated)/content-studio/page.tsx:145-149`). Feature page header pattern: icon + title left, action buttons right, responsive `flex-col sm:flex-row` (page.tsx:128-151).
- Each tab owns its own data fetch and gates on a spinner: `h-40` centered `animate-spin rounded-full border-4 border-primary border-t-transparent` (SystemPromptTab.tsx:160-166, SkillsTab.tsx:92-95, McpServersTab.tsx:61-64).

## 2. Editing patterns

### 2a. Long-text / system-prompt editor (`components/content-studio/SystemPromptTab.tsx`)
- **Explicit save, no autosave, no dirty-state tracking.** Local `useState` seeded from server config (:29-34); a single primary button bottom-right `flex justify-end` with in-flight label swap `{saving ? "Saving…" : "Save system prompt"}` and `disabled={saving}` (:148-152).
- Editor remounts when server row changes via `key={config.id}` — comment "re-inits if the config row is replaced" (:167-168). Fetch wrapper component + inner `Editor` receiving loaded config (:157-169).
- Client validation before mutate, surfaced as `toast.error` (:40-43); `toast.success("System prompt saved")` / `toast.error(e?.data?.message ?? ...)` after (:51-53).
- Each field: `Label` + `Textarea rows={5-6} className="mt-1 text-sm"` + helper microcopy `mt-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground` explaining where the text is injected (:69-97).
- **Advanced override** gated in a bordered box `rounded-lg border p-4`: label + explanation left, `Switch` right (:99-115); when on, reveals placeholder chips (`<code>` pills, `rounded bg-muted px-1 text-[10px]`, :120-127), a "Load default template" outline button that fetches the default via lazy query (:128-135, :57-64), and a `rows={14} font-mono text-xs` textarea (:137-143). Empty string on save clears the override (:48-49).

### 2b. List-of-items management (`SkillsTab.tsx`, `McpServersTab.tsx`)
- **Cards, not tables.** `Card > CardContent className="flex items-start justify-between gap-4 p-4"`, stacked `space-y-3` (SkillsTab.tsx:113-150; McpServersTab.tsx:77-127).
- Card left: `font-semibold` name + `Badge` metadata (`text-[10px]`, "disabled" outline badge when off; transport/auth badges McpServersTab.tsx:82-96), `text-sm text-muted-foreground` description, `truncate font-mono text-xs` URL (McpServersTab.tsx:101), inline error line `text-xs text-destructive` from `lastError` (McpServersTab.tsx:107-109).
- Card right: inline `Switch` (enable/disable) + two `ghost size="icon"` buttons: `Pencil` (edit) and `Trash2` (delete) (SkillsTab.tsx:135-147).
- **Toggle = immediate PATCH** of just `{ enabled: !x.enabled }`, error toast only, no confirmation (SkillsTab.tsx:49-55; McpServersTab.tsx:33-39). **Delete = immediate DELETE** with success toast, no confirm dialog (SkillsTab.tsx:57-64).
- Header row above list: muted one-liner description left, `size="sm"` action buttons right (`Plus` "New skill" / "Add server") (SkillsTab.tsx:68-90; McpServersTab.tsx:51-59). SkillsTab also has hidden-`<input type="file">` Import with `Importing…` in-flight state (:76-85).
- Create/edit share one **Dialog**, remounted per target: `key={editing?.id ?? "new"}` and only rendered while open (SkillsTab.tsx:154-161; McpServersTab.tsx:130-137).

### 2c. Dialog form (`SkillDialog.tsx`, `McpServerDialog.tsx`)
- `DialogContent className="max-h-[85vh] overflow-y-auto sm:max-w-lg"` (SkillDialog.tsx:69), body `space-y-4`, each field `Label` + `Input/Textarea className="mt-1"`, instructions body `font-mono text-xs` (SkillDialog.tsx:100-107).
- Footer: `Cancel` outline + `Save` disabled/in-flight (SkillDialog.tsx:134-141). Required-field check → `toast.error` (:52-55). Success toast differs create vs edit (:60).
- **Pill multi-select**: `rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-xs`, selected = `border-primary bg-primary/10 text-primary`, else `hover:bg-muted` (SkillDialog.tsx:111-127; same chips on main page page.tsx:207-225).
- Two-column compact selects: `grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` for Transport / Auth (McpServerDialog.tsx:190-216).

### 2d. Credential / secret inputs (`McpServerDialog.tsx`)
- Bearer token: `Input type="password" font-mono text-xs` (:221-230). **Secrets never round-trip**: server returns only a boolean `authConfigured`; when editing with a stored secret and blank input, placeholder reads `"•••••• (stored — leave blank to keep)"` (:226-228) and the payload omits auth fields, referencing the stored secret by id for tests (`useStoredSecret`, :77-88, :102).
- Custom headers as `Name: value` lines in a textarea, parsed client-side (`parseHeaders`, :43-53). Auth fields required only on create (:126-133).

### 2e. Test-connection affordance (`McpServerDialog.tsx:251-294`)
- Bordered box `rounded-lg border p-3` titled "Tools" with an outline `Test connection` button (`Testing…` in flight) (:252-257).
- POST to a `/test` endpoint (no persistence); success → `toast.success("Connected — N tool(s) found")` and renders discovered tools as a scrollable `max-h-48` checkbox allowlist (name mono + truncated description) (:104-107, :258-286). Failure → error toast (:108-113). Untested fallback microcopy explains allowlist state (:287-293). Empty allowlist = allow all (:260-262).

### 2f. Toggles + numeric params (`WebSearchTab.tsx`)
- Master feature toggle in a bordered row: label + explainer left, `Switch` right (:48-59); dependent fields greyed via `opacity-50 pointer-events-none` when off (:61).
- Numeric param: `Input type="number" min={1} max={20}` in a `max-w-[200px]` wrapper, **clamped on save** `Math.min(20, Math.max(1, n))` (:62-74, :36).
- Domain lists: newline textarea parsed to `string[]`, prefix-stripped (:16-21, :37-38). Explicit `Save web search` button same as 2a (:108-112).

## 3. Persistence

- RTK Query, **invalidate-and-refetch, no optimistic updates**: every mutation `invalidatesTags`, list queries `providesTags` (`managers/apiManager.ts:2064-2137`).
- Endpoints: `GET/POST /api/v1/social-content/skills`, `PATCH/DELETE /skills/:id`, `POST /skills/import` (FormData, :2084-2091); singleton `GET/PATCH /agent-config` (:2094-2105) + `GET /agent-config/default-template` (:2106-2108); `GET/POST /mcp-servers`, `PATCH/DELETE /mcp-servers/:id`, `POST /mcp-servers/test` (test is a mutation with no tags — never cached, :2131-2137).
- Partial PATCH everywhere: toggle sends only `{enabled}`; config PATCH sends only that tab's fields.
- **All DTO types codegen from OpenAPI** — `interfaces/contentStudioInterface.ts:1-31` derives every type from `components['schemas']` of `lib/api-types.ts`; comment: "never hand-write API payload shapes" (:2-3). Matches this repo's contract rule exactly.

## 4. Visual language — why it reads clean

- One narrow column (`max-w-3xl`), generous rhythm: `space-y-6` between form groups, `space-y-3` between cards, `p-4` card padding, `mb-4/5` section gaps.
- Three text sizes only: `text-2xl font-bold` page title, `text-sm` body/descriptions, `text-xs text-muted-foreground` helper microcopy under every field. Every tab and every field explains itself in one muted sentence.
- Cards + badges instead of tables; mono font reserved for machine text (URLs, tokens, prompt bodies, tool names).
- Empty states: `border-2 border-dashed py-16` centered — optional icon, `font-medium` title, muted subtitle, primary CTA (SkillsTab.tsx:96-111; McpServersTab.tsx:65-75 with `ServerCog` icon).
- Every async action has exactly three states: idle label, in-flight label (`Saving…`/`Testing…`/`Importing…`) + `disabled`, and a sonner toast outcome. No silent successes or failures.
- Destructive/edit actions are quiet `ghost size="icon"` buttons; primary action per view is a single filled button.

## 5. Blueprint: target app Settings (three subsections)

Route `web/app/settings/page.tsx` (or equivalent): `max-w-3xl` shell + back link + `h1 "Settings"` + muted description + `Tabs` with `Agent | Tools | Skills` — clone settings/page.tsx:12-50 structure verbatim.

### (a) Agent — system prompt + model params → SystemPromptTab pattern
- Backend: singleton `GET/PATCH /agent-config` (apiManager.ts:2094-2105 shape) in NestJS with Swagger DTOs.
- UI: fetch-wrapper + `<Editor key={config.id}>` (SystemPromptTab.tsx:157-169); `Textarea rows={10} font-mono text-xs` for the system prompt with helper microcopy; model params (temperature, max tokens, model select) as `grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` of clamped number Inputs / Select (WebSearchTab.tsx:62-74 + McpServerDialog.tsx:190-216); explicit `Save agent` bottom-right with toasts (SystemPromptTab.tsx:39-55, 148-152). Optional "Reset to default prompt" = "Load default template" affordance (:128-135).

### (b) Tools — built-in toggles + MCP servers → WebSearchTab + McpServersTab/McpServerDialog
- Top: "Web search" as a bordered master-toggle row with dependent params greyed when off (WebSearchTab.tsx:48-61); persist into the same agent-config PATCH.
- Below: "MCP servers" card list exactly per McpServersTab.tsx:49-139 — name + transport/auth/disabled badges, mono URL, `lastError` destructive line, inline enable Switch (immediate PATCH), pencil/trash ghost icons, dashed empty state, `Add server` dialog.
- Dialog per McpServerDialog.tsx: name/description/url, transport+auth selects, `type="password"` token with stored-secret placeholder + `authConfigured` boolean (never echo secrets, :77-88, :226-228), bordered **Test connection** box → `POST /mcp-servers/test` → toast + discovered-tool checkbox allowlist (:251-294).
- Backend: CRUD + `/test` endpoints per apiManager.ts:2110-2137; secrets stored server-side only.

### (c) Skills — named instruction snippets → SkillsTab/SkillDialog near-verbatim
- Backend: `GET/POST /skills`, `PATCH/DELETE /skills/:id` (apiManager.ts:2064-2083).
- UI: header description + `New skill` button (SkillsTab.tsx:68-90, drop Import unless needed); card rows with name, "disabled" badge, muted description, inline Switch (immediate `{enabled}` PATCH, :49-55), pencil/trash (:135-147); dashed empty state (:96-111); dialog `key={editing?.id ?? "new"}` with Name Input, Description Textarea (rows 2, "trigger" microcopy SkillDialog.tsx:85-97), Instructions `rows={8} font-mono text-xs` (:98-108), Enabled switch row (:129-132), Cancel/Save footer (:134-141).

### Cross-cutting for target repo (per CLAUDE.md)
- Types: NestJS Swagger → `api/openapi.json` → `openapi-typescript` → `web/lib/api-types.ts`; mirror the `Schemas = components['schemas']` re-export discipline of contentStudioInterface.ts:8-31. No hand-written payload types.
- Persistence style: invalidate-and-refetch (no optimistic writes) + sonner toast per outcome; every mutation-bearing button gets an in-flight label + `disabled`. Add `cursor-pointer` and hover states on all clickables (repo rule; reference relies on shadcn defaults).
- Every Xero-facing or agent-config mutation should also emit an `AuditEvent` row server-side (repo rule; not present in reference).

WROTE undefined/chat-charts-data.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/undefined/chat-charts-data.md)

1. 13 read-only chat tools mapped to existing services; only 2 need new small query methods (meeting_utilisation, client_revenue) — everything else calls DashboardService/ProposalsService/DetectionsService/TasksService/MeetingsService/XeroService as-is.
2. LLM plumbing: official @anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.68.0, model `claude-sonnet-5` (llm.service.ts:20), non-streaming messages.create with zod-parse + one retry (llm.service.ts:41-73).
3. Missing for chat: tools param + tool_use loop, streaming (client.messages.stream or beta toolRunner), conversation history, chat system prompt — client instance and zod pattern are reusable.
4. Chart 1 (invoices owed) and 2 (cash in/month) are live Xero reads via listInvoices/listPayments (xero-api.ts:188,259); flag: Xero JSON dates need a `/Date(ms)/` parser (none exists yet).
5. Chart 3 (money Robyn found) is pure-DB over Detection states; chart 4 (unbilled pipeline) is chartable today from existing GET /dashboard/leak-strip with zero backend work.
6. XeroModule is @Global (xero.module.ts:6) so DashboardService/ChatService inject XeroService without module changes; all new endpoints extend dashboard.service.ts.
7. Agent settings: new `AgentSetting {key varchar unique, value jsonb}` entity mirroring ConnectionState; varchar not enum (TypeORM synchronize:true at app.module.ts:35 auto-creates tables but chokes on enum alters); must be added to ALL_ENTITIES.
8. Guard-rails noted: Xero-live tools budget 60/min (429 handling already in xero-http.ts:304-331), health-probe-then-degrade pattern, AuditEvent on every tool call and settings mutation.

# Robyn — integration-point map (current state)

Terse, citation-heavy. Paths relative to repo root `/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and `HANDOFF.md` before touching anything; this file only maps where things live.

---

## 1. Backend (api/, NestJS)

### Module registration — `api/src/app.module.ts`
- Global `ConfigModule` loads `api/.env` (app.module.ts:24-27). TypeORM Postgres with `synchronize: true`, entities from `ALL_ENTITIES` (app.module.ts:28-35, entity barrel `api/src/entities/index.ts`).
- `ScheduleModule.forRoot()` at app.module.ts:36 (cron support).
- Shared kernel: `AuditModule`, `XeroModule`, `LlmModule`, `EngineModule` (app.module.ts:38-41). Feature surfaces: `ClientsModule, MeetingsModule, EmailModule, ProposalsModule, DetectionsModule, TasksModule, ConnectionsModule, DashboardModule, HealthModule` (app.module.ts:43-51). **A new feature module gets imported here** — keep the kernel/surfaces grouping.

### Meetings module — `api/src/modules/meetings/`
- **Entities**: `Meeting` (`api/src/entities/meeting.entity.ts`): `gcalEventId` (unique, :14-16), `title/start/end`, `durationHours` (numeric, :28-29), `attendees` jsonb (:31-32), `clientId` nullable uuid (:34-36), `state` enum `MeetingState` default DETECTED (:38-40), `transcriptId`, `skipReason`, `matchProposals` jsonb, `isPersonal`, `source` `'google'|'ics'` (:56-57). `Transcript` (`api/src/entities/transcript.entity.ts`): `meetingId`, `rawText`, `source` enum (PASTED/GRANOLA/UPLOAD), `parsed` jsonb `TranscriptParsed` (:8-19).
- **Endpoints** (`meetings.controller.ts`): `GET /api/meetings` (:31-40, optional `?state=`), `GET /api/meetings/:id` (:42-51), `POST /api/meetings/sync` (:53-63 — Google if configured else `.ics` fixture, then Loop-1 on eligible meetings), `POST /api/meetings/import-ics` (:65-74, body `ImportIcsDto.icsText`), `POST /api/meetings/:id/transcript` (:76-87), `POST /api/meetings/:id/confirm-client` (:89-99), `POST /api/meetings/:id/skip` (:101-111). Cron: calendar sync every 15 min, gated on `isServing()` (:113-118; `api/src/common/bootstrap-flag.ts:6-8` — `ROBYN_SERVE=1` set only by `main.ts:10`, never during OpenAPI export).
- **How meetings get billing state**: `MeetingsService.runLoop1` (`meetings.service.ts:109-373`). Deterministic classify (engine, :125), LLM only proposes fuzzy matches for humans (:126-149, "The LLM never sets clientId"). Pre-proposal decision via `engine.step` (:166); state machine at `api/src/modules/engine/state-machine.ts:32-99` (states: personal→SKIPPED, UNKNOWN→UNKNOWN_ATTENDEE+queue potential client, no transcript→AWAITING_TRANSCRIPT+PROVIDE_TRANSCRIPT task, AMBIGUOUS→CONFIRM_CLIENT_MATCH task, MATCHED+transcript→build proposal; policy decides autoSend vs REVIEW_INVOICE). Proposal persisted idempotently by `reference = ROBYN-<meetingId8>` (:264-280). Auto-send path writes to Xero via `xero.writeInvoice` (:294-304) and flips `MeetingState.SENT`; failure or review path raises `REVIEW_INVOICE` task (:332-339, :356-363).
- Calendar ingest: `ingestCalendar` (:569-588) — Google when `cfg.google.configured` (dynamic `googleapis` import :674-724, needs `GOOGLE_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`), else `.ics` fixture from `seed/data/calendar.ics` (path candidates :611-627). Upsert keyed on `gcalEventId` (:726-764). CALENDAR ConnectionState upserted at `upsertCalendarConnection` (:924-932).
- Task raise/resolve helpers with `dedupeKey = type:refId` live here (:776-843) — reuse this pattern; do not invent a second task path.

### Connections module — `api/src/modules/connections/`
- **Entity** `ConnectionState` (`api/src/entities/connection-state.entity.ts`): unique `kind` (XERO/CALENDAR/EMAIL), `status` (LIVE/FALLBACK/DOWN), `label`, `detail`, `lastSyncAt`, `nextPollAt` (:9-26). Enums at `api/src/entities/enums.ts:84-94`.
- **LIVE/FALLBACK decision per provider**: XERO row is *probed live* every read via `xero.health()` — never persisted on GET, no FALLBACK state for Xero (LIVE or DOWN only) (`connections.service.ts:77-115`). CALENDAR row is upserted by MeetingsService (`meetings.service.ts:924-932`; LIVE when Google works :575-580, FALLBACK for `.ics` :601-608, DOWN when no file :592-599). EMAIL row is upserted by EmailService (`email.service.ts:281-298`; LIVE when `cfg.imap.configured` :69-71, DOWN if IMAP configured-but-unreachable :101, FALLBACK for fixture mailbox).
- **Endpoints** (`connections.controller.ts`): `GET /api/connections` (:12-21, stable order XERO/CALENDAR/EMAIL), `POST /api/connections/xero/recheck` (:23-33 — probes, upserts XERO row, audits, returns row; never throws on Xero down).

### Email module — `api/src/modules/email/`
- One endpoint: `POST /api/email/poll` (`email.controller.ts:13-17`). **Poll flow** (`email.service.ts:58-234`): load PotentialClients in QUEUED/WATCHING (:63-67) → if `cfg.imap.configured` read IMAP per queued sender with `SINCE lastPolledAt` (:390-446), else read fixture mailbox `seed/data/mailbox/*.json` once (:79-82, :316-345) and filter to queued senders only (:114-116). **Potential-client filter is a hard invariant**: "never reads a non-queued sender — that scope widening is a bug" (:37-41). Each message classified by `llm.classifyAgreement` (:124); on agreement: PotentialClient → AGREEMENT_DETECTED with evidence (:157-167), CONFIRM_AGREEMENT task raised idempotently by dedupeKey (:237-278), audit on every edge. EMAIL ConnectionState upserted at :281-298; nextPollAt = +30 min (:35, :61).

### Env config — `api/src/config/env.ts`
- `MissingEnvError` + `req()` assert-at-boot pattern (:5-19); `loadConfig()` cached (:50-86). Required: `DATABASE_URL`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `XERO_CLIENT_ID/SECRET` (:64-68). Optional blocks: `xero.scopes` (`XERO_SCOPES` override) and `xero.paymentAccountCode` (:69-70); **imap block** `IMAP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD` with `configured` boolean (:72-78); **google block** `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET` with `configured` (:79-83). New env vars go here, following `req`/`opt` and the `configured` flag pattern; `main.ts:11` calls `loadConfig()` so a missing required var kills boot.

### Audit service usage pattern — `api/src/modules/audit/audit.service.ts`
- `audit.record({ actor, action, summary, subjectType?, subjectId?, inputs? })` (:6-34). Actors: ROBYN/HUMAN/LLM/XERO/SYSTEM (`entities/enums.ts:98-104`). Action strings are dotted lowercase (`meeting.transcript_attached`, `xero.invoice.sent`, `task.raised`, `policy.decided`, `connection.xero.recheck`). Reads: `audit.list(limit)` (:36-38) and `audit.listForSubject(subjectId, limit)` (:40-46). Every state change, Xero write, policy decision, and poll goes through this — see e.g. meetings.service.ts:133-147, :254-261, :317-325; connections.service.ts:62-72; email.service.ts:170-183, :207-221.

### Swagger / OpenAPI generation
- `api/src/swagger.ts:3-21` builds the DocumentBuilder config (tags per surface — add a tag for a new surface here). `api/src/main.ts:24-25` serves docs at `/api/docs`. `api/src/openapi.ts:11-22` exports the spec to `api/openapi.json` **without listening** (that's why `isServing()` gates all cron/lifecycle side effects). Regen chain: root `package.json:16` `"openapi": "pnpm --filter robyn-api openapi && pnpm --filter robyn-web gen:types"`; api script `api/package.json:10` (`ts-node src/openapi.ts`); web script `web/package.json:10` (`openapi-typescript ../api/openapi.json -o lib/api-types.ts`). Any endpoint change ships in the same commit as regenerated spec + types (CLAUDE.md, API contract).

### Global prefix / CORS — `api/src/main.ts`
- `app.setGlobalPrefix('api', { exclude: ['health'] })` (:14) — controllers use bare paths (`@Controller('meetings')`), URLs are `/api/...`. CORS is wide open: `NestFactory.create(AppModule, { cors: true })` (:12). Global `ValidationPipe` with `transform + whitelist + enableImplicitConversion` (:15-21). Port from `PORT` env, default 4000 (`env.ts:63`).

### Other route surfaces (for chat tools / new endpoints)
- Tasks (read-only inbox): `GET /api/tasks`, `GET /api/tasks/all`, `GET /api/tasks/counts` (`tasks.controller.ts:11-31`; service comment "Tasks inbox is READ-ONLY... each card calls the owning module's endpoint" `tasks.service.ts:26-28`).
- Proposals: `GET /api/proposals`, `GET /api/proposals/:id`, `POST :id/approve|save-draft|reject`, `PATCH :id/lines` (`proposals.controller.ts:29-95`).
- Detections: `POST /api/detections/run`, `GET /api/detections`, `POST :id/propose`, `POST :id/dismiss` (`detections.controller.ts:36-89`).
- Clients: `GET /api/clients`, `GET :id`, `PATCH :id/autonomy`, `POST/GET :id/contract` (`clients.controller.ts:12-68`); potential clients: `GET /api/potential-clients`, `POST :id/confirm|dismiss` (`potential-clients.controller.ts:8-35`).

---

## 2. Frontend (web/, Next.js 14 app router)

### Structure
- Route group `web/app/(dashboard)/` with surfaces: `page.tsx` (home = Tasks inbox), `calendar/`, `clients/`, `invoices/`, `connections/`, `tasks/`. Each surface keeps its client components in a local `_components/` dir. Shell: `web/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` — fixed 64-wide sidebar (`:20-22`), `TopBar`, persistent `LeakStrip` (`:26-29`), content max-w-[1200px].

### Theme — where to flip the default to light
- `web/app/layout.tsx:43-48`: `<ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem disableTransitionOnChange>`. **Change `defaultTheme="system"` to `defaultTheme="light"` here** (optionally drop `enableSystem`). `web/components/theme-provider.tsx` is a thin next-themes wrapper (:6-11); the toggle is `web/components/mode-toggle.tsx`. Dark styles are `.dark` class-based (Tailwind).

### Fetch layer — `web/lib/api.ts`
- `API_BASE = NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE ?? "http://localhost:4000/api"` (:12-13). `ApiError` with status+body (:16-25); `request()` never throws raw fetch errors (:27-76). Typed helpers: `apiGet` (OpenAPI-path-literal-typed GET, :101-106), loose `get/post/patch` (:109-127). Hooks: `useApi(path, {refreshMs})` — loading/error/isValidating/refetch, ticket-based race guard (:146-199); `useAction(fn)` for button mutations (:205-232). Comment at :6-9: **the ONLY source of types is `lib/api-types.ts`**, generated (header at api-types.ts:1-4), never hand-edited.

### Calendar surface — `web/app/(dashboard)/calendar/_components/calendar-surface.tsx`
- Data hook: `useApi<CalendarEvent[]>("/dashboard/calendar")` (:101-102); event shape = `Schemas["CalendarEventDto"]` (:31) → `{id,title,start,end,state,colorKey,clientName,proposalId}` (see dashboard.service.ts:151-160). Month grid math :71-98; auto-centres on busiest month, fallback June 2026 (:85-98, :109-113). Colour tokens map through `colorKeyToTone`/`TONE_CLASS` from `web/lib/states.ts` (:8-14).
- Evidence panel: `EventSheet` (:262-524) fetches `useApi<MeetingDetail>("/meetings/:id")` (:271), renders attendees, transcript scope items with verbatim quotes (:353-385), proposal lines with `ProvenanceChip`s + policy reasons + Xero deep link (:389-455), and inline actions (attach transcript / skip) for AWAITING_TRANSCRIPT (:458-507). Note the `txt()` helper for openapi-typescript nullable strings (:34-38) — reuse it.

### Connections surface
- `connections-surface.tsx`: `useApi<Row[]>("/connections", { refreshMs: 60_000 })` (:27-30), stable XERO/CALENDAR/EMAIL order (:19), full loading/error/empty handling (:65-87).
- `connection-card.tsx`: status badge via `connectionStatusMeta` (:102), human lead line per kind+status (:43-69), "Check now" routes per kind — XERO→`POST /connections/xero/recheck`, CALENDAR→`POST /meetings/sync`, EMAIL→`POST /email/poll` (:105-147), calendar card embeds `IcsImport` (:235-239), collapsible verbatim backend detail (:242-264). This is the pattern for any new provider card.

### Tasks surface + router
- `tasks-surface.tsx`: `useApi<TaskDto[]>("/tasks", { refreshMs: 20_000 })` + `/dashboard/summary` (:23-27), refetch-both-after-resolve (:29-32).
- `task-router.tsx:13-28`: switch on `task.type` → `ProvideTranscriptCard | ConfirmClientCard | ReviewInvoiceCard | ConfirmAgreementCard | AttachContractCard` (one card component per `TaskType`, enums.ts:44-50). A new task type = new card file + a case here. Shared card plumbing in `_components/use-resolve.ts`, `context.ts`, `bits.tsx`.

### Sidebar / nav — adding a Settings route
- `web/lib/nav.ts:19-30`: `NAV_ITEMS` array (five surfaces, home `/`). **Add `{ label: "Settings", href: "/settings", icon: Settings, matchPrefix: true }` here** (import icon from lucide-react, :1-8) plus a `web/app/(dashboard)/settings/page.tsx`; `isActive` handles prefix matching (:32-37). Rendered by `web/components/app-sidebar.tsx:35-51` (open-task badge only on `/`, :39).

### UI primitives available
- `web/components/ui/`: badge, button, card, **dialog**, scroll-area, separator, **sheet**, skeleton, sonner (toasts), switch, tabs, textarea, tooltip. Shared: `PageHeader`, `LoadingState`/`ErrorState`/`EmptyState`, `StateBadge`, `ProvenanceChip`, `Money`, `RelativeTime`, `LeakStrip`, `SummaryStats`.
- **No chart library.** `web/package.json:12-30` — no recharts; deps are radix primitives, `framer-motion`, `lucide-react`, `next-themes`, `sonner`. Any dashboard charts either add recharts (new dep) or are hand-rolled SVG/CSS. CLAUDE.md: `impeccable` + `ui-ux-pro-max` skills mandatory for UI, `frontend-design` prohibited; Cursor owns `web/`.

---

## 3. Data available for dashboard charts + chat tools

### Dashboard endpoints — `api/src/modules/dashboard/`
- `GET /api/dashboard/summary` → `{openTasks, unbilledGbp, invoicesSentThisMonth, clientsCount}` (dashboard.service.ts:255-284; unbilledGbp = sum of `Client.unbilledExposureGbp`).
- `GET /api/dashboard/leak-strip` → `{monthLabel, recoverableGbp, breakdown:[{source,gbp,count}]}` across OPEN detections + IN_REVIEW proposals + unbilled meetings (:164-237; unbilled meeting states listed :49-54, per-meeting £ estimate from client rate :94-103).
- `GET /api/dashboard/calendar?from&to` → `CalendarEventDto[]` with `colorKey` derived purely from meeting state (:77-91, :113-161).
- `GET /api/dashboard/audit?limit` → newest-first `AuditEventDto[]` (:240-252). Controller decorations at `dashboard.controller.ts:18-61`.

### Queryable aggregates elsewhere
- Proposals list (`GET /api/proposals`) carries per-proposal `currency, subtotal, taxTotal, total, state, policyResult, lineCount, autoSent, xeroInvoiceNumber, xeroDeepLink` (`proposals/dto/proposal-response.dto.ts:16-67`) — good for invoice charts/chat.
- Detections (`GET /api/detections`) carry `type` (QUOTE_NOT_INVOICED / RETAINER_STOPPED / EXPENSE_NOT_RECHARGED), `valueGbp`, `evidence` jsonb, `state` (`entities/detection.entity.ts:9-31`, enums.ts:31-42).
- Tasks counts by type: `GET /api/tasks/counts` (`tasks.service.ts:60-72`).
- Meetings list with duration/state/client for utilisation charts (`meetings.service.ts:379-396`).

### Xero data: cached locally vs fetched live — `api/src/modules/xero/xero-api.ts`
- **Read surface available**: `listContacts` (:33-38), `listInvoices(statuses?, page)` (:188-196), `getInvoice`/`findInvoiceByReference` (:120-131), `listPayments` (:259-262), `listQuotes(status)` (:298-303), `listAccounts`/`defaultSalesAccountCode` (:316-332), `getHistory`, `listAttachments`, and **`agedReceivablesByContact(contactId)` (:336-340) — implemented but currently UNUSED by any module** (free win for a dashboard/chat tool).
- **Cached locally in Postgres**: `Client.xeroContactId` + `unbilledExposureGbp` (client.entity.ts), `InvoiceProposal.xeroInvoiceId/Number/DeepLink` (invoice-proposal.entity.ts:43-55), `Detection` rows persist evidence derived from Xero reads. There is **no local cache table of Xero invoices/payments** — detections fetch them live at run time with graceful fallback to local fixtures on failure (detections.service.ts:165-176 quotes, :205-211 invoices, :288-291 payments).
- All access via `XeroService` (`xero.service.ts:8-52` — thin re-export of xero-api helpers plus `health()`); transport/auth/429/tenant in `xero-http.ts` (429 Retry-After retry :312-317, cached tenant :161-178, health :339-355).

---

## 4. Conventions that MUST be followed (violations get reverted)

1. **DTO decoration**: every endpoint has `@ApiOperation` + `@ApiOkResponse`/`@ApiResponse`; every DTO field `@ApiProperty` (+ class-validator on inputs — global ValidationPipe at main.ts:15-21 whitelists). Example everywhere, e.g. meetings.controller.ts:32-37, proposal-response.dto.ts.
2. **Zod on every LLM output**: the LLM module pattern is `complete(system, user, zodSchema)` — parse JSON, `schema.parse`, retry once, then throw (never guess) (`llm.service.ts:41-73`, schemas in `llm/schemas.ts`). LLM parses/proposes at the edges only; **zero LLM calls inside engine decisions** (`engine/` is pure TS, unit-tested: matching/policy/proposal/state-machine `.spec.ts` files).
3. **AuditEvent on every write**: every verdict, approval, task raise/resolve, connection change, and Xero write records via `audit.record` (audit.service.ts:15-16 "No silent mutations anywhere").
4. **Idempotent Xero writes**: check-by-reference before create (`findInvoiceByReference` → `createInvoice` xero-api.ts:133-177; `ensureContact` :57-75; attachments overwrite-by-filename; stable `reference = ROBYN-<id8>` meetings.service.ts:264). Plus `idempotencyKey` headers and `assertElementOk` on `summarizeErrors=false` responses (:171-176). All Xero access through `api/src/modules/xero/` only.
5. **State-machine invariant**: "every transition either writes to Xero or raises a Task. No silent third path" (enums.ts:1-2; state-machine.ts:1-3). New behaviours must land as a state transition with a task or a Xero write, audited.
6. **OpenAPI regen discipline**: endpoint change ⇒ same commit includes regenerated `api/openapi.json` + `web/lib/api-types.ts` (`pnpm openapi`, root package.json:16). A frontend fetch with a hand-written type is a bug even if it works (CLAUDE.md). `web/lib/api-types.ts` is never hand-edited (api.ts:6-9).
7. Ownership: Claude Code owns `api/`, `seed/`, `.claude/skills/`; Cursor owns `web/`. OpenAPI contract is the only interface (CLAUDE.md).

---

## 5. Risk notes — do not trip over these

- **React Strict Mode double-fetch**: `web/next.config.mjs:3` `reactStrictMode: true` → dev double-fires effects, so `useApi` fetches show duplicate 200s in DevTools (HANDOFF.md "Known rough edges"). Harmless, but don't "fix" it by removing StrictMode or adding fetch dedupe hacks; the `activeRef` ticket in `useApi` (api.ts:154-180) already prevents state races.
- **TypeORM `synchronize: true`, no migrations** (app.module.ts:32). New entity/column = auto schema change on boot. Never rename columns casually (data loss); adding nullable columns is safe. Not production-grade — known and accepted for the demo.
- **Port assumptions**: web is hardcoded to :3000 (`web/package.json:6` `next dev -p 3000`), API defaults to :4000 (env.ts:63), and the web fetch base defaults to `http://localhost:4000/api` (api.ts:12-13, override via `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE`). The demo runbook and docs assume `http://localhost:3000` (HANDOFF.md "Run it"). CORS is fully open (`main.ts:12` `{ cors: true }`) so no origin lists to update, but keep the global `api` prefix (`main.ts:14`) in every new controller URL you hand to the frontend.
- **OpenAPI export instantiates the full module graph** (openapi.ts:14) — any new constructor/lifecycle side effect (cron, pollers, eager connects) MUST be gated on `isServing()` (`common/bootstrap-flag.ts`), or `pnpm openapi` will fire it. The existing calendar cron shows the pattern (meetings.controller.ts:114-118). Note `LlmService`'s constructor already requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at module build (llm.service.ts:28 via loadConfig) — the export needs a populated `api/.env`.
- **Xero rate limit (60/min)**: retries on 429 exist (xero-http.ts:312-317) but don't poll Xero in loops; batch reads and prefer local DB rows for dashboards (CLAUDE.md backend rules). Detections already model the "live read with local fallback" pattern (detections.service.ts:165-176).
- **`meetings/sync` cron every 15 min** re-runs Loop-1 on unprogressed meetings (meetings.controller.ts:114-118, runEligible meetings.service.ts:537-563) — anything you add to Loop-1 must stay idempotent (task dedupeKey, proposal reference) because it WILL re-run.
- **Fallback truthfulness**: CALENDAR/EMAIL FALLBACK states are honest demo modes, not errors (HANDOFF.md "Fallbacks"). Never render FALLBACK as broken, and never fake LIVE.
- Nullable strings from openapi-typescript arrive as `string | Record<string, never>` — use the `txt()` guard pattern (calendar-surface.tsx:34-38) in new UI.
