```md
# Calendar UI Porting Blueprint

Source ("DASH") = `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/devanshkaria_dev_dashboard`
Target ("WEB") = `/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/web` (Next 14.2.15, tailwind 3.4.17, shadcn, no RTK, no date-fns)

The "perfect" UI: mini month + calendar list in a left sidebar, week view as the main surface, 48px hour rows, tinted event chips with a saturated 3px left border, red now-line, sticky hour gutter, auto-scroll to the working hours.

---

## 1. Component tree

\```
app/(authenticated)/calendar/page.tsx        page: owns currentDate + all dialog state + data fetch
├─ CalendarSidebar                           desktop-only left rail (mini month + calendar list)
│   └─ CalendarSidebarContent                reused inside a mobile <Sheet> (page.tsx:170-183)
├─ CalendarView                              toolbar (Today, chevrons, title, view Select) + view switch
│   ├─ WeekView                              THE main surface (time grid, all-day row, now-line)
│   ├─ DayView                               single-day timeline (mobile default)
│   ├─ inline month grid                     CalendarView.tsx:222-301
│   └─ DayViewDialog                         day timeline in a dialog, opened from month cells (304-313)
├─ EventDialog                               create/edit form (react-hook-form + RTK mutations)
├─ DeleteEventDialog / ConnectCalendarDialog
\```

Responsibilities and citations:

- **Page** `DASH/app/(authenticated)/calendar/page.tsx` — single source of truth `currentDate` (:33); memoized fetch range (:38-41); events query (:43-46); loading spinner (:78-84, 145-148), empty "connect a calendar" state (:129-144); layout = header row (:88-127) then `flex` of sidebar + view (:150-167). All child navigation funnels into `setCurrentDate` (:154, 164, 179).
- **CalendarView** `DASH/components/calendar/CalendarView.tsx` — local `viewType` state, responsive default: `"day"` under 768px else `"week"` (:47-49). Toolbar: Today button (:151-158), joined prev/next chevron pair (`rounded-r-none` / `rounded-l-none border-l-0`, :159-176), title h2 (:179-181), shadcn `Select` for week/day/month (:184-202). Prev/next step size depends on viewType: ±1 month / ±7 days / ±1 day (:88-114). Title text via `getWeekTitle` (:137-145).
- **WeekView** `DASH/components/calendar/WeekView.tsx` — see §2.
- **CalendarSidebar** `DASH/components/calendar/CalendarSidebar.tsx` — see §3.
- **EventDialog** `DASH/components/calendar/EventDialog.tsx` — create/edit with separate DateTimePicker/ColorPicker state (:64-97); not needed in target (read-only calendar).
- **DayView** `DASH/components/calendar/DayView.tsx` — same positioning engine, `HOUR_HEIGHT = 80` (:41), solid-fill chips (:342-371).
- **Utils** `DASH/lib/calendar-utils.ts` — all date math, zero deps (pure `Date`).
- **Event shape** `DASH/interfaces/calendarInterface.ts:21-37`.

## 2. Week view mechanics (WeekView.tsx — port this file faithfully)

**Constants** `DASH/lib/calendar-utils.ts:3-4`: `HOUR_HEIGHT = 48`, `HOURS_IN_DAY = 24`. Day column min-height = 48 × 24 = **1152px** (WeekView.tsx:393).

**Shell** (:298): `flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden rounded-lg border bg-background`. Three stacked shrink-0 sections (header, all-day) + one scrolling section.

**Day header row** (:299-324): `flex` of a `w-10 md:w-14` empty gutter spacer (:300) + `grid flex-1 grid-cols-7`. Each cell: weekday `text-[11px] uppercase text-muted-foreground` over the date number in an `h-8 w-8 rounded-full text-lg font-semibold` circle. Today: header cell `bg-primary/5` (:307) and the circle `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` (:313-318). Row sits OUTSIDE the scroll container so it never scrolls away.

**All-day row** (:326-359): rendered only when spans exist (:326). Gutter shows "All day" `text-[10px]` (:328-330). Body is `relative grid grid-cols-7 min-h-[36px] py-1` with 7 empty bordered cells as background (:332-337) and absolutely positioned span buttons on top:
- `left: calc(startCol/7*100% + 2px)`, `width: calc(span/7*100% - 4px)` (:346-347)
- fill `hexToRgba(color, 0.25)` + `borderLeft: 3px solid color` (:348-349)
- `buildAllDaySpans` (:181-219) clamps event start/end to the week's 7 day-keys and returns `{startCol, span}`.

**Scroll container** (:361): ONE `relative min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto` div holds hour gutter + grid + now-line, so everything scrolls together. Hour gutter is `sticky left-0 z-20 w-10 md:w-14 bg-background` (:363) for horizontal-scroll safety. Auto-scroll effect (:242-251): `scrollTop = targetHour * HOUR_HEIGHT` where `targetHour = max(currentHour - 2, 6)` when the week contains today, else `8`; reruns on `currentDate` change.

**Hour labels** (:364-376): 24 gutter cells each `height: 48px`, label `absolute -top-2 right-1` so "9 AM" sits ON the grid line, hour 0 label omitted (:370). `formatHourLabel` gives `12 AM / 1 AM / 12 PM / 1 PM` (`calendar-utils.ts:75-80`).

**Time grid** (:379-415): `relative grid flex-1 grid-cols-7`. Per day column (`relative border-r`, today tint `bg-primary/[0.03]` :391):
1. 24 hour-slot `<button>`s, each `height: 48px`, `border-b hover:bg-accent/30`, `onClick → onSlotClick(day, hour)` with aria-label (:395-404). z-0.
2. An event overlay `pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-10` (:406-410); each chip re-enables `pointer-events-auto` (:265). This layering is load-bearing: slots stay hoverable/clickable between chips.

**Vertical position math** `calculateTimedEventPosition` (:42-74):
- `top = startMinutes/60 * HOUR_HEIGHT`; `height = max((endMinutes-startMinutes)/60 * HOUR_HEIGHT, 22)` (:67-71)
- Multi-day events clamp to 00:00 / 24:00 on non-terminal days via date-key comparison (:56-65).

**Overlap handling** `layoutTimedEvents` (:76-179) — classic interval clustering:
1. Sort positioned events by start (:97).
2. Build transitively-overlapping groups; an event overlapping several groups merges them (:99-130). Overlap test: `a.start < b.end && a.end > b.start` (:106-108).
3. Within a group, greedy first-fit column assignment (:146-162).
4. `left = colIndex * 100/totalColumns` %, `width = 100/totalColumns` % (:164-175). Singleton groups get full width (:134-144).
5. Rendered with 2px insets: `left: calc(X% + 2px)`, `width: calc(Y% - 4px)` (:271-272).

**Event chip** `renderEventBlock` (:253-295): `absolute overflow-hidden rounded-md px-1.5 py-1 border-l-[3px] hover:opacity-90` + focus ring; `backgroundColor: hexToRgba(color, 0.22)`, `borderLeftColor: color` (:273-274). Content density thresholds: title `text-xs font-semibold truncate text-foreground` always; time row `text-[11px] text-muted-foreground` only when `height > 28` (:282); location row with `MapPin` icon when `height > 44` (:287). Native `title` tooltip with title + time range (:277).

**Now-line** (:237-240, 418-428): shown iff the week contains today (:238). `top = (hours + minutes/60) * HOUR_HEIGHT` (:239-240). Rendered as the scroll container's last child: `pointer-events-none absolute left-0 right-0 z-10 flex items-center` — a `w-10 md:w-14` box right-aligning a `h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-red-500` dot, then `h-px flex-1 bg-red-500`. Spans all 7 columns (not just today).

## 3. Mini month (CalendarSidebar.tsx)

- Rail: `aside hidden lg:flex w-64 shrink-0 flex-col border-r bg-muted/20 overflow-y-auto` (:158-164). Same content re-rendered inside a left `Sheet` for <lg (page.tsx:170-183) — export the content component separately for this (:32).
- **Week building**: no libraries. Leading `null`s for `firstDay.getDay()` offset, then one `Date` per day (:40-54); rendered as `grid grid-cols-7 gap-0.5` of `h-8` cells, `null` → empty div (:108-133). Single-letter day header row `S M T W T F S` `text-[10px] text-muted-foreground` (:97-106).
- **Header**: "July 2026" + two `ghost h-7 w-7` chevrons calling `onDateChange(new Date(year, month±1, 1))` (:66-68, 77-94). The mini month's displayed month is DERIVED from `currentDate` (:37-38), so paging the mini month moves the main week view too — one state, no drift.
- **Styling**: every day cell is a `rounded-full h-8 text-xs font-medium hover:bg-accent` button (:117-131). Current week (computed as the 7 date-keys of `currentDate`'s week, :56-64) gets `bg-accent/60` (:124) — this is the "selected range" affordance; today gets `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` (:125-126), which wins over the week tint.
- **Click-to-navigate**: `onClick={() => onDateChange(date)}` (:120) → page `setCurrentDate` → WeekView re-derives `getWeekDays(currentDate)` (WeekView.tsx:229) → main surface jumps to that week. No extra "selected day" state anywhere.
- Below the month: "My Calendars" list with `h-3 w-3 rounded-sm` colour swatches (:136-152). In the target replace this block with the billing legend (`WEB/components/calendar/calendar-legend.tsx` already exists).

## 4. Navigation state + data-fetch contract

- **State**: exactly one `currentDate: Date` at the page (page.tsx:33). Sidebar, toolbar, and views receive it + `onDateChange`.
- **View switch**: `viewType` local to CalendarView (:47-49), shadcn Select (:184-202). Prev/next (:88-114): month → ±1 month, week → ±7 days, day → ±1 day. Today: `onDateChange(new Date())` (:116-118).
- **Title**: `getWeekTitle` collapses same-month ranges: "July 6 – 12, 2026" vs "June 29 – July 5, 2026" (`calendar-utils.ts:117-141`).
- **Week math**: `getWeekStart` = subtract `getDay()` → **Sunday start** (`calendar-utils.ts:13-18`); `getWeekDays` = 7 consecutive Dates (:20-27).
- **Fetch range**: `getWeekDateRange(date, bufferDays = 14)` returns the week ±14 days as `YYYY-MM-DD` strings (`calendar-utils.ts:29-44`). Page memoizes it (page.tsx:38-41) and calls `useGetCalendarEventsQuery({startDate, endDate})` (:43-46) → RTK Query `GET /api/v1/calendar-events?startDate=&endDate=` (`DASH/managers/apiManager.ts:822-833`). The ±2-week buffer means adjacent-week paging usually hits cache; every view then filters the same `events` array client-side.
- **Expected event shape** (`DASH/interfaces/calendarInterface.ts:21-37`): `{ id, calendarProviderId, title, description, startTime: ISO, endTime: ISO, location, attendees, isAllDay, status, eventColor: string|null }`. Colour resolution: `event.eventColor || calendar.calendarColor || "#3b82f6"` (`calendar-utils.ts:54-66`).

## 5. Styling system

- **Tokens only**: the entire surface uses shadcn semantic classes — `bg-background`, `bg-muted/30|/40|/20`, `border`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-primary`/`text-primary-foreground`, `hover:bg-accent/30` — zero `dark:` variants needed; dark mode falls out of next-themes + CSS variables (dashboard uses `next-themes ^0.4.6`, `DASH/package.json`). Target already runs `darkMode: ["class"]` (`WEB/tailwind.config.ts:4`) with the same token set, so classes transfer 1:1.
- **The two non-token colours**: event hex colours from data, converted to a translucent fill via `hexToRgba(color, 0.22)` (`calendar-utils.ts:143-153`) so one hex works on both themes; and the `bg-red-500` now-line.
- **Chip anatomy that makes it read "Google Calendar"**: soft tinted fill + saturated `border-l-[3px]` + `text-foreground` (NOT white-on-solid) at week scale (WeekView.tsx:264-274); solid fills only at day scale (DayView.tsx:342-351).
- **Density numbers**: 48px hours, 36px all-day row, `w-10 md:w-14` gutter, `text-xs` titles, `text-[11px]` secondary, `text-[10px]/[11px]` hour labels, 2px chip insets, `h-8` header date circles, `min-h 22px` chips with progressive disclosure thresholds (28/44px).
- **Date libs**: `date-fns ^4.1.0` and `react-day-picker` are in `DASH/package.json` but the week surface uses NEITHER — only `DayViewDialog.tsx:13` imports date-fns. All week math is hand-rolled pure `Date`.
- **Known source quirk — do not copy**: `DayView.tsx:45-49` keys days via `toISOString().split("T")[0]` (UTC — wrong day for London BST evenings) while WeekView uses local `toDateKey` (`calendar-utils.ts:6-11`). The target must bucket in Europe/London consistently.

## 6. Port plan → WEB (Next 14 App Router, tailwind 3, shadcn)

**Target facts**
- Page exists: `WEB/app/(dashboard)/calendar/page.tsx` (server, renders `CalendarSurface`). Current surface `WEB/app/(dashboard)/calendar/_components/calendar-surface.tsx` (524 lines) is a month grid + an `EventSheet` evidence panel (:239-241, 262-522) that fetches `MeetingDetailDto` and has approve/skip actions (:473, :491). **Keep the sheet, replace the grid.**
- Event DTO: `Schemas["CalendarEventDto"] = { id, title, start, end, state: MeetingState, colorKey, clientName, proposalId }` (`WEB/lib/api-types.ts:1560-1589`). Endpoint: `GET /dashboard/calendar?from&to`, ISO bounds on event START (`WEB/lib/api-types.ts:2330-2352`). No `isAllDay`, no `location`.
- Billing colours: `colorKeyToTone` (`WEB/lib/states.ts:64-79`), `TONE_CLASS` (:25-43), `TONE_DOT` (:46-56), `meetingStateMeta` (:82-103). teal=billed/PAID-side, amber=proposal, amber-outline(dashed)=awaiting, violet=unknown, slate=skipped.
- Fetching: `useApi<T>(path)` native-fetch hook (`WEB/lib/api.ts:146-175`). Timezone: existing surface formats via `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `Europe/London` (`calendar-surface.tsx:40-56`) — keep that.

**Copy nearly verbatim**
1. `DASH/lib/calendar-utils.ts` → `WEB/lib/calendar-utils.ts`. Keep `HOUR_HEIGHT`, `HOURS_IN_DAY`, `toDateKey`, `getWeekStart/getWeekDays/getWeekDateRange`, `isToday`, `formatHourLabel`, `getWeekTitle`. Drop `getCalendarColor`/`getEventColor`/`hexToRgba` (tones replace them). Change `getWeekStart` to Monday for a UK product: `start.setDate(start.getDate() - ((start.getDay() + 6) % 7))` (source is Sunday-start, :13-18; target's existing surface is already Mon-first, `calendar-surface.tsx:41`). Make `toDateKey` use the London `Intl` formatter (`calendar-surface.tsx:44-49`) instead of local getters.
2. `DASH/components/calendar/WeekView.tsx` → `WEB/app/(dashboard)/calendar/_components/week-view.tsx`. The whole positioning/overlap engine (:42-219) ports unchanged. Render changes: chip colours (below); drop the all-day row initially (DTO has no `isAllDay`; re-add later if the API grows one); drop `onSlotClick` slot buttons' create semantics — keep the hour cells as plain divs or use the click to open nothing (Robyn is read-only). Keep the `pointer-events-none` overlay + z-index layering, 22px min height, 28/44px disclosure thresholds, sticky gutter, auto-scroll effect, now-line.
3. `DASH/components/calendar/CalendarSidebar.tsx` (:32-156) → `WEB/app/(dashboard)/calendar/_components/mini-month.tsx`. Swap the "My Calendars" list for `CalendarLegend`. 60 lines of hand-rolled month math — do NOT add react-day-picker.

**Rewrite**
4. New `calendar-surface.tsx`: client component owning `currentDate` + `viewType` ("week" | "day", default day under 768px like `CalendarView.tsx:47-49`; skip month view for the demo). Toolbar copied from `CalendarView.tsx:149-204` but the view `Select` becomes a `Tabs` or two-button segment — **`WEB/components/ui/` has no `select.tsx`** (only badge/button/card/dialog/scroll-area/separator/sheet/skeleton/sonner/switch/tabs/textarea/tooltip).
5. Fetch: `const { startDate, endDate } = getWeekDateRange(currentDate)` then `useApi<CalendarEvent[]>(`/dashboard/calendar?from=${startISO}&to=${endISO}`)` — convert the day keys to ISO datetimes (`T00:00:00Z` framing) since the endpoint filters on `start` (`api-types.ts:2330-2340`). Wire loading/empty/error to the existing `LoadingState`/`EmptyState`/`ErrorState` components (already imported in `calendar-surface.tsx:27-29`) — CLAUDE.md demands all three states.
6. **Event adapter** (the only data-shape work):
   \```ts
   // CalendarEventDto -> WeekView model
   { id, title, startTime: e.start, endTime: e.end,
     subtitle: txt(e.clientName),          // replaces `location` line, reuse txt() from calendar-surface.tsx:36-38
     tone: colorKeyToTone(e.colorKey) }    // WEB/lib/states.ts:64
   \```
   Chip styling swaps the inline hex styles (`WeekView.tsx:273-274`) for tone classes: `cn("border-l-[3px]", TONE_CLASS[tone])` — `TONE_CLASS` already encodes tinted-bg + readable-fg + border for light AND dark (`states.ts:25-43`), including the dashed treatment for `awaiting` (amber-outline). This is how PAID/teal vs UNBILLED/amber colours the blocks.
7. **Evidence panel on click**: chip `onClick → setSelectedId(event.id)`; extract the existing `EventSheet` (`calendar-surface.tsx:262-522`) into `_components/event-sheet.tsx` unchanged (it already fetches `/meetings/:id` detail, shows `StateBadge`, proposal link, approve/skip). This satisfies "open an evidence panel on click" with zero new code.
8. Mini-month week highlight: keep `bg-accent/60` for the visible week and primary circle for today (`CalendarSidebar.tsx:124-126`); clicking a day sets `currentDate` (:120).

**Deps to add: none.** No date-fns (week surface never used it), no react-day-picker; `lucide-react` already in `WEB/package.json`. Everything else is shadcn primitives the target already has.

**Do not port**: `EventDialog`, `DeleteEventDialog`, `ConnectCalendarDialog`, `DayViewDialog` (drag/resize creation UX, `DayViewDialog.tsx:31-59` — out of scope for a read-only billing calendar), RTK Query wiring.

```

You are building the Google OAuth + calendar/gmail sync backend module for the Robyn app at /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack (NestJS 10 + TypeORM + Postgres, single-user hackathon demo, no user auth).

READ FIRST, in order: (1) /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/CLAUDE.md, (2) /Users/devansh/.claude/jobs/148140d1/tmp/study/robyn-current.md (integration map of this codebase), (3) /Users/devansh/.claude/jobs/148140d1/tmp/study/oauth-backend.md (porting blueprint mined from the working myMan implementation — follow its port plan §6). The reference source itself is at /Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core/src/modules/calendar-providers/ if you need to consult actual code.

IMPORTANT PRE-CHECK: HANDOFF.md says some Google calendar OAuth already exists "gated on a token" — read api/src/modules/meetings/ and api/package.json first. If a partial google path exists, absorb/replace it cleanly through your new module rather than duplicating. Check whether `googleapis` is already a dependency; if not, note it in your final message (do NOT run pnpm add yourself).

BUILD api/src/modules/google/ with:
1. Entity `GoogleConnection` (api/src/entities/google-connection.entity.ts, follow existing entity conventions): single-row — accountEmail, accessToken, refreshToken, tokenExpiresAt, grantedScopes (text), calendarSyncToken (nullable), lastSyncAt, syncStatus, syncError. Never log token values.
2. Endpoints (decorated DTOs, class-validator, @ApiOperation/@ApiResponse — the openapi contract is generated from these):
   - GET /google/auth-url → {url}: scopes = calendar.readonly, gmail.readonly, userinfo.email; access_type=offline, prompt=consent, include_granted_scopes=true; state = random nonce (persist it, e.g. in-memory or on the row).
   - GET /google/callback?code&state → exchanges code, persists tokens + grantedScopes (from token response `scope` field) + accountEmail (oauth2 userinfo or id_token), then IMMEDIATELY triggers a first sync, and returns a small JSON {connected:true, accountEmail, grantedScopes}. Idempotent: re-hitting with a used/unknown state while a connection exists returns success, not an error. (Blueprint gotcha: the reference's guarded GET callback 401'd — ours has no guards, keep it plain.)
   - GET /google/status → {connected, accountEmail, grantedScopes, lastSyncAt, syncStatus, calendar:{granted}, gmail:{granted}}.
   - POST /google/sync → runs calendar + gmail sync now, returns counts.
   - DELETE /google/connection → best-effort token revoke, delete row, flip CALENDAR/EMAIL ConnectionState rows back to FALLBACK.
3. Redirect URI: read from env GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI — add it as an opt() to api/src/config/env.ts google block (you OWN env.ts for this wave; default 'http://localhost:3000/api/v1/calendar-providers/google/callback'). That URI is already registered in Google Console; the Next.js app will mirror the path and forward to this API (not your problem this wave).
4. Token refresh: 5-min-early expiry check before every Google call; on refresh persist new expiry; on refresh failure set syncStatus=ERROR + syncError, flip ConnectionState to DOWN with the reason.
5. Calendar sync (blueprint §2/§4): events.list calendarId=primary, singleEvents, orderBy startTime, window past 14d → future 30d, maxResults 250; use syncToken incremental with 410-fallback-to-full. Map events into the EXISTING meetings pipeline — read how meetings/import-ics upserts Meetings and reuse that upsert path/service so calendar events become Meeting rows identically (source marker e.g. 'google'). Cancelled events → soft-handling consistent with existing meeting states. Then upsert ConnectionState CALENDAR → LIVE with accountEmail detail.
6. Gmail (only when gmail.readonly is in grantedScopes — per-scope graceful degradation): poll INBOX for messages FROM addresses in the potential-clients queue ONLY (this invariant is sacred — read email module first; reuse its queued-sender filter and its downstream agreement-detection path). Feed matching messages through the same processing the fixture mailbox uses. Upsert ConnectionState EMAIL → LIVE. If scope not granted, leave EMAIL untouched (fixture fallback stays honest).
7. Cron: 15-min sync (idempotent; follow the existing cron pattern in meetings; gate with isServing() — the openapi export builds the full module graph and must not fire syncs).
8. AuditEvent rows via the existing audit.service pattern for: connected, disconnected, sync completed (with counts), sync failed.

HARD RULES: do NOT edit api/src/app.module.ts, api/src/entities/index.ts, api/openapi.json, or anything under web/ — return registration instructions instead. Do not edit dashboard/chat/xero modules (other agents own them this wave; email module + env.ts are YOURS). Do not start/stop servers or run pnpm install. Compile-check with `cd api && npx tsc --noEmit`. Follow existing code style (look at meetings module as the template). Secrets never logged.

FINAL MESSAGE: list of files created/edited; exact registration lines needed (module import for app.module.ts, entity exports for entities/index.ts); whether googleapis needs installing; any deviations from the blueprint with reasons; remaining risks.

96	- **Read surface available**: `listContacts` (:33-38), `listInvoices(statuses?, page)` (:188-196), `getInvoice`/`findInvoiceByReference` (:120-131), `listPayments` (:259-262), `listQuotes(status)` (:298-303), `listAccounts`/`defaultSalesAccountCode` (:316-332), `getHistory`, `listAttachments`, and **`agedReceivablesByContact(contactId)` (:336-340) — implemented but currently UNUSED by any module** (free win for a dashboard/chat tool).

There is **no local cache table of Xero invoices/payments** — detections fetch them live at run time with graceful fallback to local fixtures on failure (detections.service.ts:165-176 quotes, :205-211 invoices, :288-291 payments).
98	- All access via `XeroService` (`xero.service.ts:8-52` — thin re-export of xero-api helpers plus `health()`); transport/auth/429/tenant in `xero-http.ts` (429 Retry-After retry :312-317, cached tenant :161-178, health :339-355).

100	---
101	
102	## 4. Conventions that MUST be followed (violations get reverted)
103	
104	1. **DTO decoration**: every endpoint has `@ApiOperation` + `@ApiOkResponse`/`@ApiResponse`; every DTO field `@ApiProperty` (+ class-validator on inputs — global ValidationPipe at main.ts:15-21 whitelists). Example everywhere, e.g. meetings.controller.ts:32-37, proposal-response.dto.ts.
105	2. **Zod on every LLM output**: the LLM module pattern is `complete(system, user, zodSchema)` — parse JSON, `schema.parse`, retry once, then throw (never guess) (`llm.service.ts:41-73`, schemas in `llm/schemas.ts`). LLM parses/proposes at the edges only; **zero LLM calls inside engine decisions** (`engine/` is pure TS, unit-tested: matching/policy/proposal/state-machine `.spec.ts` files).
106	3. **AuditEvent on every write**: every verdict, approval, task raise/resolve, connection change, and Xero write records via `audit.record` (audit.service.ts:15-16 "No silent mutations anywhere").
107	4. **Idempotent Xero writes**: check-by-reference before create (`findInvoiceByReference` → `createInvoice` xero-api.ts:133-177; `ensureContact` :57-75; attachments overwrite-by-filename; stable `reference = ROBYN-<id8>` meetings.service.ts:264). Plus `idempotencyKey` headers and `assertElementOk` on `summarizeErrors=false` responses (:171-176). All Xero access through `api/src/modules/xero/` only.
108	5. **State-machine invariant**: "every transition either writes to Xero or raises a Task. No silent third path" (enums.ts:1-2; state-machine.ts:1-3). New behaviours must land as a state transition with a task or a Xero write, audited.
109	6. **OpenAPI regen discipline**: endpoint change ⇒ same commit includes regenerated `api/openapi.json` + `web/lib/api-types.ts` (`pnpm openapi`, root package.json:16). A frontend fetch with a hand-written type is a bug even if it works (CLAUDE.md). `web/lib/api-types.ts` is never hand-edited (api.ts:6-9).
110	7. Ownership: Claude Code owns `api/`, `seed/`, `.claude/skills/`; Cursor owns `web/`. OpenAPI contract is the only interface (CLAUDE.md).
111	
112	---
113	
114	## 5. Risk notes — do not trip over these
115	
116	- **React Strict Mode double-fetch**: `web/next.config.mjs:3` `reactStrictMode: true` → dev double-fires effects, so `useApi` fetches show duplicate 200s in DevTools (HANDOFF.md "Known rough edges"). Harmless, but don't "fix" it by removing StrictMode or adding fetch dedupe hacks; the `activeRef` ticket in `useApi` (api.ts:154-180) already prevents state races.
117	- **TypeORM `synchronize: true`, no migrations** (app.module.ts:32). New entity/column = auto schema change on boot. Never rename columns casually (data loss); adding nullable columns is safe. Not production-grade — known and accepted for the demo.
118	- **Port assumptions**: web is hardcoded to :3000 (`web/package.json:6` `next dev -p 3000`), API defaults to :4000 (env.ts:63), and the web fetch base defaults to `http://localhost:4000/api` (api.ts:12-13, override via `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE`). The demo runbook and docs assume `http://localhost:3000` (HANDOFF.md "Run it"). CORS is fully open (`main.ts:12` `{ cors: true }`) so no origin lists to update, but keep the global `api` prefix (`main.ts:14`) in every new controller URL you hand to the frontend.
119	- **OpenAPI export instantiates the full module graph** (openapi.ts:14) — any new constructor/lifecycle side effect (cron, pollers, eager connects) MUST be gated on `isServing()` (`common/bootstrap-flag.ts`), or `pnpm openapi` will fire it. The existing calendar cron shows the pattern (meetings.controller.ts:114-118). Note `LlmService`'s constructor already requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at module build (llm.service.ts:28 via loadConfig) — the export needs a populated `api/.env`.
120	- **Xero rate limit (60/min)**: retries on 429 exist (xero-http.ts:312-317) but don't poll Xero in loops; batch reads and prefer local DB rows for dashboards (CLAUDE.md backend rules). Detections already model the "live read with local fallback" pattern (detections.service.ts:165-176).
121	- **`meetings/sync` cron every 15 min** re-runs Loop-1 on unprogressed meetings (meetings.controller.ts:114-118, runEligible meetings.service.ts:537-563) — anything you add to Loop-1 must stay idempotent (task dedupeKey, proposal reference) because it WILL re-run.
122	- **Fallback truthfulness**: CALENDAR/EMAIL FALLBACK states are honest demo modes, not errors (HANDOFF.md "Fallbacks"). Never render FALLBACK as broken, and never fake LIVE.
123	- Nullable strings from openapi-typescript arrive as `string | Record<string, never>` — use the `txt()` guard pattern (calendar-surface.tsx:34-38) in new UI.
124
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-05 00:21:50Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/devansh/.claude/jobs/148140d1/tmp/study/oauth-backend.md`

```text
1	# Porting blueprint: Google OAuth + Calendar sync (from myMan_backend_core)
2	
3	Reference root: `/Users/devansh/devanshkaria_dev_ecosystem/myMan_backend_core` (abbrev `REF`). All citations `file:line` relative to `REF`.
4	Stack of reference: NestJS 11, TypeORM 0.3.27, `googleapis@^164.0.0`, `@nestjs/schedule@^6.0.1` (`package.json:49,54,64,80`).
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. The exact Google OAuth dance
9	
10	### Client construction (shared pattern)
11	- `new google.auth.OAuth2(clientId, clientSecret, redirectUri)` built once in the service constructor from `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` / `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` config — `src/modules/calendar-providers/calendar-providers.service.ts:55-67`.
12	- Redirect URI is NOT a backend route. It is `${frontendUrl}/calendar/callback` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:40-44`, with frontend URL chosen by `NODE_ENV` from `FRONTEND_URL_PROD` / `FRONTEND_URL_DEV` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:46-53`). See gotcha #1.
13	
14	### Auth-URL generation
15	`calendar-providers.service.ts:93-101`:
16	```ts
17	this.googleOAuth2Client.generateAuthUrl({
18	  access_type: 'offline',                       // required to get refresh_token
19	  scope: ['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly',
20	          'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events'],
21	  prompt: 'consent',                            // forces refresh_token on re-consent
22	  state: JSON.stringify({ provider: 'google', timestamp: Date.now() }),
23	});
24	```
25	Returned as `{ authUrl }` (`dto/oauth-url-response.dto.ts:3-10`).
26	
27	### Callback / code exchange
28	There is no backend GET callback (deliberately removed, see gotcha #1). Frontend lands on `/calendar/callback?code=...&state=...`, then POSTs the code back to an authenticated endpoint. Exchange:
29	- `const { tokens } = await this.googleOAuth2Client.getToken(code); this.googleOAuth2Client.setCredentials(tokens);` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:133-134`.
30	- Then `calendar.calendarList.list()` to find the `primary` calendar (`calendar-providers.service.ts:137-139`), and a `CalendarProvider` row is created with `accessToken: tokens.access_token`, `refreshToken: tokens.refresh_token`, `tokenExpiresAt: new Date(tokens.expiry_date)` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:145-158`.
31	- On save, an immediate background sync is fired: `this.calendarSyncService.syncProvider(savedProvider.id).catch(...)` — `calendar-providers.service.ts:161-165`.
32	- Two-step variant: `listGoogleCalendars(code)` exchanges the code ONCE and returns `{ calendars, tokens }` to the frontend (`calendar-providers.service.ts:224-253`); `connectSpecificGoogleCalendars(tokens, calendarIds)` then reuses those tokens without a second exchange (`calendar-providers.service.ts:296-358`). Important: an auth code is single-use; that's why tokens are round-tripped.
33	
34	### Token storage (entity)
35	`src/modules/calendar-providers/entities/calendar-provider.entity.ts`:
36	- `accessToken: text` (line 37-38), `refreshToken: text nullable` (40-41), `tokenExpiresAt: timestamp nullable` (43-44)
37	- `providerType` enum google|microsoft (28-32), `providerAccountId` = Google calendarId (34-35)
38	- `syncStatus` enum active|syncing|error|inactive (61-66), `syncError: text` (68-69), `lastSyncedAt` (58-59)
39	- `syncToken: varchar(500) nullable` for incremental sync (71-72), `isActive` (55-56), `isDeleted` soft delete (83-84)
40	
41	### Refresh logic
42	`src/modules/calendar-sync/calendar-sync.service.ts:376-401`:
43	- Trigger: before each sync, `isTokenExpired(provider.tokenExpiresAt)` with a 5-minute early buffer (`calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`); if expired → `refreshGoogleToken(provider)` then reload the row (`calendar-sync.service.ts:101-109`).
44	- Mechanics: fresh `new google.auth.OAuth2(clientId, clientSecret)` (no redirect URI needed), `setCredentials({ refresh_token })`, `await oauth2Client.refreshAccessToken()`, then persist `accessToken` + `tokenExpiresAt = new Date(credentials.expiry_date)` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:379-394`). Refresh token itself is not rotated/rewritten.
45	- Failure handling: log + rethrow (`calendar-sync.service.ts:397-400`); the caller marks the provider `syncStatus: ERROR, syncError: error.message` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:65-71`). No automatic re-consent; user must reconnect.
46	
47	### Disconnect
48	Soft delete only — `isDeleted = true, isActive = false`, tokens left in row; no Google token revocation call (`calendar-providers.service.ts:494-506`). Controller: `DELETE /calendar-providers/:id`, 204 (`calendar-providers.controller.ts:146-153`).
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 2. googleapis usage details
53	
54	- Client per sync run: `google.calendar({ version: 'v3', auth: oauth2Client })` with `setCredentials({ access_token, refresh_token })` — `calendar-sync.service.ts:111-121`.
55	- `calendar.events.list` params — `calendar-sync.service.ts:124-130`:
56	  ```ts
57	  { calendarId: provider.providerAccountId, maxResults: 250,
58	    singleEvents: true, orderBy: 'startTime', timeMin: new Date().toISOString() }
59	  ```
60	  (future events only; `singleEvents: true` expands recurrences; no `timeMax` in reference).
61	- Incremental sync: if `provider.syncToken` exists, set `syncParams.syncToken` and DELETE `timeMin` — they are mutually exclusive (`calendar-sync.service.ts:132-136`). After sync, persist `response.data.nextSyncToken` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:158-163`). On HTTP 410 / "Sync token" error: null out syncToken and recurse into a full sync (`calendar-sync.service.ts:168-179`).
62	- Pagination: NOT implemented in the reference — single page, `maxResults: 250`, `nextPageToken` ignored (`calendar-sync.service.ts:141-142`). Fine for a demo; loop on `pageToken = response.data.nextPageToken` if you need >250.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## 3. Controller surface
67	
68	`src/modules/calendar-providers/calendar-providers.controller.ts`:
69	- Class-level `@UseGuards(FirebaseAuthGuard)` + `@ApiBearerAuth()` on `@Controller('calendar-providers')` (lines 32-36). Global prefix makes it `/api/v1/calendar-providers`.
70	- Routes: `GET google/auth-url` (41-46), `POST google/connect` body `{code}` (55-62), `POST google/list-calendars` (75-83), `POST google/connect-multiple` body `{tokens, calendarIds}` (95-105), `GET /` (119-124), `GET :id` (126-132), `PATCH :id` (134-144), `DELETE :id` 204 (146-153), `POST :id/sync` (155-163). Microsoft mirrors omitted here.
71	- DTOs: `ConnectGoogleCalendarDto { code: string }` with `@IsString @IsNotEmpty` (`dto/connect-google-calendar.dto.ts`); `ConnectWithTokensDto { tokens: object, calendarIds?: string[] }` (`dto/connect-with-tokens.dto.ts`).
72	- State param does NOT carry user identity — it is only `{ provider, timestamp }` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:100`). Identity comes from the Firebase bearer token on the POST that carries the code; the reference is effectively single-user (`CALENDAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md:210` "Single User System"). For our single-user demo, same shape works with no guard.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 4. Sync strategy
77	
78	- Triggers: (a) immediately after connect, fire-and-forget (`calendar-providers.service.ts:161-165`); (b) manual `POST :id/sync` → `triggerSync` sets `syncStatus=SYNCING` then awaits `syncProvider` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:508-533`); (c) cron every 15 min via `@Cron('*/15 * * * *')` → `syncAllProviders()` — `src/modules/calendar-sync/calendar-sync.scheduler.ts:14-26`, registered with `ScheduleModule.forRoot()` in `calendar-sync.module.ts:12`.
79	- Circular dep note: providers module and sync module reference each other; broken with `forwardRef` + a string-token provider `'CalendarSyncService'` (`calendar-sync.module.ts:17-20`, `calendar-providers.service.ts:33-34`, `calendar-providers.module.ts:13`). For the port: avoid entirely by putting sync + oauth in one module.
80	- Upsert key: `(calendarProviderId, externalEventId)` — lookup at `calendar-sync.service.ts:251-256`; migration adds a unique constraint on that pair (`CALENDAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md:239`).
81	- Update detection: skip write when `existingEvent.externalUpdatedAt === event.updated` (Google's `updated` timestamp stored as string) — `calendar-sync.service.ts:297-302`; entity field `externalUpdatedAt: varchar(100)` (`calendar-event.entity.ts:69-70`).
82	- Cancellations: `event.status === 'cancelled'` → soft delete (`isDeleted: true, status: CANCELLED`) — `calendar-sync.service.ts:258-270`. All-day detection: `!!event.start.date` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:289`); start/end use `dateTime || date` (285-286).
83	- Full-sync reconciliation: after a non-syncToken sync, local future events whose externalEventId wasn't returned get soft-deleted (`removeStaleLocalEvents`, `calendar-sync.service.ts:466-489`, called at 154-156).
84	- Event entity fields worth copying: `externalEventId varchar(500)`, `title`, `description`, `startTime/endTime timestamp`, `location`, `attendees jsonb`, `isAllDay`, `status enum`, `rawData jsonb`, `externalUpdatedAt`, `isDeleted` — `calendar-event.entity.ts:32-98`.
85	- Read API: `GET /calendar-events` filters by providerId/status/date-range with TypeORM `Between/MoreThanOrEqual/LessThanOrEqual` on `startTime` — `src/modules/calendar-events/calendar-events.service.ts:25-51`.
86	
87	---
88	
89	## 5. Gotchas (from OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md / CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md)
90	
91	1. **Backend GET callback + auth guard = 401.** Google redirects the browser with no bearer token; a guarded backend callback endpoint 401s. Fix used: redirect to a FRONTEND page, which extracts `code` and POSTs it to an authenticated endpoint (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:3-8,13-22`). For our demo (no auth), a plain unguarded backend `GET /callback` is fine — simpler than the reference.
92	2. **Redirect URI must match Google Cloud Console EXACTLY** — every URI used must be registered (dev + prod separately) (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:100-118`). `redirect_uri_mismatch` is the classic failure.
93	3. **Auth codes are single-use.** The list-then-connect flow exchanges once and hands `tokens` back to the client for the second call (`calendar-providers.service.ts:246-249`) — never call `getToken(code)` twice.
94	4. **`prompt: 'consent'` + `access_type: 'offline'` or no refresh_token.** Google only returns `refresh_token` on first consent unless you force the consent screen (`calendar-providers.service.ts:94,99`). `tokens.refresh_token` can still be null — entity column is nullable (`calendar-provider.entity.ts:40-41`).
95	5. **`syncToken` and `timeMin` are mutually exclusive**; and a 410 means the token expired → clear and full-resync (`calendar-sync.service.ts:132-136,168-179`).
96	6. **Refresh 5 min early** to avoid mid-request expiry races (`calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`); after refreshing, RELOAD the entity so the sync uses the new access token (`calendar-sync.service.ts:104-108`).
97	7. **Sync failures must land in `syncStatus`/`syncError`** or debugging is blind — the whole CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md workflow is "check syncStatus, check syncError, resync per provider" (`CALENDAR_SYNC_DEBUG.md:13-18,43-46`).
98	8. **Scopes changed mid-project** (readonly → events) requiring re-consent (`OAUTH_FIX_SUMMARY.md:33-38`) — pick final scopes before first connect or you'll reconnect.
99	
100	---
101	
102	## 6. Port plan for the target app (NestJS 10, TypeORM, single-user demo)
103	
104	One module, e.g. `api/src/modules/google/`. No guards, no multi-user, backend-callback flow (valid here because nothing is guarded — gotcha #1 does not apply). `npm i googleapis`.
105	
106	### Entity (single row, `google_connection`)
107	```ts
108	id uuid PK; accessToken text; refreshToken text nullable;
109	tokenExpiresAt timestamptz nullable; grantedScopes text;      // space-separated, from token response
110	calendarSyncToken varchar(500) nullable; lastSyncedAt timestamptz nullable;
111	syncStatus varchar; syncError text nullable;
112	```
113	(Trimmed from `calendar-provider.entity.ts`; drop providerType/isPrimary/color. `grantedScopes` is new — see degradation below.) Plus a `google_event` table if events must persist: copy `externalEventId` + unique `(connectionId, externalEventId)` upsert key per section 4.
114	
115	### Endpoints (mirror reference shapes, citations = pattern source)
116	- `GET /google/auth-url` → `generateAuthUrl({ access_type:'offline', prompt:'consent', state: <random nonce>, scope: [calendar.readonly, gmail.readonly] })` — pattern `calendar-providers.service.ts:93-101`. Persist the nonce; single user means state is CSRF-only.
117	- `GET /google/callback?code&state` (registered as the redirect URI): verify state, `oauth2Client.getToken(code)` (`calendar-providers.service.ts:133`), upsert the single row storing `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `new Date(tokens.expiry_date)`, **and `tokens.scope`** into `grantedScopes`; fire background sync (`calendar-providers.service.ts:145-165`); redirect (302) to the web app's settings page.
118	- `POST /google/sync` → refresh-if-needed (5-min buffer, `calendar-sync.service.ts:443-448`) then `events.list` with `timeMin`/`singleEvents:true`/`orderBy:'startTime'` or `syncToken` (`calendar-sync.service.ts:124-141`), upsert per section 4, save `nextSyncToken`, handle 410 (`calendar-sync.service.ts:168-179`).
119	- `DELETE /google/disconnect` → soft-clear the row (pattern `calendar-providers.service.ts:494-506`); optionally also `oauth2Client.revokeToken(accessToken)` (improvement over reference).
120	- Skip the cron initially; the reference's 15-min `@Cron` (`calendar-sync.scheduler.ts:14`) is a 10-line add later.
121	
122	### Gmail readonly in the same consent + per-scope degradation
123	- Add `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly` to the same `scope` array in `generateAuthUrl` — one consent screen, one token pair.
124	- Google users can UNCHECK individual scopes; the token response's `scope` field (space-separated) lists what was actually granted. Store it (`grantedScopes`) at callback time; gate each feature: `grantedScopes.includes('gmail.readonly')` → enable mail ingest, else show "Gmail not granted" without breaking calendar. Reference has no equivalent — this is the one net-new piece.
125	- Gmail read basics (same authed client):
126	  ```ts
127	  const gmail = google.gmail({ version: 'v1', auth: oauth2Client });
128	  const list = await gmail.users.messages.list({ userId: 'me', q: 'has:attachment newer_than:30d', maxResults: 50 });
129	  // list.data.messages: [{id, threadId}]; page with list.data.nextPageToken
130	  const msg = await gmail.users.messages.get({ userId: 'me', id, format: 'full' }); // payload.parts for attachments
131	  ```
132	  A Gmail call with an ungranted scope returns 403 `insufficientPermissions` — catch it and flip the feature flag rather than erroring the whole sync.
133	- Reuse the exact refresh routine from `calendar-sync.service.ts:376-401` for both APIs (one token pair covers all granted scopes).
134	
135	### Env
136	`GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI` (the backend callback URL, registered verbatim in Google Cloud Console — gotcha #2), asserted non-empty at boot per this repo's rules.
137
```
</details></tool-use>
